Any boundary-preserving isomorphism from a distinction to \texttt{Two}
agrees with the normal-form map. This is another application of the
eliminator: once the values at $\ell$ and $r$ are fixed, the map is fixed
everywhere. The same reasoning determines the inverse map.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Uniqueness of the to-direction.
toTwo-unique :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (f : S d ‚Üí Two) ‚Üí
  f (‚Ąď d) ‚Č° L ‚Üí
  f (r d) ‚Č° R ‚Üí
  f ‚Čó toTwo d
toTwo-unique d f f‚Ąď fr =
  Distinction-elim d
    (trans f‚Ąď (sym (toTwo-‚Ąď d)))
    (trans fr (sym (toTwo-r d)))

-- ¬ß Uniqueness of the swap-direction.
toTwo-swap-unique :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (f : S d ‚Üí Two) ‚Üí
  f (‚Ąď d) ‚Č° R ‚Üí
  f (r d) ‚Č° L ‚Üí
  f ‚Čó toTwo-swap d
toTwo-swap-unique d f f‚Ąď fr =
  Distinction-elim d
    (trans f‚Ąď (sym (toTwo-swap-‚Ąď d)))
    (trans fr (sym (toTwo-swap-r d)))

-- ¬ß Law 1.9: iso-to is unique.
law1-9-iso-to-unique : (d : Distinction) ‚Üí (i : DistinctionIso d Two-distinction) ‚Üí to i ‚Čó toTwo d
law1-9-iso-to-unique d i =
  toTwo-unique d (to i)
    (trans (to-‚Ąď i) refl)
    (trans (to-r i) refl)

-- ¬ß Uniqueness of the from-direction.
fromTwo-unique :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (g : Two ‚Üí S d) ‚Üí
  g L ‚Č° ‚Ąď d ‚Üí
  g R ‚Č° r d ‚Üí
  g ‚Čó fromTwo d
fromTwo-unique d g gL gR L = trans gL refl
fromTwo-unique d g gL gR R = trans gR refl

-- ¬ß Law 1.9: iso-from is unique.
law1-9-iso-from-unique : (d : Distinction) ‚Üí (i : DistinctionIso d Two-distinction) ‚Üí from i ‚Čó fromTwo d
law1-9-iso-from-unique d i =
  fromTwo-unique d (from i)
    (trans (sym (cong (from i) (to-‚Ąď i))) (from-to i (‚Ąď d)))
    (trans (sym (cong (from i) (to-r i))) (from-to i (r d)))
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 1.10: Two Normal Form Has Exactly Two Orientations}
\label{law:two-normal-form:two-orientations}

Every equivalence from a distinction to \texttt{Two} has one of the two
orientations just described. This is an absolute classification because
the proof eliminates the two equal-image cases by contradiction.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Law 1.10: orientation is exhaustive.
orientation-exhaustive :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (e : DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction) ‚Üí
  (to e ‚Čó toTwo d) ‚äé (to e ‚Čó toTwo-swap d)
orientation-exhaustive d e with Two-cover (to e (‚Ąď d)) | Two-cover (to e (r d))
... | inj‚āĀ t‚Ąď‚Č°L | inj‚āĀ tr‚Č°L = ‚ä•-elim (to-distinct-on-boundary d e (trans t‚Ąď‚Č°L (sym tr‚Č°L)))
... | inj‚āā t‚Ąď‚Č°R | inj‚āā tr‚Č°R = ‚ä•-elim (to-distinct-on-boundary d e (trans t‚Ąď‚Č°R (sym tr‚Č°R)))
... | inj‚āĀ t‚Ąď‚Č°L | inj‚āā tr‚Č°R = inj‚āĀ (toTwo-unique d (to e) t‚Ąď‚Č°L tr‚Č°R)
... | inj‚āā t‚Ąď‚Č°R | inj‚āĀ tr‚Č°L = inj‚āā (toTwo-swap-unique d (to e) t‚Ąď‚Č°R tr‚Č°L)

law1-10-orientation-exhaustive :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (e : DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction) ‚Üí
  (to e ‚Čó toTwo d) ‚äé (to e ‚Čó toTwo-swap d)
law1-10-orientation-exhaustive = orientation-exhaustive
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 1.11: Automorphisms Are Exactly id Or dual}
\label{law:two-normal-form:automorphisms-id-or-dual}

Automorphisms are therefore exhausted by the identity and the dual map.
The word ``exactly'' is justified by \texttt{Aut-sound}: preservation and
exchange are the only boundary behaviours compatible with injectivity and
coverage.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Law 1.11: Automorphisms are exactly id or dual.
law1-11-Aut-sound :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (a : Aut d) ‚Üí
  (to a ‚Čó id) ‚äé (to a ‚Čó Distinction-dual d)
law1-11-Aut-sound = Aut-sound
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 1.12: Two-Orientation Classification Is Sound And Unique}
\label{law:two-normal-form:orientation-classification}

The next step packages the two orientations into an explicit classifier.
Each equivalence to \texttt{Two} receives one of two labels,
\texttt{case-preserve} or \texttt{case-swap}, together with a proof that
the chosen label reproduces the map. Uniqueness is immediate because the
two interpretations already disagree at the left boundary point; to let a
single equivalence realize both would force $L \equiv R$.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Orientation case type.
data OrientationCase : Set where
  case-preserve : OrientationCase
  case-swap     : OrientationCase

-- ¬ß Interpret an orientation case as a map.
orientationInterpret : (d : Distinction) ‚Üí OrientationCase ‚Üí S d ‚Üí Two
orientationInterpret d case-preserve = toTwo d
orientationInterpret d case-swap     = toTwo-swap d

-- ¬ß Law 1.12: orientation classification is sound.
orientationCase-sound :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (e : DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction) ‚Üí
  ő£ OrientationCase (őĽ c ‚Üí to e ‚Čó orientationInterpret d c)
orientationCase-sound d e with orientation-exhaustive d e
... | inj‚āĀ p = case-preserve , p
... | inj‚āā p = case-swap     , p

-- ¬ß Law 1.12: orientation classification is unique.
orientationCase-unique :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (e : DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction) ‚Üí
  (c‚āĀ c‚āā : OrientationCase) ‚Üí
  to e ‚Čó orientationInterpret d c‚āĀ ‚Üí
  to e ‚Čó orientationInterpret d c‚āā ‚Üí
  c‚āĀ ‚Č° c‚āā
orientationCase-unique d e case-preserve case-preserve _ _ = refl
orientationCase-unique d e case-swap     case-swap     _ _ = refl
orientationCase-unique d e case-preserve case-swap p q =
  ‚ä•-elim (Two-L‚Č†R (sym toR‚Č°L))
  where
    toR‚Č°L : R ‚Č° L
    toR‚Č°L =
      trans (sym (toTwo-swap-‚Ąď d))
        (trans (sym (q (‚Ąď d)))
          (trans (p (‚Ąď d)) (toTwo-‚Ąď d)))
orientationCase-unique d e case-swap case-preserve p q =
  ‚ä•-elim (Two-L‚Č†R (sym toR‚Č°L))
  where
    toR‚Č°L : R ‚Č° L
    toR‚Č°L =
      trans (sym (toTwo-swap-‚Ąď d))
        (trans (sym (p (‚Ąď d)))
          (trans (q (‚Ąď d)) (toTwo-‚Ąď d)))

law1-12-orientationCase-sound :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (e : DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction) ‚Üí
  ő£ OrientationCase (őĽ c ‚Üí to e ‚Čó orientationInterpret d c)
law1-12-orientationCase-sound = orientationCase-sound

law1-12-orientationCase-unique :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (e : DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction) ‚Üí
  (c‚āĀ c‚āā : OrientationCase) ‚Üí
  to e ‚Čó orientationInterpret d c‚āĀ ‚Üí
  to e ‚Čó orientationInterpret d c‚āā ‚Üí
  c‚āĀ ‚Č° c‚āā
law1-12-orientationCase-unique = orientationCase-unique
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 1.13: Automorphism Classification Is Sound And Unique}
\label{law:two-normal-form:automorphism-classification}

This final classification statement records the same two properties as
before: under the two-class constraint every automorphism falls into one
of the two surviving forms, and that form is uniquely determined.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Automorphism case type.
data AutCase : Set where
  case-id   : AutCase
  case-dual : AutCase

-- ¬ß Interpret an automorphism case.
autInterpret : (d : Distinction) ‚Üí AutCase ‚Üí S d ‚Üí S d
autInterpret d case-id   = id
autInterpret d case-dual = Distinction-dual d

-- ¬ß Automorphism classification is sound.
autCase-sound :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (a : Aut d) ‚Üí
  ő£ AutCase (őĽ c ‚Üí to a ‚Čó autInterpret d c)
autCase-sound d a with Aut-sound d a
... | inj‚āĀ p = case-id   , p
... | inj‚āā p = case-dual , p

-- ¬ß Automorphism classification is unique.
autCase-unique :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (a : Aut d) ‚Üí
  (c‚āĀ c‚āā : AutCase) ‚Üí
  to a ‚Čó autInterpret d c‚āĀ ‚Üí
  to a ‚Čó autInterpret d c‚āā ‚Üí
  c‚āĀ ‚Č° c‚āā
autCase-unique d a case-id case-id _ _ = refl
autCase-unique d a case-dual case-dual _ _ = refl
autCase-unique d a case-id case-dual p q =
  ‚ä•-elim ((‚Ąď‚Č†r d) ‚Ąď‚Č°r)
  where
    ‚Ąď‚Č°r : ‚Ąď d ‚Č° r d
    ‚Ąď‚Č°r =
      trans (sym (p (‚Ąď d)))
        (trans (q (‚Ąď d)) (Distinction-dual-‚Ąď d))
autCase-unique d a case-dual case-id p q =
  ‚ä•-elim ((‚Ąď‚Č†r d) ‚Ąď‚Č°r)
  where
    ‚Ąď‚Č°r : ‚Ąď d ‚Č° r d
    ‚Ąď‚Č°r =
      trans (sym (q (‚Ąď d)))
        (trans (p (‚Ąď d)) (Distinction-dual-‚Ąď d))

-- ¬ß Law 1.13: Automorphism classification is sound and unique.
law1-13-autCase-sound :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (a : Aut d) ‚Üí
  ő£ AutCase (őĽ c ‚Üí to a ‚Čó autInterpret d c)
law1-13-autCase-sound = autCase-sound

law1-13-autCase-unique :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (a : Aut d) ‚Üí
  (c‚āĀ c‚āā : AutCase) ‚Üí
  to a ‚Čó autInterpret d c‚āĀ ‚Üí
  to a ‚Čó autInterpret d c‚āā ‚Üí
  c‚āĀ ‚Č° c‚āā
law1-13-autCase-unique = autCase-unique
\end{code}

\section{K\texorpdfstring{$_4$}{4} Witness Interface}
\label{sec:classification:k4-witness-interface}

The classification results for endomorphisms are now packaged once more
in witness form. For a given endomorphism, the witness consists of a case
label together with a proof that the interpreted case agrees pointwise
with the original map. This adds no new assumption; it exposes the earlier
module-level statements as a global interface.

\phantomsection\label{law:k4:witness-sound}
\phantomsection\label{law:k4:witness-unique}

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Law 1.14: K‚āĄ classification produces a witness.
law1-14-k4-classification-sound :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (f : S d ‚Üí S d) ‚Üí
  ő£ EndoCase (őĽ c ‚Üí K‚āĄ.interpret d c ‚Čó f)
law1-14-k4-classification-sound = k4-classification-sound

-- ¬ß Law 1.15: K‚āĄ classification witness is unique.
law1-15-k4-classification-unique :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (f : S d ‚Üí S d) ‚Üí
  (c‚āĀ c‚āā : EndoCase) ‚Üí
  K‚āĄ.interpret d c‚āĀ ‚Čó f ‚Üí
  K‚āĄ.interpret d c‚āā ‚Čó f ‚Üí
  c‚āĀ ‚Č° c‚āā
law1-15-k4-classification-unique = k4-classification-unique
\end{code}

\section{Two-Class Coverage Without Definitional Equality}
\label{sec:classification:coverage-without-definitional-equality}

Up to this point the development has used propositional equality throughout. For later applications the elimination argument must be separated from that particular equality relation and stated instead for an arbitrary equivalence relation.

The record \texttt{DistinctionClass} provides this generalization in explicit form. It consists of a carrier equipped with a reflexive, symmetric, and transitive relation, together with two distinguished elements that remain separate under that relation and a corresponding two-class cover. The eliminator from the earlier sections then extends to this broader setting without changing its structural role.

\phantomsection\label{law:classification:cover-under-equivalence}
\phantomsection\label{law:classification:distinction-to-class}

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß DistinctionClass: two-class coverage with an arbitrary equivalence relation.
record DistinctionClass : Set1 where
  field
    S      : Set
    _‚Čą_    : S ‚Üí S ‚Üí Set
    ‚Čą-refl : (x : S) ‚Üí x ‚Čą x
    ‚Čą-sym  : {x y : S} ‚Üí x ‚Čą y ‚Üí y ‚Čą x
    ‚Čą-trans : {x y z : S} ‚Üí x ‚Čą y ‚Üí y ‚Čą z ‚Üí x ‚Čą z

    ‚Ąď      : S
    r      : S
    ‚Ąď‚ČČr    : ¬¨ (‚Ąď ‚Čą r)
    cover‚Čą : (x : S) ‚Üí (x ‚Čą ‚Ąď) ‚äé (x ‚Čą r)

open DistinctionClass public

-- ¬ß Respect predicate for ‚Čą-elimination.
Respect‚Čą : (d : DistinctionClass) ‚Üí (S d ‚Üí Set) ‚Üí Set
Respect‚Čą d P = {x y : S d} ‚Üí _‚Čą_ d x y ‚Üí P x ‚Üí P y

-- ¬ß Law 1.16: Elimination is forced by cover‚Čą.
DistinctionClass-elim :
  (d : DistinctionClass) ‚Üí
  {P : S d ‚Üí Set} ‚Üí
  Respect‚Čą d P ‚Üí
  P (‚Ąď d) ‚Üí
  P (r d) ‚Üí
  (x : S d) ‚Üí
  P x
DistinctionClass-elim d {P} resp p‚Ąď pr x with cover‚Čą d x
... | inj‚āĀ x‚Čą‚Ąď = resp ((‚Čą-sym d) x‚Čą‚Ąď) p‚Ąď
... | inj‚āā x‚Čąr = resp ((‚Čą-sym d) x‚Čąr) pr

law1-16-class-elim :
  (d : DistinctionClass) ‚Üí
  {P : S d ‚Üí Set} ‚Üí
  Respect‚Čą d P ‚Üí
  P (‚Ąď d) ‚Üí
  P (r d) ‚Üí
  (x : S d) ‚Üí
  P x
law1-16-class-elim = DistinctionClass-elim

-- ¬ß Law 1.17: Every Distinction induces a DistinctionClass.
Distinction‚ÜíDistinctionClass : Distinction ‚Üí DistinctionClass
Distinction‚ÜíDistinctionClass d = record
  { S       = S d
  ; _‚Čą_     = _‚Č°_
  ; ‚Čą-refl  = őĽ x ‚Üí refl
  ; ‚Čą-sym   = sym
  ; ‚Čą-trans = trans
  ; ‚Ąď       = ‚Ąď d
  ; r       = r d
  ; ‚Ąď‚ČČr     = ‚Ąď‚Č†r d
  ; cover‚Čą  = cover d
  }

law1-17-distinction-to-class : Distinction ‚Üí DistinctionClass
law1-17-distinction-to-class = Distinction‚ÜíDistinctionClass
\end{code}

\section{Presentation Uniqueness}
\label{sec:presentation:elimination}

The canonical classifier is not the only possible way to index the four endomorphism cases. One may choose a different datatype $X$ together with interpretation and classification maps. The next theorem shows that any such presentation is equivalent to the chosen one, provided it is sound and its interpretation map is injective up to pointwise equality.

In other words, once soundness and injectivity are fixed, the classification is unique up to reindexing. The proof constructs explicit maps between $X$ and \texttt{EndoCase} and then verifies the two round-trip laws.

\phantomsection\label{law:presentation:endo-unique}

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Endo presentation record.
record EndoPresentation (d : Distinction) (X : Set) : Set where
  field
    present-interpret           : X ‚Üí (S d ‚Üí S d)
    present-classify            : (S d ‚Üí S d) ‚Üí X
    present-classify-sound      : (f : S d ‚Üí S d) ‚Üí present-interpret (present-classify f) ‚Čó f
    present-interpret-injective : (x y : X) ‚Üí present-interpret x ‚Čó present-interpret y ‚Üí x ‚Č° y

-- ¬ß Law 1.18: Endo presentation is unique up to isomorphism.
law1-18-endo-presentation-unique :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  {X : Set} ‚Üí
  EndoPresentation d X ‚Üí
  SetIso X EndoCase
law1-18-endo-presentation-unique d {X} pres =
  let
    module K = K‚āĄ d
    open EndoPresentation pres
      renaming
        ( present-interpret to interpretX
        ; present-classify to classifyX
        ; present-classify-sound to classifyX-sound
        ; present-interpret-injective to interpretX-injective
        )

    to' : X ‚Üí EndoCase
    to' x = K.classify (interpretX x)

    from' : EndoCase ‚Üí X
    from' c = classifyX (K.interpret c)

    to-from' : (c : EndoCase) ‚Üí to' (from' c) ‚Č° c
    to-from' c =
      sym
        (K.classify-unique
          (interpretX (classifyX (K.interpret c)))
          c
          (K.‚Čó-sym (classifyX-sound (K.interpret c))))

    from-to' : (x : X) ‚Üí from' (to' x) ‚Č° x
    from-to' x =
      interpretX-injective
        (classifyX (K.interpret (K.classify (interpretX x))))
        x
        (K.‚Čó-trans
          (classifyX-sound (K.interpret (K.classify (interpretX x))))
          (K.classify-sound (interpretX x)))
  in
  record
    { to = to'
    ; from = from'
    ; to-from = to-from'
    ; from-to = from-to'
    }
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 1.19--1.20: Orientation and Automorphism Presentation Uniqueness}
\label{laws:presentation:orientation-automorphism-elimination}

The same argument applies to the orientation and automorphism classifications. Once the classifier/interpreter pair is fixed, any alternative presentation satisfying the same soundness and injectivity requirements is isomorphic to that presentation. The freedom left by relabelling is therefore harmless: it changes names, not structure.

The following helper definitions prepare these two analogues of the endomorphism presentation theorem.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Helpers for orientation presentation elimination.
orientationCase-classify :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (e : DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction) ‚Üí
  OrientationCase
orientationCase-classify d e = fst (orientationCase-sound d e)

orientationCase-classify-sound :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (e : DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction) ‚Üí
  to e ‚Čó orientationInterpret d (orientationCase-classify d e)
orientationCase-classify-sound d e = snd (orientationCase-sound d e)

autCase-classify :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (a : Aut d) ‚Üí
  AutCase
autCase-classify d a = fst (autCase-sound d a)

autCase-classify-sound :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (a : Aut d) ‚Üí
  to a ‚Čó autInterpret d (autCase-classify d a)
autCase-classify-sound d a = snd (autCase-sound d a)

orientationEquivInterpret :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  OrientationCase ‚Üí
  DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction
orientationEquivInterpret d case-preserve = two-normal-form-equiv d
orientationEquivInterpret d case-swap     = two-normal-form-equiv-swap d

orientationEquivInterpret-sound :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (c : OrientationCase) ‚Üí
  to (orientationEquivInterpret d c) ‚Čó orientationInterpret d c
orientationEquivInterpret-sound d case-preserve x = refl
orientationEquivInterpret-sound d case-swap     x = refl

-- ¬ß Identity equivalence.
idEquiv : (d : Distinction) ‚Üí DistinctionEquiv d d
idEquiv d = record
  { to      = id
  ; from    = id
  ; to-from = őĽ y ‚Üí refl
  ; from-to = őĽ x ‚Üí refl
  }

-- ¬ß Dual equivalence.
dualEquiv : (d : Distinction) ‚Üí DistinctionEquiv d d
dualEquiv d = record
  { to      = Distinction-dual d
  ; from    = Distinction-dual d
  ; to-from = law1-3-dual-involutive d
  ; from-to = law1-3-dual-involutive d
  }

autEquivInterpret :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  AutCase ‚Üí
  Aut d
autEquivInterpret d case-id   = idEquiv d
autEquivInterpret d case-dual = dualEquiv d

autEquivInterpret-sound :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (c : AutCase) ‚Üí
  to (autEquivInterpret d c) ‚Čó autInterpret d c
autEquivInterpret-sound d case-id   x = refl
autEquivInterpret-sound d case-dual x = refl
\end{code}

\paragraph{Presentation records.}
A presentation of either classification, orientation or automorphism is a set-valued interface carrying interpretation, classification, soundness, and injectivity. The corresponding records package exactly the data required to compare an arbitrary labeling with the fixed classifier/interpreter pair.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Orientation presentation record.
record OrientationPresentation (d : Distinction) (X : Set) : Set1 where
  field
    op-interpret           : X ‚Üí DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction
    op-classify            : DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction ‚Üí X
    op-classify-sound      : (e : DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction) ‚Üí to (op-interpret (op-classify e)) ‚Čó to e
    op-interpret-injective : (x y : X) ‚Üí to (op-interpret x) ‚Čó to (op-interpret y) ‚Üí x ‚Č° y

-- ¬ß Automorphism presentation record.
record AutPresentation (d : Distinction) (X : Set) : Set1 where
  field
    ap-interpret           : X ‚Üí Aut d
    ap-classify            : Aut d ‚Üí X
    ap-classify-sound      : (a : Aut d) ‚Üí to (ap-interpret (ap-classify a)) ‚Čó to a
    ap-interpret-injective : (x y : X) ‚Üí to (ap-interpret x) ‚Čó to (ap-interpret y) ‚Üí x ‚Č° y
\end{code}

\paragraph{Law 1.19: Orientation presentation uniqueness.}
Every orientation presentation satisfying the stated interface is therefore isomorphic to \texttt{OrientationCase}. The isomorphism is obtained by composing classification on one side with interpretation on the other, while soundness and injectivity force the two round-trip properties.

\phantomsection\label{law:presentation:orientation-unique}

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Law 1.19: Orientation presentation is unique up to isomorphism.
law1-19-orientation-presentation-unique :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  {X : Set} ‚Üí
  OrientationPresentation d X ‚Üí
  SetIso X OrientationCase
law1-19-orientation-presentation-unique d {X} pres =
  let
    open OrientationPresentation pres
      renaming
        ( op-interpret to interpretX
        ; op-classify to classifyX
        ; op-classify-sound to classifyX-sound
        ; op-interpret-injective to interpretX-injective
        )

    ‚Čó-sym : {A B : Set} {f g : A ‚Üí B} ‚Üí f ‚Čó g ‚Üí g ‚Čó f
    ‚Čó-sym p x = sym (p x)

    ‚Čó-trans : {A B : Set} {f g h : A ‚Üí B} ‚Üí f ‚Čó g ‚Üí g ‚Čó h ‚Üí f ‚Čó h
    ‚Čó-trans p q x = trans (p x) (q x)

    to' : X ‚Üí OrientationCase
    to' x = orientationCase-classify d (interpretX x)

    from' : OrientationCase ‚Üí X
    from' c = classifyX (orientationEquivInterpret d c)

    to-from' : (c : OrientationCase) ‚Üí to' (from' c) ‚Č° c
    to-from' c =
      orientationCase-unique d (interpretX (classifyX (orientationEquivInterpret d c)))
        (to' (from' c))
        c
        (orientationCase-classify-sound d (interpretX (classifyX (orientationEquivInterpret d c))))
        (‚Čó-trans
          (classifyX-sound (orientationEquivInterpret d c))
          (orientationEquivInterpret-sound d c))

    from-to' : (x : X) ‚Üí from' (to' x) ‚Č° x
    from-to' x =
      interpretX-injective
        (classifyX (orientationEquivInterpret d (to' x)))
        x
        (‚Čó-trans
          (classifyX-sound (orientationEquivInterpret d (to' x)))
          (‚Čó-trans
            (orientationEquivInterpret-sound d (to' x))
            (‚Čó-sym (orientationCase-classify-sound d (interpretX x)))))
  in
  record
    { to = to'
    ; from = from'
    ; to-from = to-from'
    ; from-to = from-to'
    }

-- ¬ß Law 1.20: Automorphism presentation is unique up to isomorphism.
\end{code}

\paragraph{Law 1.20: Automorphism presentation uniqueness.}
The same argument applies to automorphisms. Any sound and injective presentation of automorphisms is isomorphic to the two-element case type \texttt{AutCase}; relabelling can change the interface, not the classified structure.

\phantomsection\label{law:presentation:automorphism-unique}

\begin{code}
law1-20-aut-presentation-unique :
  (d : Distinction) ‚Üí
  {X : Set} ‚Üí
  AutPresentation d X ‚Üí
  SetIso X AutCase
law1-20-aut-presentation-unique d {X} pres =
  let
    open AutPresentation pres
      renaming
        ( ap-interpret to interpretX
        ; ap-classify to classifyX
        ; ap-classify-sound to classifyX-sound
        ; ap-interpret-injective to interpretX-injective
        )

    ‚Čó-sym : {A B : Set} {f g : A ‚Üí B} ‚Üí f ‚Čó g ‚Üí g ‚Čó f
    ‚Čó-sym p x = sym (p x)

    ‚Čó-trans : {A B : Set} {f g h : A ‚Üí B} ‚Üí f ‚Čó g ‚Üí g ‚Čó h ‚Üí f ‚Čó h
    ‚Čó-trans p q x = trans (p x) (q x)

    to' : X ‚Üí AutCase
    to' x = autCase-classify d (interpretX x)

    from' : AutCase ‚Üí X
    from' c = classifyX (autEquivInterpret d c)

    to-from' : (c : AutCase) ‚Üí to' (from' c) ‚Č° c
    to-from' c =
      autCase-unique d (interpretX (classifyX (autEquivInterpret d c)))
        (to' (from' c))
        c
        (autCase-classify-sound d (interpretX (classifyX (autEquivInterpret d c))))
        (‚Čó-trans
          (classifyX-sound (autEquivInterpret d c))
          (autEquivInterpret-sound d c))

    from-to' : (x : X) ‚Üí from' (to' x) ‚Č° x
    from-to' x =
      interpretX-injective
        (classifyX (autEquivInterpret d (to' x)))
        x
        (‚Čó-trans
          (classifyX-sound (autEquivInterpret d (to' x)))
          (‚Čó-trans
            (autEquivInterpret-sound d (to' x))
            (‚Čó-sym (autCase-classify-sound d (interpretX x)))))
  in
  record
    { to = to'
    ; from = from'
    ; to-from = to-from'
    ; from-to = from-to'
    }
\end{code}

The concrete Two reference and $D_0$ are fixed last.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß D‚āÄ is the canonical first distinction.
D‚āÄ : Set
D‚āÄ = Two

-- ¬ß The left boundary.
left : D‚āÄ
left = L

-- ¬ß The right boundary.
right : D‚āÄ
right = R

-- ¬ß D‚āÄ as a Distinction record.
D‚āÄ-distinction : Distinction
D‚āÄ-distinction = Two-distinction
\end{code}

At this point the primitive distinction has been fixed concretely as \texttt{Two-distinction}. The basic logical infrastructure, the distinction record, the four endomorphism cases, and the associated presentation results are now all available in explicit, machine-checked form.

The next step is to examine what happens when the same classification process is applied one level higher. This leads to the hierarchy called \emph{drift}.

\part{Drift and Reachability}

Part I analysed a single distinction and the finite classification data attached to it. Part II turns to the hierarchy obtained by iterating that analysis. Once classification data are themselves treated as mathematical objects, the development moves through higher universe levels in a controlled way.

This is the role of drift. The underlying idea is straightforward: once a structure has been introduced and classified at one level, the resulting data becomes the object of further classification at the next. The next chapters make that transition explicit and study the linear hierarchy it generates.

\chapter{Drift}
\label{ch:drift}

Drift is the basic operation that moves a distinction one universe level upward. It is not introduced as additional structure; it is the lift required once the development begins to quantify over classification data themselves.

The construction uses \texttt{Lift} on the carrier and transports the two boundary points and the cover along with it. Later results show that this lift is universal among boundary-preserving lifts of the same kind.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Level-polymorphic distinction record.
record Distinction‚Ąď (‚Ąď : Level) : Set (lsuc ‚Ąď) where
  field
    S     : Set ‚Ąď
    ‚Ąď‚āÄ    : S
    r‚āÄ    : S
    ‚Ąď‚āÄ‚Č†r‚āÄ : ‚Ąď‚āÄ ‚Č† r‚āÄ
    cover : (x : S) ‚Üí (x ‚Č° ‚Ąď‚āÄ) ‚äé (x ‚Č° r‚āÄ)

open Distinction‚Ąď public

-- ¬ß Canonical embedding from base-level Distinction.
Distinction‚ÜíDistinction‚Ąď : Distinction ‚Üí Distinction‚Ąď lzero
Distinction‚ÜíDistinction‚Ąď d = record
  { S     = S d
  ; ‚Ąď‚āÄ    = ‚Ąď d
  ; r‚āÄ    = r d
  ; ‚Ąď‚āÄ‚Č†r‚āÄ = ‚Ąď‚Č†r d
  ; cover = cover d
  }

-- ¬ß A drift step: the next-level distinction with an embedding.
record DriftStep {‚Ąď : Level} (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) : Set (lsuc (lsuc ‚Ąď)) where
  field
    d‚ÜĎ    : Distinction‚Ąď (lsuc ‚Ąď)
    embed : S d ‚Üí S d‚ÜĎ

open DriftStep public

-- ¬ß Drift is the canonical lift to the next universe level.
drift : {‚Ąď : Level} ‚Üí (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí DriftStep d
drift d = record
  { d‚ÜĎ = record
      { S     = Lift (S d)
      ; ‚Ąď‚āÄ    = lift (‚Ąď‚āÄ d)
      ; r‚āÄ    = lift (r‚āÄ d)
      ; ‚Ąď‚āÄ‚Č†r‚āÄ = őĽ eq ‚Üí ‚Ąď‚āÄ‚Č†r‚āÄ d (lift-injective eq)
    ; cover = cover‚ÜĎ
      }
  ; embed = lift
  }
  where
  cover‚ÜĎ : (y : Lift (S d)) ‚Üí (y ‚Č° lift (‚Ąď‚āÄ d)) ‚äé (y ‚Č° lift (r‚āÄ d))
  cover‚ÜĎ (lift x) with cover d x
  ... | inj‚āĀ x‚Č°‚Ąď = inj‚āĀ (cong lift x‚Č°‚Ąď)
  ... | inj‚āā x‚Č°r = inj‚āā (cong lift x‚Č°r)
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 2.1: No Classification May Remain Unclassified}
\label{law:drift:no-classification-unclassified}

This law records the drift construction as the formal expression of closure under reclassification.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Law 2.1: No classification may remain unclassified.
law2-1-drift : {‚Ąď : Level} ‚Üí (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí DriftStep d
law2-1-drift = drift

-- ¬ß Extract the next-level distinction from a drift step.
drift-next : {‚Ąď : Level} ‚Üí (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí Distinction‚Ąď (lsuc ‚Ąď)
drift-next d = d‚ÜĎ (drift d)

-- ¬ß Extract the embedding from a drift step.
drift-embed : {‚Ąď : Level} ‚Üí (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí S d ‚Üí S (drift-next d)
drift-embed d = embed (drift d)

-- ¬ß Drift-embedded elements satisfy coverage.
drift-embed-cover : {‚Ąď : Level} ‚Üí (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí (x : S d)
                 ‚Üí (drift-embed d x ‚Č° ‚Ąď‚āÄ (drift-next d)) ‚äé (drift-embed d x ‚Č° r‚āÄ (drift-next d))
drift-embed-cover d x = cover (drift-next d) (drift-embed d x)
\end{code}

The point is that drift preserves the full two-class structure. The lifted distinction still has distinguished boundary points, a cover, and an embedding of the original carrier.

\chapter{Non-Collapse of Drift}
\label{ch:drift-noncollapse}

Since drift is implemented by \texttt{Lift}, the relevant question is whether it preserves distinctions rather than collapsing them. This chapter records the answer formally. The key point is that \texttt{lift} is injective, so different elements of the original carrier remain different after embedding.

\subsection{Law 3.1: Drift Does Not Fold The Prior Carrier}
\label{law:drift:prior-carrier-not-folded}

The proof is immediate from injectivity of \texttt{lift}. If two embedded elements were equal, their preimages would already have been equal in the original carrier. In particular, the two distinguished boundary points remain distinct after embedding.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Law 3.1: Drift does not fold the prior carrier.
law3-1-embed-injective :
  {‚Ąď : Level} (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí {x y : S d} ‚Üí
  drift-embed d x ‚Č° drift-embed d y ‚Üí x ‚Č° y
law3-1-embed-injective d = lift-injective

-- ¬ß Drift preserves boundary distinctness.
drift-embed-‚Ąď‚āÄ‚Č†r‚āÄ :
  {‚Ąď : Level} (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí
  drift-embed d (‚Ąď‚āÄ d) ‚Č† drift-embed d (r‚āÄ d)
drift-embed-‚Ąď‚āÄ‚Č†r‚āÄ d eq = ‚Ąď‚āÄ‚Č†r‚āÄ d (law3-1-embed-injective d eq)
\end{code}

With non-collapse in place, the next question is whether drift is merely one possible lift or whether it has a distinguished universal role among all boundary-preserving lifts.

\chapter{Cross-Stage Comparison}
\label{ch:cross-stage-comparison}

Drift is not only a lift; the next theorem proves its canonical role. To formulate this precisely, the text compares drift with arbitrary boundary-preserving lifts of the same distinction.

The result is a familiar universal property. Every such lift receives a mediating map from the drift construction, and that map commutes with the given embeddings. The uniqueness proof below then closes the universal statement. The surrounding definitions package this result in a form that can later be read categorically.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Level-polymorphic eliminator for Distinction‚Ąď.
Distinction‚Ąď-elim :
  {‚Ąď ‚ĄďP : Level} ‚Üí (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí
  {P : S d ‚Üí Set ‚ĄďP} ‚Üí
  P (‚Ąď‚āÄ d) ‚Üí
  P (r‚āÄ d) ‚Üí
  (x : S d) ‚Üí
  P x
Distinction‚Ąď-elim d {P} p‚Ąď pr x with cover d x
... | inj‚āĀ x‚Č°‚Ąď = subst P (sym x‚Č°‚Ąď) p‚Ąď
... | inj‚āā x‚Č°r = subst P (sym x‚Č°r) pr

-- ¬ß Functions out of Distinction‚Ąď are determined by boundary values.
Distinction‚Ąď-determined :
  {‚Ąď ‚ĄďB : Level} ‚Üí (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí {B : Set ‚ĄďB} ‚Üí
  (f g : S d ‚Üí B) ‚Üí
  f (‚Ąď‚āÄ d) ‚Č° g (‚Ąď‚āÄ d) ‚Üí
  f (r‚āÄ d) ‚Č° g (r‚āÄ d) ‚Üí
  f ‚Čó g
Distinction‚Ąď-determined d f g f‚Ąď‚Č°g‚Ąď fr‚Č°gr =
  Distinction‚Ąď-elim d
    (subst (őĽ y ‚Üí f y ‚Č° g y) refl f‚Ąď‚Č°g‚Ąď)
    (subst (őĽ y ‚Üí f y ‚Č° g y) refl fr‚Č°gr)

-- ¬ß A boundary-preserving lift: target distinction + embedding + boundary proofs.
record LiftedBP {‚Ąď : Level} (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) : Set (lsuc (lsuc ‚Ąď)) where
  field
    e        : Distinction‚Ąď (lsuc ‚Ąď)
    embed    : S d ‚Üí S e
    embed-‚Ąď‚āÄ : embed (‚Ąď‚āÄ d) ‚Č° ‚Ąď‚āÄ e
    embed-r‚āÄ : embed (r‚āÄ d) ‚Č° r‚āÄ e

open LiftedBP public

-- ¬ß Drift universality: drift is initial among boundary-preserving lifts.
record DriftUniversal : SetŌČ where
  field
    preserves-‚Ąď‚āÄ : {‚Ąď : Level} (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí
      drift-embed d (‚Ąď‚āÄ d) ‚Č° ‚Ąď‚āÄ (drift-next d)
    preserves-r‚āÄ : {‚Ąď : Level} (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí
      drift-embed d (r‚āÄ d) ‚Č° r‚āÄ (drift-next d)

    mediator : {‚Ąď : Level} (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí (t : LiftedBP d) ‚Üí
      S (drift-next d) ‚Üí S (e t)

    mediator-commutes : {‚Ąď : Level} (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí (t : LiftedBP d) ‚Üí
      (x : S d) ‚Üí mediator d t (drift-embed d x) ‚Č° embed t x

open DriftUniversal public

-- ¬ß The canonical drift universality witness.
driftUniversal : DriftUniversal
driftUniversal = record
  { preserves-‚Ąď‚āÄ = őĽ d ‚Üí refl
  ; preserves-r‚āÄ = őĽ d ‚Üí refl
  ; mediator = őĽ d t y ‚Üí embed t (lower y)
  ; mediator-commutes = őĽ d t x ‚Üí refl
  }
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 4.1: Drift Is Universal Among Boundary-Preserving Lifts}
\label{law:drift:universal-lift}

The mediator is defined by applying the target embedding after lowering
out of \texttt{Lift}. Its commutativity with the original embedding is
definitional, and uniqueness follows from boundary determination at the
lifted level.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Law 4.1: Drift is universal among boundary-preserving lifts.
law4-1-mediator-commutes :
  {‚Ąď : Level} (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí (t : LiftedBP d) ‚Üí
  (x : S d) ‚Üí mediator driftUniversal d t (drift-embed d x) ‚Č° embed t x
law4-1-mediator-commutes d t x = mediator-commutes driftUniversal d t x

-- ¬ß Mediator uniqueness: any factorizing map agrees with the canonical one.
mediator-unique :
  {‚Ąď : Level} (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí (t : LiftedBP d) ‚Üí
  (g : S (drift-next d) ‚Üí S (e t)) ‚Üí
  ((x : S d) ‚Üí g (drift-embed d x) ‚Č° embed t x) ‚Üí
  g ‚Čó mediator driftUniversal d t
mediator-unique d t g g-comm =
  Distinction‚Ąď-determined (drift-next d) g h g‚Ąď‚Č°h‚Ąď gr‚Č°hr
  where
    h : S (drift-next d) ‚Üí S (e t)
    h = mediator driftUniversal d t

    g‚Ąď‚Č°h‚Ąď : g (‚Ąď‚āÄ (drift-next d)) ‚Č° h (‚Ąď‚āÄ (drift-next d))
    g‚Ąď‚Č°h‚Ąď =
      trans (cong g (sym (preserves-‚Ąď‚āÄ driftUniversal d)))
        (trans (g-comm (‚Ąď‚āÄ d))
          (trans (sym (mediator-commutes driftUniversal d t (‚Ąď‚āÄ d)))
            (cong h (preserves-‚Ąď‚āÄ driftUniversal d))))

    gr‚Č°hr : g (r‚āÄ (drift-next d)) ‚Č° h (r‚āÄ (drift-next d))
    gr‚Č°hr =
      trans (cong g (sym (preserves-r‚āÄ driftUniversal d)))
        (trans (g-comm (r‚āÄ d))
          (trans (sym (mediator-commutes driftUniversal d t (r‚āÄ d)))
            (cong h (preserves-r‚āÄ driftUniversal d))))

-- ¬ß Drift as a LiftedBP instance.
driftLiftedBP : {‚Ąď : Level} (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí LiftedBP d
driftLiftedBP d = record
  { e        = drift-next d
  ; embed    = drift-embed d
  ; embed-‚Ąď‚āÄ = preserves-‚Ąď‚āÄ driftUniversal d
  ; embed-r‚āÄ = preserves-r‚āÄ driftUniversal d
  }

-- ¬ß Morphism between lifted boundary-preserving presentations.
record LiftMorph {‚Ąď : Level} (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) (t u : LiftedBP d) : Set (lsuc (lsuc ‚Ąď)) where
  field
    map  : S (e t) ‚Üí S (e u)
    comm : (x : S d) ‚Üí map (embed t x) ‚Č° embed u x

open LiftMorph public

-- ¬ß The canonical factorization through drift.
drift-factor :
  {‚Ąď : Level} (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí (t : LiftedBP d) ‚Üí
  LiftMorph d (driftLiftedBP d) t
drift-factor d t = record
  { map  = mediator driftUniversal d t
  ; comm = mediator-commutes driftUniversal d t
  }

-- ¬ß Factorization through drift is unique.
drift-factor-unique :
  {‚Ąď : Level} (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí (t : LiftedBP d) ‚Üí
  (m : LiftMorph d (driftLiftedBP d) t) ‚Üí
  map m ‚Čó mediator driftUniversal d t
drift-factor-unique d t m =
  mediator-unique d t (map m) (comm m)
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 4.2: Drift-Step Factorization Is Unique}
\label{law:drift:factorization-unique}

This is the corresponding uniqueness statement for factorizations through the drift step. Once a lift factors through drift and commutes with the boundary embedding, boundary determination leaves no second factorizing map.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Law 4.2: Drift-step factorization is unique.
law4-2-factor-unique :
  {‚Ąď : Level} (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí (t : LiftedBP d) ‚Üí
  (m : LiftMorph d (driftLiftedBP d) t) ‚Üí
  map m ‚Čó mediator driftUniversal d t
law4-2-factor-unique = drift-factor-unique
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 4.3: The Category of Boundary-Preserving Lifts}
\label{law:drift:category-of-lifts}

Once the universal property has been stated, boundary-preserving lifts
and their commuting maps can be organized into a category. The
definitions below provide identities and composition, and the previous
universal property then becomes the statement that drift is an initial
object in this category.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Identity morphism on a lifted boundary-preserving presentation.
liftId : {‚Ąď : Level} (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí (t : LiftedBP d) ‚Üí LiftMorph d t t
liftId d t = record
  { map  = őĽ y ‚Üí y
  ; comm = őĽ x ‚Üí refl
  }

-- ¬ß Composition of LiftMorphs.
liftComp :
  {‚Ąď : Level} (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí {t u v : LiftedBP d} ‚Üí
  LiftMorph d u v ‚Üí LiftMorph d t u ‚Üí LiftMorph d t v
liftComp d {t} {u} {v} g f = record
  { map  = őĽ y ‚Üí map g (map f y)
  ; comm = őĽ x ‚Üí trans (cong (map g) (comm f x)) (comm g x)
  }

-- ¬ß Left identity law (pointwise).
liftId-left :
  {‚Ąď : Level} (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí {t u : LiftedBP d} ‚Üí
  (f : LiftMorph d t u) ‚Üí
  (y : S (e t)) ‚Üí map (liftComp d (liftId d u) f) y ‚Č° map f y
liftId-left d f y = refl

-- ¬ß Right identity law (pointwise).
liftId-right :
  {‚Ąď : Level} (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí {t u : LiftedBP d} ‚Üí
  (f : LiftMorph d t u) ‚Üí
  (y : S (e t)) ‚Üí map (liftComp d f (liftId d t)) y ‚Č° map f y
liftId-right d f y = refl

-- ¬ß Associativity (pointwise).
liftComp-assoc :
  {‚Ąď : Level} (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí {t u v w : LiftedBP d} ‚Üí
  (h : LiftMorph d v w) (g : LiftMorph d u v) (f : LiftMorph d t u) ‚Üí
  (y : S (e t)) ‚Üí
  map (liftComp d h (liftComp d g f)) y
    ‚Č°
  map (liftComp d (liftComp d h g) f) y
liftComp-assoc d h g f y = refl

-- ¬ß Initiality of drift in the category of boundary-preserving lifts.
record DriftInitial {‚Ąď : Level} (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) : Set (lsuc (lsuc ‚Ąď)) where
  field
    initial-mediator : (t : LiftedBP d) ‚Üí LiftMorph d (driftLiftedBP d) t
    initial-unique   : (t : LiftedBP d) ‚Üí (m : LiftMorph d (driftLiftedBP d) t) ‚Üí
                       (y : S (e (driftLiftedBP d))) ‚Üí
                       map m y ‚Č° map (initial-mediator t) y

-- ¬ß Law 4.3: Drift is the initial object.
law4-3-drift-initial :
  {‚Ąď : Level} (d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď) ‚Üí DriftInitial d
law4-3-drift-initial d = record
  { initial-mediator = drift-factor d
  ; initial-unique   = őĽ t m y ‚Üí drift-factor-unique d t m y
  }
\end{code}

This completes the universal characterization of drift. The construction
is no longer merely a chosen lift: in the category just displayed, it is
the initial boundary-preserving lift. The next step is to iterate it and
study the reachability relation generated by repeated drift steps.

\chapter{Drift Reachability}
\label{ch:drift-reachability}

Drift can now be iterated. A drift state packages a universe level
together with the distinction living there, and \texttt{stepState} moves
one stage upward. The resulting reachability relations record whether one
state can be obtained from another by finitely many drift steps.

Two versions are needed: strict reachability, written \texttt{Reach‚Āļ},
for one or more steps, and reflexive-transitive reachability, written
\texttt{Reach}, when zero steps are also allowed.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß A drift state packages a universe level with its distinction.
record DriftState : SetŌČ where
  constructor ‚ü®_,_‚ü©
  field
    ‚Ąď : Level
    d : Distinction‚Ąď ‚Ąď

open DriftState public

-- ¬ß Step a drift state forward.
stepState : DriftState ‚Üí DriftState
stepState s = ‚ü® lsuc (‚Ąď s) , drift-next (d s) ‚ü©

-- ¬ß Extract the carrier of a drift state.
Carrier : (s : DriftState) ‚Üí Set (‚Ąď s)
Carrier s = S (d s)

-- ¬ß The canonical embedding between consecutive carriers.
state-embed : (s : DriftState) ‚Üí Carrier s ‚Üí Carrier (stepState s)
state-embed s = drift-embed (d s)

-- ¬ß Strict reachability: one or more drift steps.
data Reach‚Āļ : DriftState ‚Üí DriftState ‚Üí SetŌČ where
  one  : {s : DriftState} ‚Üí Reach‚Āļ s (stepState s)
  more : {s t : DriftState} ‚Üí Reach‚Āļ (stepState s) t ‚Üí Reach‚Āļ s t

-- ¬ß Reflexive-transitive reachability.
data Reach : DriftState ‚Üí DriftState ‚Üí SetŌČ where
  stop : {s : DriftState} ‚Üí Reach s s
  next : {s t : DriftState} ‚Üí Reach‚Āļ s t ‚Üí Reach s t
\end{code}

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Reach‚Āļ eliminator.
Reach‚Āļ-elim :
  {P : (s t : DriftState) ‚Üí Reach‚Āļ s t ‚Üí SetŌČ} ‚Üí
  ({s : DriftState} ‚Üí P s (stepState s) one) ‚Üí
  ({s t : DriftState} ‚Üí (p : Reach‚Āļ (stepState s) t) ‚Üí P (stepState s) t p ‚Üí P s t (more p)) ‚Üí
  {s t : DriftState} ‚Üí (p : Reach‚Āļ s t) ‚Üí P s t p
Reach‚Āļ-elim {P} base step one = base
Reach‚Āļ-elim {P} base step (more p) = step p (Reach‚Āļ-elim {P} base step p)

-- ¬ß Reach eliminator.
Reach-elim :
  {P : (s t : DriftState) ‚Üí Reach s t ‚Üí SetŌČ} ‚Üí
  ({s : DriftState} ‚Üí P s s stop) ‚Üí
  ({s t : DriftState} ‚Üí (p : Reach‚Āļ s t) ‚Üí P s t (next p)) ‚Üí
  {s t : DriftState} ‚Üí (p : Reach s t) ‚Üí P s t p
Reach-elim stopCase nextCase stop = stopCase
Reach-elim stopCase nextCase (next p) = nextCase p

-- ¬ß Strict reachability is transitive.
Reach‚Āļ-trans : {s t u : DriftState} ‚Üí Reach‚Āļ s t ‚Üí Reach‚Āļ t u ‚Üí Reach‚Āļ s u
Reach‚Āļ-trans one      q = more q
Reach‚Āļ-trans (more p) q = more (Reach‚Āļ-trans p q)

-- ¬ß Strict successors are comparable.
Reach‚Āļ-comparable :
  {s t‚āĀ t‚āā : DriftState} ‚Üí
  Reach‚Āļ s t‚āĀ ‚Üí Reach‚Āļ s t‚āā ‚Üí
  (Reach t‚āĀ t‚āā) ‚äéŌČ (Reach t‚āā t‚āĀ)
Reach‚Āļ-comparable one one = inj‚āĀŌČ stop
Reach‚Āļ-comparable one (more q) = inj‚āĀŌČ (next q)
Reach‚Āļ-comparable (more p) one = inj‚āāŌČ (next p)
Reach‚Āļ-comparable (more p) (more q) =
  Reach‚Āļ-comparable p q

-- ¬ß Reflexive-transitive reachability is transitive.
reach-trans : {s t u : DriftState} ‚Üí Reach s t ‚Üí Reach t u ‚Üí Reach s u
reach-trans stop     q        = q
reach-trans (next p) stop     = next p
reach-trans (next p) (next q) = next (Reach‚Āļ-trans p q)

-- ¬ß Every strict chain extends by one step.
Reach‚Āļ-extend : {s t : DriftState} ‚Üí Reach‚Āļ s t ‚Üí Reach‚Āļ s (stepState t)
Reach‚Āļ-extend p = Reach‚Āļ-trans p one

-- ¬ß Every reflexive chain extends by one step.
Reach-extend : {s t : DriftState} ‚Üí Reach s t ‚Üí Reach s (stepState t)
Reach-extend stop     = next one
Reach-extend (next p) = next (Reach‚Āļ-extend p)

-- ¬ß Strict reachability as an infix operator.
infix 20 _‚Čļ_
_‚Čļ_ : DriftState ‚Üí DriftState ‚Üí SetŌČ
s ‚Čļ t = Reach‚Āļ s t

-- ¬ß Strict reachability is transitive (infix form).
‚Čļ-trans : {s t u : DriftState} ‚Üí (s ‚Čļ t) ‚Üí (t ‚Čļ u) ‚Üí (s ‚Čļ u)
‚Čļ-trans p q = Reach‚Āļ-trans p q

-- ¬ß Every drift state has a strict successor.
drift-progress : (s : DriftState) ‚Üí s ‚Čļ stepState s
drift-progress s = one

-- ¬ß Terminal state: no strict successor exists.
Terminal : DriftState ‚Üí SetŌČ
Terminal s = (t : DriftState) ‚Üí (s ‚Čļ t) ‚Üí ‚ä•

-- ¬ß No terminal state exists (internal proof).
no-terminal : (s : DriftState) ‚Üí Terminal s ‚Üí ‚ä•
no-terminal s term = term (stepState s) (drift-progress s)

-- ¬ß Reachability induces carrier embedding (strict).
reach‚Āļ-embed : {s t : DriftState} ‚Üí Reach‚Āļ s t ‚Üí Carrier s ‚Üí Carrier t
reach‚Āļ-embed {s} one      x = state-embed s x
reach‚Āļ-embed {s} (more p) x = reach‚Āļ-embed p (state-embed s x)

-- ¬ß Reachability induces carrier embedding (reflexive).
reach-embed : {s t : DriftState} ‚Üí Reach s t ‚Üí Carrier s ‚Üí Carrier t
reach-embed {s} stop     x = x
reach-embed {s} (next p) x = reach‚Āļ-embed p x
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 5.0: Drift Admits No Terminal State}
\label{law:reachability:no-terminal-state}

If a terminal state existed, then the strict successor supplied by
\texttt{Reach‚Āļ.one} would contradict terminality.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Law 5.0: Drift admits no terminal state.
law5-0-no-terminal : (s : DriftState) ‚Üí Terminal s ‚Üí ‚ä•
law5-0-no-terminal = no-terminal
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 5.1: Strict Successors Are Comparable}
\label{law:reachability:strict-successors-comparable}

Any two states strictly reachable from the same source are comparable:
one reaches the other. This reflects the linear structure of drift. Each
state has a single immediate successor, so the reachability chain does
not branch.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Law 5.1: Strict successors are comparable.
law5-1-comparable :
  {s t‚āĀ t‚āā : DriftState} ‚Üí
  s ‚Čļ t‚āĀ ‚Üí
  s ‚Čļ t‚āā ‚Üí
  (Reach t‚āĀ t‚āā) ‚äéŌČ (Reach t‚āā t‚āĀ)
law5-1-comparable = Reach‚Āļ-comparable
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 5.2: Every Finite Chain Extends}
\label{law:reachability:finite-chain-extends}

Every finite chain of drift steps can be extended by one more step. This
is the formal expression of the fact that drift does not terminate after
finitely many stages.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Law 5.2: Every finite chain extends (strict).
law5-2-extend‚Āļ :
  {s t : DriftState} ‚Üí
  s ‚Čļ t ‚Üí
  s ‚Čļ stepState t
law5-2-extend‚Āļ p = Reach‚Āļ-extend p

-- ¬ß Law 5.2: Every finite chain extends (reflexive).
law5-2-extend :
  {s t : DriftState} ‚Üí
  Reach s t ‚Üí
  Reach s (stepState t)
law5-2-extend = Reach-extend
\end{code}

\subsection{Laws 5.3--5.7: Closure and Induction}
\label{laws:reachability:closure-induction}

The remaining structural laws are direct consequences of the inductive
definitions: transitivity of strict and reflexive reachability, the
carrier embedding determined by each reachability witness, and the
eliminators for both \texttt{Reach‚Āļ} and \texttt{Reach}. Together they
close the reachability algebra and provide the induction principles used
in all later arguments over drift chains.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Law 5.3: Strict reachability is transitive.
law5-3-‚Čļ-trans : {s t u : DriftState} ‚Üí (s ‚Čļ t) ‚Üí (t ‚Čļ u) ‚Üí (s ‚Čļ u)
law5-3-‚Čļ-trans = ‚Čļ-trans

-- ¬ß Law 5.4: Reachability is transitive.
law5-4-reach-trans : {s t u : DriftState} ‚Üí Reach s t ‚Üí Reach t u ‚Üí Reach s u
law5-4-reach-trans = reach-trans

-- ¬ß Law 5.5: Reachability forces carrier-embedding.
law5-5-reach-embed : {s t : DriftState} ‚Üí Reach s t ‚Üí Carrier s ‚Üí Carrier t
law5-5-reach-embed = reach-embed

-- ¬ß Law 5.6: Reach‚Āļ eliminator is forced.
law5-6-Reach‚Āļ-elim :
  {P : (s t : DriftState) ‚Üí Reach‚Āļ s t ‚Üí SetŌČ} ‚Üí
  ({s : DriftState} ‚Üí P s (stepState s) one) ‚Üí
  ({s t : DriftState} ‚Üí (p : Reach‚Āļ (stepState s) t) ‚Üí P (stepState s) t p ‚Üí P s t (more p)) ‚Üí
  {s t : DriftState} ‚Üí (p : Reach‚Āļ s t) ‚Üí P s t p
law5-6-Reach‚Āļ-elim = Reach‚Āļ-elim

-- ¬ß Law 5.7: Reach eliminator is forced.
law5-7-Reach-elim :
  {P : (s t : DriftState) ‚Üí Reach s t ‚Üí SetŌČ} ‚Üí
  ({s : DriftState} ‚Üí P s s stop) ‚Üí
  ({s t : DriftState} ‚Üí (p : Reach‚Āļ s t) ‚Üí P s t (next p)) ‚Üí
  {s t : DriftState} ‚Üí (p : Reach s t) ‚Üí P s t p
law5-7-Reach-elim = Reach-elim
\end{code}

the chain might loop back on itself.
The reachability structure is now explicit: every finite chain extends,
strict successors are comparable, and the relevant eliminators are in
place. What remains is to exclude the residual hazard that the chain
might loop back on itself.

\chapter{Drift Acyclicity}
\label{ch:drift-acyclicity}

To keep the hierarchy meaningful, repeated drift must not fold back on itself. This chapter records acyclicity as an explicit constraint and derives its standard consequences for the strict reachability relation.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Acyclicity constraint on drift.
record DriftAcyclic : SetŌČ where
  field
    no-cycle : (s : DriftState) ‚Üí (s ‚Čļ s) ‚Üí ‚ä•

open DriftAcyclic public
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 6.0: Drift Has No Cycles}
\label{law:acyclicity:no-cycles}

the \texttt{DriftAcyclic} record.
Acyclicity is recorded as a constraint: any cycle $s \prec s$ leads to
absurdity. The implementation extracts that contradiction from the
\texttt{DriftAcyclic} record.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Law 6.0: Drift has no cycles.
law6-0-no-cycle : DriftAcyclic ‚Üí (s : DriftState) ‚Üí (s ‚Čļ s) ‚Üí ‚ä•
law6-0-no-cycle ac s p = no-cycle ac s p
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 6.1: Strict Reachability Is Irreflexive}
\label{law:acyclicity:irreflexive}

Irreflexivity is the immediate instance of acyclicity: $s \prec s$ is itself a cycle of length one.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Law 6.1: Strict reachability is irreflexive.
law6-1-irreflexive : DriftAcyclic ‚Üí (s : DriftState) ‚Üí (s ‚Čļ s) ‚Üí ‚ä•
law6-1-irreflexive ac s = no-cycle ac s
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 6.2: Strict Reachability Is Asymmetric}
\label{law:acyclicity:asymmetric}

If both $s \prec t$ and $t \prec s$ held, transitivity would produce the cycle $s \prec s$, contradicting acyclicity.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Law 6.2: Strict reachability is asymmetric.
law6-2-asymmetric :
  DriftAcyclic ‚Üí {s t : DriftState} ‚Üí
  (s ‚Čļ t) ‚Üí (t ‚Čļ s) ‚Üí ‚ä•
law6-2-asymmetric ac p q = no-cycle ac _ (‚Čļ-trans p q)
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 6.3: Drift-Step Has No Fixed Point}
\label{law:acyclicity:no-fixed-point}

If \texttt{stepState}~$s$ equalled $s$, then the single-step witness \texttt{one} could be transported into a self-loop $s \prec s$, contradicting acyclicity.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Law 6.3: Drift-step has no fixed point.
law6-3-no-fixpoint-stepState : DriftAcyclic ‚Üí (s : DriftState) ‚Üí stepState s ‚Č°ŌČ s ‚Üí ‚ä•
law6-3-no-fixpoint-stepState ac s eq =
  no-cycle ac s (substŌČ (őĽ u ‚Üí Reach‚Āļ s u) eq one)
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 6.4: Drift Is a Strict Total Order}
\label{law:acyclicity:strict-total-order}

Taken together, transitivity, irreflexivity, asymmetry, and
comparability show that strict reachability behaves like a strict total
order along each drift chain. The record below packages those properties
for later reference.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Strict total order on drift states
record DriftStrictTotalOrder (ac : DriftAcyclic) : SetŌČ where
  field
    sto-trans       : {s t u : DriftState} ‚Üí (s ‚Čļ t) ‚Üí (t ‚Čļ u) ‚Üí (s ‚Čļ u)
    sto-irrefl      : (s : DriftState) ‚Üí (s ‚Čļ s) ‚Üí ‚ä•
    sto-asym        : {s t : DriftState} ‚Üí (s ‚Čļ t) ‚Üí (t ‚Čļ s) ‚Üí ‚ä•
    sto-comparable  : {s t‚āĀ t‚āā : DriftState} ‚Üí
                       s ‚Čļ t‚āĀ ‚Üí s ‚Čļ t‚āā ‚Üí (Reach t‚āĀ t‚āā) ‚äéŌČ (Reach t‚āā t‚āĀ)

-- ¬ß Theorem: drift is a strict total order
theorem-drift-strict-total-order :
  (ac : DriftAcyclic) ‚Üí DriftStrictTotalOrder ac
theorem-drift-strict-total-order ac = record
  { sto-trans      = law5-3-‚Čļ-trans
  ; sto-irrefl     = law6-1-irreflexive ac
  ; sto-asym       = law6-2-asymmetric ac
  ; sto-comparable = law5-1-comparable
  }
\end{code}

The formal picture is now clear. Drift produces a linear hierarchy, the
hierarchy never terminates after finitely many stages, and the
acyclicity constraint prevents it from folding back into itself.

The next part extends the same classification ideas from endomorphisms to maps between different distinction carriers.

\part{Heteromorphisms and Presentation}

The previous parts classified self-maps of a single distinction and analysed the hierarchy generated by drift. The next step is to consider maps between different distinctions. Although the source and target carriers need not coincide, the same two-by-two boundary combinatorics remain in force.

\chapter{Heteromorphisms}
\label{ch:heteromorphisms}

For maps $f : S_{d_1} \to S_{d_2}$ between two possibly different
distinctions, the same counting principle still applies: the map is
determined by the images of the two source boundary points, and each
image lies at one of the two target boundary points. There are therefore
again exactly four cases.

The code below introduces these four maps explicitly. They play for
heteromorphisms the same role that \texttt{constL}, \texttt{constR},
\texttt{id}, and \texttt{dual} played for endomorphisms.

\paragraph{Step 1: Map type and canonical inter-distinction maps.}
The map type, pointwise equivalence, and four reference maps between two
distinction carriers mirror the endomorphism case. The two mixed maps
\texttt{LR} and \texttt{RL} use source-side coverage to assign the
appropriate target boundary witness.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß K‚āĄ maps between two distinctions.
module K‚āĄMap (d‚āĀ d‚āā : Distinction) where
  Map : Set
  Map = S d‚āĀ ‚Üí S d‚āā

  -- ¬ß Pointwise equality on maps.
  ‚Čó-refl : {f : Map} ‚Üí f ‚Čó f
  ‚Čó-refl x = refl

  ‚Čó-sym : {f g : Map} ‚Üí f ‚Čó g ‚Üí g ‚Čó f
  ‚Čó-sym p x = sym (p x)

  ‚Čó-trans : {f g h : Map} ‚Üí f ‚Čó g ‚Üí g ‚Čó h ‚Üí f ‚Čó h
  ‚Čó-trans p q x = trans (p x) (q x)

  -- ¬ß The four canonical maps.
  constL : Map
  constL _ = ‚Ąď d‚āā

  constR : Map
  constR _ = r d‚āā

  LR : Map
  LR x with cover d‚āĀ x
  ... | inj‚āĀ _ = ‚Ąď d‚āā
  ... | inj‚āā _ = r d‚āā

  RL : Map
  RL x with cover d‚āĀ x
  ... | inj‚āĀ _ = r d‚āā
  ... | inj‚āā _ = ‚Ąď d‚āā

  -- ¬ß Boundary behavior of LR.
  LR-‚Ąď : LR (‚Ąď d‚āĀ) ‚Č° ‚Ąď d‚āā
  LR-‚Ąď with cover d‚āĀ (‚Ąď d‚āĀ)
  ... | inj‚āĀ _   = refl
  ... | inj‚āā ‚Ąď‚Č°r = ‚ä•-elim ((‚Ąď‚Č†r d‚āĀ) ‚Ąď‚Č°r)

  LR-r : LR (r d‚āĀ) ‚Č° r d‚āā
  LR-r with cover d‚āĀ (r d‚āĀ)
  ... | inj‚āĀ r‚Č°‚Ąď = ‚ä•-elim ((‚Ąď‚Č†r d‚āĀ) (sym r‚Č°‚Ąď))
  ... | inj‚āā _   = refl

  -- ¬ß Boundary behavior of RL.
  RL-‚Ąď : RL (‚Ąď d‚āĀ) ‚Č° r d‚āā
  RL-‚Ąď with cover d‚āĀ (‚Ąď d‚āĀ)
  ... | inj‚āĀ _   = refl
  ... | inj‚āā ‚Ąď‚Č°r = ‚ä•-elim ((‚Ąď‚Č†r d‚āĀ) ‚Ąď‚Č°r)

  RL-r : RL (r d‚āĀ) ‚Č° ‚Ąď d‚āā
  RL-r with cover d‚āĀ (r d‚āĀ)
  ... | inj‚āĀ r‚Č°‚Ąď = ‚ä•-elim ((‚Ąď‚Č†r d‚āĀ) (sym r‚Č°‚Ąď))
  ... | inj‚āā _   = refl
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 2: Interpretation and classification of inter-distinction maps.}
Each \texttt{EndoCase} label interprets as one of the four canonical
maps, and every concrete map is classified by its boundary outputs.

\begin{code}
  -- ¬ß Interpret an EndoCase as a map between distinctions.
  interpret : EndoCase ‚Üí Map
  interpret case-constL = constL
  interpret case-constR = constR
  interpret case-id     = LR
  interpret case-dual   = RL

  -- ¬ß Classify a map by its boundary behavior.
  classify : Map ‚Üí EndoCase
  classify f with cover d‚āā (f (‚Ąď d‚āĀ)) | cover d‚āā (f (r d‚āĀ))
  ... | inj‚āĀ _ | inj‚āĀ _ = case-constL
  ... | inj‚āā _ | inj‚āā _ = case-constR
  ... | inj‚āĀ _ | inj‚āā _ = case-id
  ... | inj‚āā _ | inj‚āĀ _ = case-dual
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 3: Soundness of inter-distinction classification.}
Classification followed by interpretation recovers the original map at
both boundary witnesses.

\begin{code}
  -- ¬ß Soundness at ‚Ąď.
  sound-at-‚Ąď : (f : Map) ‚Üí interpret (classify f) (‚Ąď d‚āĀ) ‚Č° f (‚Ąď d‚āĀ)
  sound-at-‚Ąď f with cover d‚āā (f (‚Ąď d‚āĀ)) | cover d‚āā (f (r d‚āĀ))
  ... | inj‚āĀ fl‚Č°‚Ąď | inj‚āĀ _     = sym fl‚Č°‚Ąď
  ... | inj‚āā fl‚Č°r | inj‚āā _     = sym fl‚Č°r
  ... | inj‚āĀ fl‚Č°‚Ąď | inj‚āā _     = trans LR-‚Ąď (sym fl‚Č°‚Ąď)
  ... | inj‚āā fl‚Č°r | inj‚āĀ _     = trans RL-‚Ąď (sym fl‚Č°r)

  -- ¬ß Soundness at r.
  sound-at-r : (f : Map) ‚Üí interpret (classify f) (r d‚āĀ) ‚Č° f (r d‚āĀ)
  sound-at-r f with cover d‚āā (f (‚Ąď d‚āĀ)) | cover d‚āā (f (r d‚āĀ))
  ... | inj‚āĀ _     | inj‚āĀ fr‚Č°‚Ąď = sym fr‚Č°‚Ąď
  ... | inj‚āā _     | inj‚āā fr‚Č°r = sym fr‚Č°r
  ... | inj‚āĀ _     | inj‚āā fr‚Č°r = trans LR-r (sym fr‚Č°r)
  ... | inj‚āā _     | inj‚āĀ fr‚Č°‚Ąď = trans RL-r (sym fr‚Č°‚Ąď)

  -- ¬ß Classification is sound: the interpreted classify agrees pointwise with f.
  classify-sound : (f : Map) ‚Üí interpret (classify f) ‚Čó f
  classify-sound f x = Distinction-elim d‚āĀ (sound-at-‚Ąď f) (sound-at-r f) x

  -- ¬ß Maps are determined by their boundary values.
  map-determined :
    (f g : Map) ‚Üí
    f (‚Ąď d‚āĀ) ‚Č° g (‚Ąď d‚āĀ) ‚Üí
    f (r d‚āĀ) ‚Č° g (r d‚āĀ) ‚Üí
    f ‚Čó g
  map-determined f g eq‚Ąď eqr x = Distinction-elim d‚āĀ eq‚Ąď eqr x

  -- ¬ß Absurdity helpers.
  absurd-‚Ąďr : {A : Set} ‚Üí (‚Ąď d‚āā ‚Č° r d‚āā) ‚Üí A
  absurd-‚Ąďr e = ‚ä•-elim ((‚Ąď‚Č†r d‚āā) e)

  absurd-r‚Ąď : {A : Set} ‚Üí (r d‚āā ‚Č° ‚Ąď d‚āā) ‚Üí A
  absurd-r‚Ąď e = ‚ä•-elim ((‚Ąď‚Č†r d‚āā) (sym e))
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 4: Injectivity and uniqueness.}
Interpretation is injective because different case labels produce
different boundary behaviour in the target. Combined with soundness,
this yields uniqueness of classification.

\begin{code}
  interpret-injective :
    (c c' : EndoCase) ‚Üí
    interpret c ‚Čó interpret c' ‚Üí
    c ‚Č° c'
  interpret-injective case-constL case-constL _ = refl
  interpret-injective case-constL case-constR p = absurd-‚Ąďr (p (‚Ąď d‚āĀ))
  interpret-injective case-constL case-id     p =
    absurd-‚Ąďr (trans (p (r d‚āĀ)) LR-r)
  interpret-injective case-constL case-dual   p =
    absurd-‚Ąďr (trans (p (‚Ąď d‚āĀ)) RL-‚Ąď)

  interpret-injective case-constR case-constL p =
    absurd-r‚Ąď (p (‚Ąď d‚āĀ))
  interpret-injective case-constR case-constR _ = refl
  interpret-injective case-constR case-id     p =
    absurd-‚Ąďr (trans (sym LR-‚Ąď) (sym (p (‚Ąď d‚āĀ))))
  interpret-injective case-constR case-dual   p =
    absurd-‚Ąďr (sym (trans (p (r d‚āĀ)) RL-r))

  interpret-injective case-id     case-constL p =
    absurd-‚Ąďr (sym (trans (sym LR-r) (p (r d‚āĀ))))
  interpret-injective case-id     case-constR p =
    absurd-‚Ąďr (trans (sym LR-‚Ąď) (p (‚Ąď d‚āĀ)))
  interpret-injective case-id     case-id     _ = refl
  interpret-injective case-id     case-dual   p =
    absurd-‚Ąďr (trans (sym LR-‚Ąď) (trans (p (‚Ąď d‚āĀ)) RL-‚Ąď))

  interpret-injective case-dual   case-constL p =
    absurd-‚Ąďr (trans (sym (p (‚Ąď d‚āĀ))) RL-‚Ąď)
  interpret-injective case-dual   case-constR p =
    absurd-‚Ąďr (sym (trans (sym (p (r d‚āĀ))) RL-r))
  interpret-injective case-dual   case-id     p =
    absurd-‚Ąďr (sym (trans (sym RL-‚Ąď) (trans (p (‚Ąď d‚āĀ)) LR-‚Ąď)))
  interpret-injective case-dual   case-dual   _ = refl

  -- ¬ß Classify is unique relative to interpretation.
  classify-unique : (f : Map) ‚Üí (c : EndoCase) ‚Üí interpret c ‚Čó f ‚Üí c ‚Č° classify f
  classify-unique f c c‚Čóf =
    interpret-injective c (classify f) (‚Čó-trans c‚Čóf (‚Čó-sym (classify-sound f)))

  -- ¬ß Classify is a left-inverse of interpretation: forced by uniqueness on refl.
  classify-interpret : (c : EndoCase) ‚Üí classify (interpret c) ‚Č° c
  classify-interpret c = sym (classify-unique (interpret c) c ‚Čó-refl)

  -- ¬ß The bijection between EndoCase and (Map / ‚Čó) for inter-distinction maps.
  record MapBijection : Set where
    field
      to        : Map ‚Üí EndoCase
      from      : EndoCase ‚Üí Map
      to-from   : (c : EndoCase) ‚Üí to (from c) ‚Č° c
      from-to   : (f : Map) ‚Üí from (to f) ‚Čó f
      to-unique : (f : Map) ‚Üí (c : EndoCase) ‚Üí from c ‚Čó f ‚Üí c ‚Č° to f

  k4map-bijection : MapBijection
  k4map-bijection = record
    { to        = classify
    ; from      = interpret
    ; to-from   = classify-interpret
    ; from-to   = classify-sound
    ; to-unique = classify-unique
    }
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 7.1: A Map Is Determined By Boundary Values}
\label{law:heteromorphisms:boundary-determination}

Every input falls into one of the two boundary cases. Once the values at
$\ell$ and $r$ are fixed, pointwise equality follows.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Top-level classification soundness.
k4map-classification-sound :
  (d‚āĀ d‚āā : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (f : S d‚āĀ ‚Üí S d‚āā) ‚Üí
  ő£ EndoCase (őĽ c ‚Üí K‚āĄMap.interpret d‚āĀ d‚āā c ‚Čó f)
k4map-classification-sound d‚āĀ d‚āā f =
  K‚āĄMap.classify d‚āĀ d‚āā f , K‚āĄMap.classify-sound d‚āĀ d‚āā f

-- ¬ß Top-level classification uniqueness.
k4map-classification-unique :
  (d‚āĀ d‚āā : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (f : S d‚āĀ ‚Üí S d‚āā) ‚Üí
  (c‚āĀ c‚āā : EndoCase) ‚Üí
  K‚āĄMap.interpret d‚āĀ d‚āā c‚āĀ ‚Čó f ‚Üí
  K‚āĄMap.interpret d‚āĀ d‚āā c‚āā ‚Čó f ‚Üí
  c‚āĀ ‚Č° c‚āā
k4map-classification-unique d‚āĀ d‚āā f c‚āĀ c‚āā p‚āĀ p‚āā =
  K‚āĄMap.interpret-injective d‚āĀ d‚āā c‚āĀ c‚āā (K‚āĄMap.‚Čó-trans d‚āĀ d‚āā p‚āĀ (K‚āĄMap.‚Čó-sym d‚āĀ d‚āā p‚āā))

-- ¬ß Law 7.1: A map is determined by boundary values.
law7-1-map-determined :
  (d‚āĀ d‚āā : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (f g : S d‚āĀ ‚Üí S d‚āā) ‚Üí
  f (‚Ąď d‚āĀ) ‚Č° g (‚Ąď d‚āĀ) ‚Üí
  f (r d‚āĀ) ‚Č° g (r d‚āĀ) ‚Üí
  f ‚Čó g
law7-1-map-determined d‚āĀ d‚āā = K‚āĄMap.map-determined d‚āĀ d‚āā
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 7.2: K\texorpdfstring{$_4$}{4} Classification Produces A Witness For Any Map}
\label{law:heteromorphisms:k4-witness-sound}

Every map between two distinctions receives a classification witness: a
case label paired with a proof that the labeled reference map agrees
with the given map at every point. The proof strategy is the same as in
the endomorphism case: inspect the two boundary outputs, match the
resulting pattern, and conclude pointwise.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Law 7.2: K‚āĄ classification produces a witness for any map.
law7-2-k4map-classification-sound :
  (d‚āĀ d‚āā : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (f : S d‚āĀ ‚Üí S d‚āā) ‚Üí
  ő£ EndoCase (őĽ c ‚Üí K‚āĄMap.interpret d‚āĀ d‚āā c ‚Čó f)
law7-2-k4map-classification-sound = k4map-classification-sound
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 7.3: K\texorpdfstring{$_4$}{4} Witness For Maps Is Unique}
\label{law:heteromorphisms:k4-witness-unique}

Two distinct case labels cannot interpret to the same map. If they did,
their boundary outputs would agree simultaneously, forcing the two
boundary witnesses in the codomain to be equal and contradicting
$\ell \neq r$.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Law 7.3: K‚āĄ witness for maps is unique.
law7-3-k4map-classification-unique :
  (d‚āĀ d‚āā : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (f : S d‚āĀ ‚Üí S d‚āā) ‚Üí
  (c‚āĀ c‚āā : EndoCase) ‚Üí
  K‚āĄMap.interpret d‚āĀ d‚āā c‚āĀ ‚Čó f ‚Üí
  K‚āĄMap.interpret d‚āĀ d‚āā c‚āā ‚Čó f ‚Üí
  c‚āĀ ‚Č° c‚āā
law7-3-k4map-classification-unique = k4map-classification-unique
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 7.4: Map Presentation Is Unique Up To Isomorphism}
\label{law:heteromorphisms:presentation-unique}

Any alternative presentation of the four-case map classification, using
a different carrier type $X$ together with its own interpretation and
classification functions, is isomorphic to \texttt{EndoCase}. The proof
constructs the isomorphism by composing the alternative classifier with
the fixed interpreter and conversely, then uses soundness and
injectivity to verify the two round trips.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Map presentation record.
record MapPresentation (d‚āĀ d‚āā : Distinction) (X : Set) : Set where
  field
    mp-interpret            : X ‚Üí (S d‚āĀ ‚Üí S d‚āā)
    mp-classify             : (S d‚āĀ ‚Üí S d‚āā) ‚Üí X
    mp-classify-sound       : (f : S d‚āĀ ‚Üí S d‚āā) ‚Üí mp-interpret (mp-classify f) ‚Čó f
    mp-interpret-injective  : (x y : X) ‚Üí mp-interpret x ‚Čó mp-interpret y ‚Üí x ‚Č° y

open MapPresentation public

-- ¬ß Law 7.4: Map presentation is unique up to isomorphism.
law7-4-map-presentation-unique :
  (d‚āĀ d‚āā : Distinction) ‚Üí
  {X : Set} ‚Üí
  MapPresentation d‚āĀ d‚āā X ‚Üí
  SetIso X EndoCase
law7-4-map-presentation-unique d‚āĀ d‚āā {X} pres =
  let
    module K = K‚āĄMap d‚āĀ d‚āā

    to' : X ‚Üí EndoCase
    to' x = K.classify (MapPresentation.mp-interpret pres x)

    from' : EndoCase ‚Üí X
    from' c = MapPresentation.mp-classify pres (K.interpret c)

    to-from' : (c : EndoCase) ‚Üí to' (from' c) ‚Č° c
    to-from' c =
      sym
        (K.classify-unique
          (MapPresentation.mp-interpret pres (MapPresentation.mp-classify pres (K.interpret c)))
          c
          (K.‚Čó-sym (MapPresentation.mp-classify-sound pres (K.interpret c))))

    from-to' : (x : X) ‚Üí from' (to' x) ‚Č° x
    from-to' x =
      MapPresentation.mp-interpret-injective pres
        (MapPresentation.mp-classify pres (K.interpret (K.classify (MapPresentation.mp-interpret pres x))))
        x
        (K.‚Čó-trans
          (MapPresentation.mp-classify-sound pres (K.interpret (K.classify (MapPresentation.mp-interpret pres x))))
          (K.classify-sound (MapPresentation.mp-interpret pres x)))
  in
  record
    { to = to'
    ; from = from'
    ; to-from = to-from'
    ; from-to = from-to'
    }
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 7.5: Map Classification Is a Bijection}
\label{law:heteromorphisms:k4-map-bijection}

The inter-distinction classifier satisfies the same round-trip property
as the endomorphism classifier. In particular, classification is a left
inverse to interpretation, so the four map cases again give a bijective
description of maps modulo pointwise equality.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Law 7.5: classify ‚ąė interpret = id at the case-label level for inter-distinction maps.
law7-5-k4map-classify-interpret :
  (d‚āĀ d‚āā : Distinction) ‚Üí
  (c : EndoCase) ‚Üí
  K‚āĄMap.classify d‚āĀ d‚āā (K‚āĄMap.interpret d‚āĀ d‚āā c) ‚Č° c
law7-5-k4map-classify-interpret d‚āĀ d‚āā = K‚āĄMap.classify-interpret d‚āĀ d‚āā
\end{code}

The four-case classification therefore extends from endomorphisms to
arbitrary maps between distinct carriers. The same four cases appear,
and the same uniqueness statement remains valid. The classification is
therefore structural rather than an artifact of the self-mapping case.

\chapter{Indexed Ledger}
\label{ch:indexed-ledger}

The drift hierarchy can be indexed explicitly by natural numbers. This
does not alter the underlying drift structure, but it provides a
bookkeeping device for later arguments about monotonicity and
acyclicity.

The record \texttt{DriftState‚Ąē} pairs an ordinary drift state with a
natural-number tick. The successor operation increments both components
in parallel, and the indexed reachability relation tracks strict
progression along this ledger.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Natural numbers.
data ‚Ąē : Set where
  zero : ‚Ąē
  suc  : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē

-- ¬ß Standard ordering on ‚Ąē.
infix 4 _‚Č§_
data _‚Č§_ : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí Set where
  z‚Č§n : {n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí zero ‚Č§ n
  s‚Č§s : {m n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí m ‚Č§ n ‚Üí suc m ‚Č§ suc n

-- ¬ß ‚Č§ is reflexive.
‚Č§-refl : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí n ‚Č§ n
‚Č§-refl zero    = z‚Č§n
‚Č§-refl (suc n) = s‚Č§s (‚Č§-refl n)

-- ¬ß ‚Č§ is transitive.
‚Č§-trans : {a b c : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí a ‚Č§ b ‚Üí b ‚Č§ c ‚Üí a ‚Č§ c
‚Č§-trans z‚Č§n       _          = z‚Č§n
‚Č§-trans (s‚Č§s p) (s‚Č§s q) = s‚Č§s (‚Č§-trans p q)

-- ¬ß Step lemma.
‚Č§-step : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí n ‚Č§ suc n
‚Č§-step zero    = z‚Č§n
‚Č§-step (suc n) = s‚Č§s (‚Č§-step n)

-- ¬ß suc n ‚Č§ n is absurd.
suc‚Č§-impossible : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí suc n ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ‚ä•
suc‚Č§-impossible zero ()
suc‚Č§-impossible (suc n) (s‚Č§s p) = suc‚Č§-impossible n p

-- ¬ß Tick-indexed drift state.
record DriftState‚Ąē : SetŌČ where
  constructor ‚ü™_,_‚üę
  field
    tick : ‚Ąē
    base : DriftState

open DriftState‚Ąē public

-- ¬ß Step the indexed state forward.
stepState‚Ąē : DriftState‚Ąē ‚Üí DriftState‚Ąē
stepState‚Ąē s = ‚ü™ suc (tick s) , stepState (base s) ‚üę

-- ¬ß Extract the carrier of an indexed state.
Carrier‚Ąē : (s : DriftState‚Ąē) ‚Üí Set (‚Ąď (base s))
Carrier‚Ąē s = Carrier (base s)

-- ¬ß Embed between consecutive indexed carriers.
state-embed‚Ąē : (s : DriftState‚Ąē) ‚Üí Carrier‚Ąē s ‚Üí Carrier‚Ąē (stepState‚Ąē s)
state-embed‚Ąē s = state-embed (base s)

-- ¬ß Strict reachability on indexed states.
data Reach‚Āļ‚Ąē : DriftState‚Ąē ‚Üí DriftState‚Ąē ‚Üí SetŌČ where
  one‚Ąē  : {s : DriftState‚Ąē} ‚Üí Reach‚Āļ‚Ąē s (stepState‚Ąē s)
  more‚Ąē : {s t : DriftState‚Ąē} ‚Üí Reach‚Āļ‚Ąē (stepState‚Ąē s) t ‚Üí Reach‚Āļ‚Ąē s t

-- ¬ß Strict reachability as infix.
infix 20 _‚Čļ‚Ąē_
_‚Čļ‚Ąē_ : DriftState‚Ąē ‚Üí DriftState‚Ąē ‚Üí SetŌČ
s ‚Čļ‚Ąē t = Reach‚Āļ‚Ąē s t
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 8.0: Tick Strictly Increases Along Reach\texorpdfstring{$^+_{\mathbb{N}}$}{+N}}
\label{law:indexed-ledger:tick-increases}

The key property of the indexed ledger is that every strict reachability
witness increases the tick. This is proved directly by induction on the
indexed reach proof.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Law 8.0: Tick strictly increases along Reach‚Āļ‚Ąē.
law8-0-tick-increases : {s t : DriftState‚Ąē} ‚Üí (s ‚Čļ‚Ąē t) ‚Üí suc (tick s) ‚Č§ tick t
law8-0-tick-increases one‚Ąē = ‚Č§-refl _
law8-0-tick-increases {s} (more‚Ąē p) =
  ‚Č§-trans (‚Č§-step (suc (tick s))) (law8-0-tick-increases p)
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 8.1: Indexed Drift Has No Cycles}
\label{law:indexed-ledger:no-cycles}

The indexed acyclicity statement is then immediate: a cycle would force
$\mathrm{suc}\,(\mathrm{tick}\,s) \le \mathrm{tick}\,s$, which is
impossible in \texttt{‚Ąē}.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Law 8.1: Indexed drift has no cycles.
law8-1-no-cycle‚Ąē : (s : DriftState‚Ąē) ‚Üí (s ‚Čļ‚Ąē s) ‚Üí ‚ä•
law8-1-no-cycle‚Ąē s p = suc‚Č§-impossible (tick s) (law8-0-tick-increases p)
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 6.6 (deferred): Tick Presents Every Drift Orbit As \texorpdfstring{$\omega$}{omega}}
\label{law:indexed-ledger:tick-iso-omega}

Fixing a base state produces a single drift orbit. The indexed
presentation of that orbit is \emph{ŌČ}-shaped: the natural number
parameter records how many drift steps have been taken from the base.

The definitions and lemmas below make this correspondence explicit by
relating strict indexed reachability on the orbit to the standard order
on \texttt{‚Ąē}.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Iterate drift states from a chosen base.
orbitBase : ‚Ąē ‚Üí DriftState ‚Üí DriftState
orbitBase zero    b = b
orbitBase (suc n) b = stepState (orbitBase n b)

-- ¬ß The tick-indexed orbit generated by a chosen base.
orbitState : DriftState ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí DriftState‚Ąē
orbitState b n = ‚ü™ n , orbitBase n b ‚üę

-- ¬ß Equality of orbit indices lifts to SetŌČ-equality of orbit states.
orbitState-congŌČ : (b : DriftState) ‚Üí {n m : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí n ‚Č° m ‚Üí orbitState b n ‚Č°ŌČ orbitState b m
orbitState-congŌČ b refl = reflŌČ

-- ¬ß Tick reads off the index of the orbit state definitionally.
orbit-tick : (b : DriftState) ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí tick (orbitState b n) ‚Č° n
orbit-tick b n = refl

-- ¬ß Orbit successor is definitionally the indexed drift step.
orbit-step : (b : DriftState) ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí orbitState b (suc n) ‚Č°ŌČ stepState‚Ąē (orbitState b n)
orbit-step b n = reflŌČ

-- ¬ß Advance an index by a finite number of successor steps.
advance‚Ąē : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē
advance‚Ąē zero    n = n
advance‚Ąē (suc k) n = suc (advance‚Ąē k n)

-- ¬ß Local truncated subtraction for the orbit theorem.
diff‚Ąē : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē
diff‚Ąē zero    n       = zero
diff‚Ąē (suc m) zero    = suc m
diff‚Ąē (suc m) (suc n) = diff‚Ąē m n

-- ¬ß Advancing after a successor is the successor of advancing.
advance‚Ąē-suc-right : (k n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí advance‚Ąē k (suc n) ‚Č° suc (advance‚Ąē k n)
advance‚Ąē-suc-right zero    n = refl
advance‚Ąē-suc-right (suc k) n = cong suc (advance‚Ąē-suc-right k n)

-- ¬ß Shifting the offset by one step can be moved from the offset slot to the base slot.
advance‚Ąē-shift : (k n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí advance‚Ąē (suc k) n ‚Č° advance‚Ąē k (suc n)
advance‚Ąē-shift zero    n = refl
advance‚Ąē-shift (suc k) n = cong suc (advance‚Ąē-shift k n)

-- ¬ß Advancing zero by k steps returns k.
advance‚Ąē-zero : (k : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí advance‚Ąē k zero ‚Č° k
advance‚Ąē-zero zero    = refl
advance‚Ąē-zero (suc k) = cong suc (advance‚Ąē-zero k)

-- ¬ß Any ‚Č§-witness splits the upper index into the lower index plus a finite advance.
‚Č§-advance-split : {n m : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí n ‚Č§ m ‚Üí advance‚Ąē (diff‚Ąē m n) n ‚Č° m
‚Č§-advance-split {zero}  {zero}  z‚Č§n = advance‚Ąē-zero zero
‚Č§-advance-split {zero}  {suc m} z‚Č§n = advance‚Ąē-zero (suc m)
‚Č§-advance-split {suc n} {zero}  ()
‚Č§-advance-split {suc n} {suc m} (s‚Č§s p) =
  trans (advance‚Ąē-suc-right (diff‚Ąē m n) n) (cong suc (‚Č§-advance-split p))

-- ¬ß Local indexed transitivity for the orbit block.
Reach‚Āļ‚Ąē-trans-local : {s t u : DriftState‚Ąē} ‚Üí (s ‚Čļ‚Ąē t) ‚Üí (t ‚Čļ‚Ąē u) ‚Üí (s ‚Čļ‚Ąē u)
Reach‚Āļ‚Ąē-trans-local one‚Ąē      q = more‚Ąē q
Reach‚Āļ‚Ąē-trans-local (more‚Ąē p) q = more‚Ąē (Reach‚Āļ‚Ąē-trans-local p q)

-- ¬ß Local indexed extension for the orbit block.
Reach‚Āļ‚Ąē-extend-local : {s t : DriftState‚Ąē} ‚Üí (s ‚Čļ‚Ąē t) ‚Üí (s ‚Čļ‚Ąē stepState‚Ąē t)
Reach‚Āļ‚Ąē-extend-local p = Reach‚Āļ‚Ąē-trans-local p one‚Ąē

-- ¬ß Every positive offset along the orbit yields a strict indexed reach proof.
orbit-reach : (b : DriftState) ‚Üí (n k : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí orbitState b n ‚Čļ‚Ąē orbitState b (advance‚Ąē (suc k) n)
orbit-reach b n zero    = one‚Ąē
orbit-reach b n (suc k) = Reach‚Āļ‚Ąē-extend-local (orbit-reach b n k)

-- ¬ß Any strict indexed reach on the orbit forces a positive tick increase.
orbit-reach-complete :
  (b : DriftState) ‚Üí {n m : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí
  orbitState b n ‚Čļ‚Ąē orbitState b m ‚Üí suc n ‚Č§ m
orbit-reach-complete b p = law8-0-tick-increases p

-- ¬ß Any positive index difference on the orbit yields a strict indexed reach proof.
orbit-reach-sound :
  (b : DriftState) ‚Üí {n m : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí
  suc n ‚Č§ m ‚Üí orbitState b n ‚Čļ‚Ąē orbitState b m
orbit-reach-sound b {n} {m} le =
  substŌČ
    (őĽ u ‚Üí orbitState b n ‚Čļ‚Ąē u)
    (orbitState-congŌČ b
      (trans (advance‚Ąē-shift (diff‚Ąē m (suc n)) n)
             (‚Č§-advance-split le)))
    (orbit-reach b n (diff‚Ąē m (suc n)))

-- ¬ß Tick presents the orbit over b as an ŌČ-shaped indexed chain.
record TickIsoŌČ (b : DriftState) : SetŌČ where
  field
    stateAt        : ‚Ąē ‚Üí DriftState‚Ąē
    tickAt         : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí tick (stateAt n) ‚Č° n
    stepAt         : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí stateAt (suc n) ‚Č°ŌČ stepState‚Ąē (stateAt n)
    reachAt        : (n k : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí stateAt n ‚Čļ‚Ąē stateAt (advance‚Ąē (suc k) n)
    reachSound     : {n m : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí suc n ‚Č§ m ‚Üí stateAt n ‚Čļ‚Ąē stateAt m
    reachComplete  : {n m : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí stateAt n ‚Čļ‚Ąē stateAt m ‚Üí suc n ‚Č§ m

-- ¬ß Law 6.6: The drift orbit over any chosen base is indexed by ŌČ through tick.
law6-6-tick-iso-ŌČ : (b : DriftState) ‚Üí TickIsoŌČ b
law6-6-tick-iso-ŌČ b = record
  { stateAt       = orbitState b
  ; tickAt        = orbit-tick b
  ; stepAt        = orbit-step b
  ; reachAt       = orbit-reach b
  ; reachSound    = orbit-reach-sound b
  ; reachComplete = orbit-reach-complete b
  }
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 6.4 Refined: Orbit Trichotomy}
\label{law:acyclicity:orbit-trichotomy}

With the indexed orbit in hand, one obtains the expected trichotomy statement: along a fixed orbit, two states are either ordered one way, equal, or ordered the other way.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Strict trichotomy on ‚Ąē: every pair is lt, eq, or gt.
data ‚Ąē-Tri (n m : ‚Ąē) : Set where
  lt   : suc n ‚Č§ m ‚Üí ‚Ąē-Tri n m
  same : n ‚Č° m     ‚Üí ‚Ąē-Tri n m
  gt   : suc m ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ‚Ąē-Tri n m

‚Ąē-tri : (n m : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí ‚Ąē-Tri n m
‚Ąē-tri zero    zero    = same refl
‚Ąē-tri zero    (suc m) = lt (s‚Č§s z‚Č§n)
‚Ąē-tri (suc n) zero    = gt (s‚Č§s z‚Č§n)
‚Ąē-tri (suc n) (suc m) with ‚Ąē-tri n m
... | lt p   = lt (s‚Č§s p)
... | same p = same (cong suc p)
... | gt p   = gt (s‚Č§s p)

-- ¬ß Index equality implies orbit-state SetŌČ-equality.
orbitState-eq‚Ąē : (b : DriftState) ‚Üí {n m : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí n ‚Č° m ‚Üí orbitState b n ‚Č°ŌČ orbitState b m
orbitState-eq‚Ąē b refl = reflŌČ

-- ¬ß SetŌČ-level trichotomy witness for the orbit.
data OrbitTri (b : DriftState) (n m : ‚Ąē) : SetŌČ where
  orbit-lt : orbitState b n ‚Čļ‚Ąē orbitState b m ‚Üí OrbitTri b n m
  orbit-eq : orbitState b n ‚Č°ŌČ orbitState b m ‚Üí OrbitTri b n m
  orbit-gt : orbitState b m ‚Čļ‚Ąē orbitState b n ‚Üí OrbitTri b n m

-- ¬ß Law 6.4 (orbit refinement): the orbit over b is strictly totally ordered.
law6-4-orbit-total : (b : DriftState) ‚Üí (n m : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí OrbitTri b n m
law6-4-orbit-total b n m with ‚Ąē-tri n m
... | lt p   = orbit-lt (orbit-reach-sound b p)
... | same p = orbit-eq (orbitState-eq‚Ąē b p)
... | gt p   = orbit-gt (orbit-reach-sound b p)
\end{code}

\chapter{Presentation of Reachability}
\label{ch:presentation-of-reachability}

The indexed presentation is useful, but it also introduces bookkeeping
data that should not count as invariant mathematical content. The purpose
of this chapter is to separate the underlying reachability information
from the additional index-tracking apparatus.

The main constructions are therefore the forgetful map from indexed
reachability to ordinary reachability and the converse lifting
operation.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Forget the tick index.
forgetState : DriftState‚Ąē ‚Üí DriftState
forgetState = base

-- ¬ß Forget preserves strict reachability.
forgetReach‚Āļ : {s t : DriftState‚Ąē} ‚Üí (s ‚Čļ‚Ąē t) ‚Üí (forgetState s ‚Čļ forgetState t)
forgetReach‚Āļ one‚Ąē = one
forgetReach‚Āļ (more‚Ąē p) = more (forgetReach‚Āļ p)

-- ¬ß Compute the lifted target.
liftTarget : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí {s t : DriftState} ‚Üí (p : s ‚Čļ t) ‚Üí DriftState‚Ąē
liftTarget n {s} one = stepState‚Ąē ‚ü™ n , s ‚üę
liftTarget n (more p) = liftTarget (suc n) p

-- ¬ß Lift a base reach proof into the indexed ledger.
liftReach‚Āļ : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí {s t : DriftState} ‚Üí (p : s ‚Čļ t) ‚Üí (‚ü™ n , s ‚üę ‚Čļ‚Ąē liftTarget n p)
liftReach‚Āļ n {s} one = one‚Ąē
liftReach‚Āļ n (more p) = more‚Ąē (liftReach‚Āļ (suc n) p)

-- ¬ß Lifted target forgets to the original target.
liftTarget-base : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí {s t : DriftState} ‚Üí (p : s ‚Čļ t) ‚Üí forgetState (liftTarget n p) ‚Č°ŌČ t
liftTarget-base n {s} one = reflŌČ
liftTarget-base n (more p) = liftTarget-base (suc n) p

-- ¬ß Substitution lemma for more-constructor.
substŌČ-more :
  {s : DriftState} {t u : DriftState} ‚Üí
  (eq : t ‚Č°ŌČ u) ‚Üí
  (p : Reach‚Āļ (stepState s) t) ‚Üí
  substŌČ (őĽ x ‚Üí Reach‚Āļ s x) eq (more p) ‚Č°ŌČ more (substŌČ (őĽ x ‚Üí Reach‚Āļ (stepState s) x) eq p)
substŌČ-more reflŌČ p = reflŌČ
\end{code}

\subsection{Laws 9.0--9.2: Forget/Lift Round-Trip}
\label{laws:observables:forget-lift-roundtrip}

These first lemmas show that forgetting and lifting behave exactly as the
definitions require. Indexed reachability projects to ordinary
reachability, every ordinary reach witness admits an indexed lift, and
forgetting after lifting recovers the original proof up to the
appropriate equality.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Law 9.0: Forget preserves strict reachability.
law9-0-forget-preserves : {s t : DriftState‚Ąē} ‚Üí (s ‚Čļ‚Ąē t) ‚Üí (forgetState s ‚Čļ forgetState t)
law9-0-forget-preserves = forgetReach‚Āļ

-- ¬ß Law 9.1: Every strict reachability proof lifts to the indexed ledger.
law9-1-lift-exists : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí {s t : DriftState} ‚Üí (p : s ‚Čļ t) ‚Üí (‚ü™ n , s ‚üę ‚Čļ‚Ąē liftTarget n p)
law9-1-lift-exists = liftReach‚Āļ

-- ¬ß Law 9.2: Forget after lift recovers the original proof.
law9-2-forget-after-lift :
  (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí {s t : DriftState} ‚Üí
  (p : s ‚Čļ t) ‚Üí
  substŌČ (őĽ u ‚Üí Reach‚Āļ s u) (liftTarget-base n p) (forgetReach‚Āļ (liftReach‚Āļ n p)) ‚Č°ŌČ p
law9-2-forget-after-lift n one = reflŌČ
law9-2-forget-after-lift n {s} (more p) =
  let
    eq = liftTarget-base (suc n) p
    q  = forgetReach‚Āļ (liftReach‚Āļ (suc n) p)
  in
  transŌČ
    (substŌČ-more {s = s} eq q)
    (congŌČ more (law9-2-forget-after-lift (suc n) p))
\end{code}

\subsection{Laws 9.3--9.9: Admissible Observables}
\label{laws:observables:admissible}

An indexed observable is called admissible when it depends only on the
underlying base state and not on the particular index used to present
that state. The next definitions and lemmas formalize this factorization
requirement.

\phantomsection\label{law:observables:factor-through-base}
\phantomsection\label{law:observables:factor-unique}

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Pointwise equality on ŌČ-valued maps.
infix 4 _‚ČóŌČ_
_‚ČóŌČ_ : {A B : SetŌČ} ‚Üí (A ‚Üí B) ‚Üí (A ‚Üí B) ‚Üí SetŌČ
_‚ČóŌČ_ {A = A} f g = (x : A) ‚Üí f x ‚Č°ŌČ g x

-- ¬ß Canonical inclusion.
canonicalState : DriftState ‚Üí DriftState‚Ąē
canonicalState s = ‚ü™ zero , s ‚üę

-- ¬ß Respecting the base projection.
record RespectsBase {Y : SetŌČ} (f : DriftState‚Ąē ‚Üí Y) : SetŌČ where
  field
    respects : (x y : DriftState‚Ąē) ‚Üí forgetState x ‚Č°ŌČ forgetState y ‚Üí f x ‚Č°ŌČ f y

open RespectsBase public

-- ¬ß Factorization through forgetState.
record FactorsThroughBase {Y : SetŌČ} (f : DriftState‚Ąē ‚Üí Y) : SetŌČ where
  field
    g    : DriftState ‚Üí Y
    comm : (x : DriftState‚Ąē) ‚Üí f x ‚Č°ŌČ g (forgetState x)

open FactorsThroughBase public

-- ¬ß Law 9.3: Base-respecting observables factor through forget.
law9-3-factor-through-base :
  {Y : SetŌČ} ‚Üí
  (f : DriftState‚Ąē ‚Üí Y) ‚Üí
  RespectsBase f ‚Üí
  FactorsThroughBase f
law9-3-factor-through-base f rb = record
  { g = őĽ s ‚Üí f (canonicalState s)
  ; comm = őĽ x ‚Üí
      respects rb x (canonicalState (forgetState x)) reflŌČ
  }

-- ¬ß Law 9.4: Factorization through forget is unique.
law9-4-factor-unique :
  {Y : SetŌČ} ‚Üí
  {f : DriftState‚Ąē ‚Üí Y} ‚Üí
  (u v : FactorsThroughBase f) ‚Üí
  g u ‚ČóŌČ g v
law9-4-factor-unique {f = f} u v s =
  let
    x = canonicalState s
  in
  transŌČ (symŌČ (comm u x)) (comm v x)

-- ¬ß Admissible observable record.
record AdmissibleObservable (Y : SetŌČ) : SetŌČ where
  field
    obs  : DriftState‚Ąē ‚Üí Y
    base : RespectsBase obs

open AdmissibleObservable public

-- ¬ß Extract the base observable.
observe : {Y : SetŌČ} ‚Üí AdmissibleObservable Y ‚Üí DriftState ‚Üí Y
observe {Y} a = g (law9-3-factor-through-base (obs a) (base a))

-- ¬ß Commutation witness.
