  }
\end{code}

\subsection*{Law 23.4: Equivalence forces non-strict order}

Equivalent representatives satisfy the same non-strict order relation.
The eventual distance bounds allow the termwise comparison to be
transferred from one representative to the other.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Equivalence implies ‚Č§‚ĄĚ
‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ‚Üí‚Č§‚ĄĚ : {x y : ‚ĄĚ} ‚Üí x ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ y ‚Üí x ‚Č§‚ĄĚ y
‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ‚Üí‚Č§‚ĄĚ {x} {y} x‚ČÉy = record
  { leReal = őĽ őĶ őĶpos ‚Üí
      let
        pack : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí dist‚Ąö (seq x n) (seq y n) <‚Ąö őĶ)
        pack = _‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ_.conv0 x‚ČÉy őĶ őĶpos

        N : ‚Ąē
        N = fst pack

        conv : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí dist‚Ąö (seq x n) (seq y n) <‚Ąö őĶ
        conv = snd pack
      in
      N , (őĽ n N‚Č§n ‚Üí
        dist‚Ąö‚Č§őĶ‚Üíx‚Č§y+őĶ (seq x n) (seq y n) őĶ (<‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö (conv n N‚Č§n)))
  }
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Transitivity of Strict Order}
\label{sec:reals:strict-order-transitivity}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

If $x$ is eventually separated from $y$ by a positive margin, and $y$
from $z$ by another, then $x$ is eventually separated from $z$ as
well. The only technical point is to shrink the given margins so that
they can be combined within a single estimate. The quartering device
provides a convenient common reserve.

\subsection*{Law 23.5: Strict order is transitive}

The two witness margins compose after a controlled shrinking step.
Quartering keeps the combined estimate within the available budget and
leaves a positive margin for the final comparison.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Transitivity of strict real order
<‚ĄĚ-trans : {x y z : ‚ĄĚ} ‚Üí x <‚ĄĚ y ‚Üí y <‚ĄĚ z ‚Üí x <‚ĄĚ z
<‚ĄĚ-trans {x} {y} {z} x<y y<z = record
  { ltWitness =
      let
        -- ¬ß Phase 1: Unpack both strict-order witnesses
        wxy : ő£ ‚Ąö (őĽ őĶ ‚Üí (0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ) √ó ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq x n) +‚Ąö őĶ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq y n)))
        wxy = _<‚ĄĚ_.ltWitness x<y

        őĶ‚āĀ : ‚Ąö
        őĶ‚āĀ = fst wxy

        wxyRest : (0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ‚āĀ) √ó ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq x n) +‚Ąö őĶ‚āĀ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq y n))
        wxyRest = snd wxy

        őĶ‚āĀpos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ‚āĀ
        őĶ‚āĀpos = fst wxyRest

        packXY : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq x n) +‚Ąö őĶ‚āĀ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq y n))
        packXY = snd wxyRest

        Nxy : ‚Ąē
        Nxy = fst packXY

        convXY : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Nxy ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq x n) +‚Ąö őĶ‚āĀ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq y n)
        convXY = snd packXY

        wyz : ő£ ‚Ąö (őĽ őĶ ‚Üí (0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ) √ó ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq z n)))
        wyz = _<‚ĄĚ_.ltWitness y<z

        őĶ‚āā : ‚Ąö
        őĶ‚āā = fst wyz

        wyzRest : (0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ‚āā) √ó ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶ‚āā) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq z n))
        wyzRest = snd wyz

        őĶ‚āāpos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ‚āā
        őĶ‚āāpos = fst wyzRest

        packYZ : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶ‚āā) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq z n))
        packYZ = snd wyzRest

        Nyz : ‚Ąē
        Nyz = fst packYZ

        convYZ : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Nyz ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶ‚āā) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq z n)
        convYZ = snd packYZ

        -- ¬ß Phase 2: Shrink the surviving margin
        őĶ : ‚Ąö
        őĶ = őĶQuarter őĶ‚āĀ

        őĶpos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ
        őĶpos = őĶQuarter-pos őĶ‚āĀ

        N : ‚Ąē
        N = Nxy +‚Ąē Nyz

        Nxy‚Č§N : Nxy ‚Č§ N
        Nxy‚Č§N =
          let
            mono : (Nxy +‚Ąē zero) ‚Č§ (Nxy +‚Ąē Nyz)
            mono = ‚Č§-+‚Ąē-monoň° {a = zero} {b = Nyz} z‚Č§n Nxy
          in
          subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ (Nxy +‚Ąē Nyz)) (+‚Ąē-zero-right Nxy) mono

        Nyz‚Č§N : Nyz ‚Č§ N
        Nyz‚Č§N =
          let
            mono : (Nyz +‚Ąē zero) ‚Č§ (Nyz +‚Ąē Nxy)
            mono = ‚Č§-+‚Ąē-monoň° {a = zero} {b = Nxy} z‚Č§n Nyz

            base : Nyz ‚Č§ (Nyz +‚Ąē Nxy)
            base = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ (Nyz +‚Ąē Nxy)) (+‚Ąē-zero-right Nyz) mono
          in
          subst (őĽ t ‚Üí Nyz ‚Č§ t) (+‚Ąē-comm Nyz Nxy) base
      in
      őĶ , (őĶpos ,
        (N , (őĽ n N‚Č§n ‚Üí
          let
            -- ¬ß Phase 3: Chain the inequalities
            Nxy‚Č§n : Nxy ‚Č§ n
            Nxy‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans Nxy‚Č§N N‚Č§n

            Nyz‚Č§n : Nyz ‚Č§ n
            Nyz‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans Nyz‚Č§N N‚Č§n

            xn : ‚Ąö
            xn = seq x n

            yn : ‚Ąö
            yn = seq y n

            zn : ‚Ąö
            zn = seq z n

            xőĶ‚āĀ‚Č§y : (xn +‚Ąö őĶ‚āĀ) ‚Č§‚Ąö yn
            xőĶ‚āĀ‚Č§y = convXY n Nxy‚Č§n

            xőĶ‚Č§xőĶ‚āĀ : (xn +‚Ąö őĶ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (xn +‚Ąö őĶ‚āĀ)
            xőĶ‚Č§xőĶ‚āĀ =
              ‚Č§‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-mono-left xn őĶ őĶ‚āĀ (<‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö (őĶQuarter<őĶ őĶ‚āĀ őĶ‚āĀpos))

            xőĶ‚Č§y : (xn +‚Ąö őĶ) ‚Č§‚Ąö yn
            xőĶ‚Č§y = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {(xn +‚Ąö őĶ)} {(xn +‚Ąö őĶ‚āĀ)} {yn} xőĶ‚Č§xőĶ‚āĀ xőĶ‚āĀ‚Č§y

            y‚Č§y+őĶ‚āā : yn ‚Č§‚Ąö (yn +‚Ąö őĶ‚āā)
            y‚Č§y+őĶ‚āā = ‚Č§‚Ąö-add-nonneg-right yn őĶ‚āā (<‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö őĶ‚āāpos)

            xőĶ‚Č§y+őĶ‚āā : (xn +‚Ąö őĶ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (yn +‚Ąö őĶ‚āā)
            xőĶ‚Č§y+őĶ‚āā = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {(xn +‚Ąö őĶ)} {yn} {(yn +‚Ąö őĶ‚āā)} xőĶ‚Č§y y‚Č§y+őĶ‚āā
          in
            ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans xőĶ‚Č§y+őĶ‚āā (convYZ n Nyz‚Č§n))))
  }

\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Congruence of Strict Order under \texorpdfstring{$\simeq_{\mathbb{R}}$}{‚ČÉR}}
\label{sec:reals:strict-order-congruence}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The strict relation must also be independent of the chosen
representatives. Starting from a witness for $x<y$, one transports the
estimate along equivalences on both sides. The transport introduces
correction terms, so the original margin is reduced by repeated
quartering to leave a positive remainder. This shows that strict order
is well defined on the quotient.

\subsection*{Law 23.6: Strict order is invariant under equivalence}

Repeated quartering leaves enough of the original witness margin to
absorb both equivalence corrections. The strict gap therefore persists
under replacement by equivalent representatives.

\begin{code}

-- ¬ß Strict order respects ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ on both sides
<‚ĄĚ-resp-‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ : {x x' y y' : ‚ĄĚ} ‚Üí x ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ x' ‚Üí y ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ y' ‚Üí x <‚ĄĚ y ‚Üí x' <‚ĄĚ y'
<‚ĄĚ-resp-‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ {x} {x'} {y} {y'} x‚ČÉx' y‚ČÉy' x<y =
  let
    -- ¬ß Phase 1: Unpack the strict-order witness
    wxy = _<‚ĄĚ_.ltWitness x<y

    őĶ‚āÄ : ‚Ąö
    őĶ‚āÄ = fst wxy

    wxyRest = snd wxy

    őĶ‚āÄpos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ‚āÄ
    őĶ‚āÄpos = fst wxyRest

    packXY = snd wxyRest

    Nxy : ‚Ąē
    Nxy = fst packXY

    convXY : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Nxy ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ((seq x n) +‚Ąö őĶ‚āÄ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (seq y n)
    convXY = snd packXY

    -- ¬ß Phase 2: Quarter the margin twice
    őĶ : ‚Ąö
    őĶ = őĶQuarter őĶ‚āÄ

    őĶpos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ
    őĶpos = őĶQuarter-pos őĶ‚āÄ

    őĪ : ‚Ąö
    őĪ = őĶQuarter őĶ

    ő≤ : ‚Ąö
    ő≤ = őĶQuarter őĶ

    őĪpos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĪ
    őĪpos = őĶQuarter-pos őĶ

    ő≤pos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö ő≤
    ő≤pos = őĶQuarter-pos őĶ

    -- ¬ß Phase 3: Derive closeness from equivalences
    x'‚Č§x : x' ‚Č§‚ĄĚ x
    x'‚Č§x = ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ‚Üí‚Č§‚ĄĚ (‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ-sym x‚ČÉx')

    y‚Č§y' : y ‚Č§‚ĄĚ y'
    y‚Č§y' = ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ‚Üí‚Č§‚ĄĚ y‚ČÉy'

    packX = _‚Č§‚ĄĚ_.leReal x'‚Č§x őĪ őĪpos
    packY = _‚Č§‚ĄĚ_.leReal y‚Č§y' ő≤ ő≤pos

    Nx : ‚Ąē
    Nx = fst packX

    Ny : ‚Ąē
    Ny = fst packY

    boundX : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Nx ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq x' n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq x n) +‚Ąö őĪ)
    boundX = snd packX

    boundY : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Ny ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq y n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq y' n) +‚Ąö ő≤)
    boundY = snd packY

    -- ¬ß Phase 4: Global modulus N
    N : ‚Ąē
    N = Nxy +‚Ąē (Nx +‚Ąē Ny)

    Nxy‚Č§N : Nxy ‚Č§ N
    Nxy‚Č§N =
      let
        step : (Nxy +‚Ąē zero) ‚Č§ (Nxy +‚Ąē (Nx +‚Ąē Ny))
        step = ‚Č§-+‚Ąē-monoň° {a = zero} {b = (Nx +‚Ąē Ny)} z‚Č§n Nxy
      in
      subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ (Nxy +‚Ąē (Nx +‚Ąē Ny))) (+‚Ąē-zero-right Nxy) step

    Nx‚Č§N : Nx ‚Č§ N
    Nx‚Č§N =
      let
        step : (Nx +‚Ąē zero) ‚Č§ (Nx +‚Ąē (Nxy +‚Ąē Ny))
        step = ‚Č§-+‚Ąē-monoň° {a = zero} {b = (Nxy +‚Ąē Ny)} z‚Č§n Nx

        base : Nx ‚Č§ (Nx +‚Ąē (Nxy +‚Ąē Ny))
        base = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ (Nx +‚Ąē (Nxy +‚Ąē Ny))) (+‚Ąē-zero-right Nx) step

        eq : (Nx +‚Ąē (Nxy +‚Ąē Ny)) ‚Č° (Nxy +‚Ąē (Nx +‚Ąē Ny))
        eq =
          trans
            (sym (+‚Ąē-assoc Nx Nxy Ny))
            (trans
              (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t +‚Ąē Ny) (+‚Ąē-comm Nx Nxy))
              (+‚Ąē-assoc Nxy Nx Ny))
      in
      subst (őĽ t ‚Üí Nx ‚Č§ t) eq base

    Ny‚Č§N : Ny ‚Č§ N
    Ny‚Č§N =
      let
        step : (Ny +‚Ąē zero) ‚Č§ (Ny +‚Ąē (Nxy +‚Ąē Nx))
        step = ‚Č§-+‚Ąē-monoň° {a = zero} {b = (Nxy +‚Ąē Nx)} z‚Č§n Ny

        base : Ny ‚Č§ (Ny +‚Ąē (Nxy +‚Ąē Nx))
        base = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ (Ny +‚Ąē (Nxy +‚Ąē Nx))) (+‚Ąē-zero-right Ny) step

        eq : (Ny +‚Ąē (Nxy +‚Ąē Nx)) ‚Č° (Nxy +‚Ąē (Nx +‚Ąē Ny))
        eq =
          trans
            (sym (+‚Ąē-assoc Ny Nxy Nx))
            (trans
              (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t +‚Ąē Nx) (+‚Ąē-comm Ny Nxy))
              (trans
                (+‚Ąē-assoc Nxy Ny Nx)
                (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí Nxy +‚Ąē t) (+‚Ąē-comm Ny Nx))))
      in
      subst (őĽ t ‚Üí Ny ‚Č§ t) eq base
  in
  record
    { ltWitness =
        őĶ ,
        ( őĶpos
        , ( N
          , (őĽ n N‚Č§n ‚Üí
              let
                -- ¬ß Phase 5: The chain x'_n + őĶ ‚Č§ y'_n
                Nxy‚Č§n : Nxy ‚Č§ n
                Nxy‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans Nxy‚Č§N N‚Č§n

                Nx‚Č§n : Nx ‚Č§ n
                Nx‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans Nx‚Č§N N‚Č§n

                Ny‚Č§n : Ny ‚Č§ n
                Ny‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans Ny‚Č§N N‚Č§n

                xn : ‚Ąö
                xn = seq x n

                x'n : ‚Ąö
                x'n = seq x' n

                yn : ‚Ąö
                yn = seq y n

                y'n : ‚Ąö
                y'n = seq y' n

                x'n‚Č§xn+őĪ : x'n ‚Č§‚Ąö (xn +‚Ąö őĪ)
                x'n‚Č§xn+őĪ = boundX n Nx‚Č§n

                őĪ+ő≤<őĶ : (őĪ +‚Ąö ő≤) <‚Ąö őĶ
                őĪ+ő≤<őĶ = őĶQuarter-double<őĶ őĶ őĶpos

                őĪ+ő≤‚Č§őĶ : (őĪ +‚Ąö ő≤) ‚Č§‚Ąö őĶ
                őĪ+ő≤‚Č§őĶ = <‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {(őĪ +‚Ąö ő≤)} {őĶ} őĪ+ő≤<őĶ

                őĶ+őĪ+ő≤‚Č§őĶ+őĶ : (őĶ +‚Ąö (őĪ +‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚Č§‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö őĶ)
                őĶ+őĪ+ő≤‚Č§őĶ+őĶ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-mono-left őĶ (őĪ +‚Ąö ő≤) őĶ őĪ+ő≤‚Č§őĶ

                őĶ+őĶ<őĶ‚āÄ : (őĶ +‚Ąö őĶ) <‚Ąö őĶ‚āÄ
                őĶ+őĶ<őĶ‚āÄ = őĶQuarter-double<őĶ őĶ‚āÄ őĶ‚āÄpos

                őĶ+őĶ‚Č§őĶ‚āÄ : (őĶ +‚Ąö őĶ) ‚Č§‚Ąö őĶ‚āÄ
                őĶ+őĶ‚Č§őĶ‚āÄ = <‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {(őĶ +‚Ąö őĶ)} {őĶ‚āÄ} őĶ+őĶ<őĶ‚āÄ

                őĶ+őĪ+ő≤‚Č§őĶ‚āÄ : (őĶ +‚Ąö (őĪ +‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚Č§‚Ąö őĶ‚āÄ
                őĶ+őĪ+ő≤‚Č§őĶ‚āÄ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {(őĶ +‚Ąö (őĪ +‚Ąö ő≤))} {(őĶ +‚Ąö őĶ)} {őĶ‚āÄ} őĶ+őĪ+ő≤‚Č§őĶ+őĶ őĶ+őĶ‚Č§őĶ‚āÄ

                t : ‚Ąö
                t = őĪ +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤)

                t‚ČÉőĶ+őĪ+ő≤ : t ‚ČÉ‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö (őĪ +‚Ąö ő≤))
                t‚ČÉőĶ+őĪ+ő≤ =
                  ‚ČÉ‚Ąö-trans
                    (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-sym (+‚Ąö-assoc őĪ őĶ ő≤))
                    (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-trans
                      (+‚Ąö-resp-‚ČÉ (+‚Ąö-comm őĪ őĶ) (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-refl ő≤))
                      (+‚Ąö-assoc őĶ őĪ ő≤))

                t‚Č§őĶ‚āÄ : t ‚Č§‚Ąö őĶ‚āÄ
                t‚Č§őĶ‚āÄ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans (‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąöň° t‚ČÉőĶ+őĪ+ő≤) őĶ+őĪ+ő≤‚Č§őĶ‚āÄ

                xnt‚Č§xnőĶ‚āÄ : (xn +‚Ąö t) ‚Č§‚Ąö (xn +‚Ąö őĶ‚āÄ)
                xnt‚Č§xnőĶ‚āÄ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-mono-left xn t őĶ‚āÄ t‚Č§őĶ‚āÄ

                x'n+őĶ+ő≤‚Č§xn+t : (x'n +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚Č§‚Ąö (xn +‚Ąö t)
                x'n+őĶ+ő≤‚Č§xn+t =
                  let
                    step‚āĀ : (x'n +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((xn +‚Ąö őĪ) +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤))
                    step‚āĀ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-mono-right x'n (xn +‚Ąö őĪ) (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤) x'n‚Č§xn+őĪ

                    lhsEq : ((xn +‚Ąö őĪ) +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö (xn +‚Ąö t)
                    lhsEq = +‚Ąö-assoc xn őĪ (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤)
                  in
                  ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans step‚āĀ (‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąöň° lhsEq)

                x'n+őĶ+ő≤‚Č§xn+őĶ‚āÄ : (x'n +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚Č§‚Ąö (xn +‚Ąö őĶ‚āÄ)
                x'n+őĶ+ő≤‚Č§xn+őĶ‚āÄ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {(x'n +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤))} {(xn +‚Ąö t)} {(xn +‚Ąö őĶ‚āÄ)} x'n+őĶ+ő≤‚Č§xn+t xnt‚Č§xnőĶ‚āÄ

                xn+őĶ‚āÄ‚Č§yn : (xn +‚Ąö őĶ‚āÄ) ‚Č§‚Ąö yn
                xn+őĶ‚āÄ‚Č§yn = convXY n Nxy‚Č§n

                x'n+őĶ+ő≤‚Č§yn : (x'n +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚Č§‚Ąö yn
                x'n+őĶ+ő≤‚Č§yn = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {(x'n +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤))} {(xn +‚Ąö őĶ‚āÄ)} {yn} x'n+őĶ+ő≤‚Č§xn+őĶ‚āÄ xn+őĶ‚āÄ‚Č§yn

                x'n+őĶ‚Č§yn-ő≤ : (x'n +‚Ąö őĶ) ‚Č§‚Ąö (yn +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤))
                x'n+őĶ‚Č§yn-ő≤ =
                  let
                    step‚āĀ : ((x'n +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤)) +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚Č§‚Ąö (yn +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤))
                    step‚āĀ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-mono-right (x'n +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤)) yn (-‚Ąö ő≤) x'n+őĶ+ő≤‚Č§yn

                    lhsEq‚āĀ : ((x'n +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤)) +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö (x'n +‚Ąö ((őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤) +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤)))
                    lhsEq‚āĀ = +‚Ąö-assoc x'n (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤) (-‚Ąö ő≤)

                    lhsEq‚āā : ((őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤) +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö őĶ
                    lhsEq‚āā =
                      ‚ČÉ‚Ąö-trans
                        (+‚Ąö-assoc őĶ ő≤ (-‚Ąö ő≤))
                        (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-trans
                          (+‚Ąö-resp-‚ČÉ (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-refl őĶ) (+‚Ąö-inv-right ő≤))
                          (+‚Ąö-zero-right őĶ))

                    lhsEq : ((x'n +‚Ąö (őĶ +‚Ąö ő≤)) +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö (x'n +‚Ąö őĶ)
                    lhsEq = ‚ČÉ‚Ąö-trans lhsEq‚āĀ (+‚Ąö-resp-‚ČÉ (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-refl x'n) lhsEq‚āā)
                  in
                  ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans (‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö ≥ lhsEq) step‚āĀ

                yn‚Č§y'n+ő≤ : yn ‚Č§‚Ąö (y'n +‚Ąö ő≤)
                yn‚Č§y'n+ő≤ = boundY n Ny‚Č§n

                yn-ő≤‚Č§y'n : (yn +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚Č§‚Ąö y'n
                yn-ő≤‚Č§y'n =
                  let
                    step‚āĀ : (yn +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((y'n +‚Ąö ő≤) +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤))
                    step‚āĀ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-mono-right yn (y'n +‚Ąö ő≤) (-‚Ąö ő≤) yn‚Č§y'n+ő≤

                    rhsEq‚āĀ : ((y'n +‚Ąö ő≤) +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö (y'n +‚Ąö (ő≤ +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤)))
                    rhsEq‚āĀ = +‚Ąö-assoc y'n ő≤ (-‚Ąö ő≤)

                    rhsEq‚āā : (ő≤ +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö 0‚Ąö
                    rhsEq‚āā = +‚Ąö-inv-right ő≤

                    rhsEq : ((y'n +‚Ąö ő≤) +‚Ąö (-‚Ąö ő≤)) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö y'n
                    rhsEq =
                      ‚ČÉ‚Ąö-trans
                        rhsEq‚āĀ
                        (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-trans
                          (+‚Ąö-resp-‚ČÉ (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-refl y'n) rhsEq‚āā)
                          (+‚Ąö-zero-right y'n))
                  in
                  ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans step‚āĀ (‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąöň° rhsEq)
              in
              ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans x'n+őĶ‚Č§yn-ő≤ yn-ő≤‚Č§y'n
            )
          )
        )
    }
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Reflexivity of \texorpdfstring{$\le_{\mathbb{R}}$}{<=R}}
\label{sec:reals:leq-reflexivity}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

Reflexivity is immediate. For any positive margin $\varepsilon$, each
term of a representative sequence is at most itself plus
$\varepsilon$, so the asymptotic definition gives self-comparison on
every tail.

\subsection*{Law 23.7: Non-strict order is reflexive}

Reflexivity follows pointwise from the inequality
$a \leq a + \varepsilon$.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Reflexivity of ‚Č§‚ĄĚ
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-refl : (x : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí x ‚Č§‚ĄĚ x
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-refl x = record
  { leReal = őĽ őĶ őĶpos ‚Üí
      zero , (őĽ n _ ‚Üí
        ‚Č§‚Ąö-add-nonneg-right (seq x n) őĶ (<‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö őĶpos))
  }
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Transitivity of \texorpdfstring{$\le_{\mathbb{R}}$}{<=R}}
\label{sec:reals:leq-transitivity}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

Transitivity is proved by evaluating both given comparisons with a
reduced tolerance and then chaining the resulting inequalities. The
only bookkeeping is to ensure that the two error terms stay below the
requested $\varepsilon$.

\subsection*{Law 23.8: Non-strict order is transitive}

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Transitivity of ‚Č§‚ĄĚ via őĶ-splitting
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-trans : {x y z : ‚ĄĚ} ‚Üí x ‚Č§‚ĄĚ y ‚Üí y ‚Č§‚ĄĚ z ‚Üí x ‚Č§‚ĄĚ z
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-trans {x} {y} {z} x‚Č§y y‚Č§z = record
  { leReal = őĽ őĶ őĶpos ‚Üí
      let
        -- ¬ß Phase 1: Budget allocation
        őĶq : ‚Ąö
        őĶq = őĶQuarter őĶ

        őĶqPos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶq
        őĶqPos = őĶQuarter-pos őĶ

        őĶqNonneg : 0‚Ąö ‚Č§‚Ąö őĶq
        őĶqNonneg = <‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {0‚Ąö} {őĶq} őĶqPos

        őĶq+őĶq<őĶ : (őĶq +‚Ąö őĶq) <‚Ąö őĶ
        őĶq+őĶq<őĶ = őĶQuarter-double<őĶ őĶ őĶpos

        NxyPack : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq x n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶq))
        NxyPack = _‚Č§‚ĄĚ_.leReal x‚Č§y őĶq őĶqPos

        NyzPack : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq y n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq z n) +‚Ąö őĶq))
        NyzPack = _‚Č§‚ĄĚ_.leReal y‚Č§z őĶq őĶqPos

        Nxy : ‚Ąē
        Nxy = fst NxyPack

        Nyz : ‚Ąē
        Nyz = fst NyzPack

        NxyConv : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Nxy ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq x n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶq)
        NxyConv = snd NxyPack

        NyzConv : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Nyz ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq y n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq z n) +‚Ąö őĶq)
        NyzConv = snd NyzPack

        -- ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Phase 2: Global modulus N ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
        N : ‚Ąē
        N = Nxy +‚Ąē Nyz

        Nxy‚Č§N : Nxy ‚Č§ N
        Nxy‚Č§N =
          let
            mono : (Nxy +‚Ąē zero) ‚Č§ (Nxy +‚Ąē Nyz)
            mono = ‚Č§-+‚Ąē-monoň° {a = zero} {b = Nyz} z‚Č§n Nxy
          in
          subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ (Nxy +‚Ąē Nyz)) (+‚Ąē-zero-right Nxy) mono

        Nyz‚Č§N : Nyz ‚Č§ N
        Nyz‚Č§N =
          let
            mono : (Nyz +‚Ąē zero) ‚Č§ (Nyz +‚Ąē Nxy)
            mono = ‚Č§-+‚Ąē-monoň° {a = zero} {b = Nxy} z‚Č§n Nyz

            base : Nyz ‚Č§ (Nyz +‚Ąē Nxy)
            base = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ (Nyz +‚Ąē Nxy)) (+‚Ąē-zero-right Nyz) mono
          in
          subst (őĽ t ‚Üí Nyz ‚Č§ t) (+‚Ąē-comm Nyz Nxy) base
      in
      N , (őĽ n N‚Č§n ‚Üí
        let
          -- ¬ß Phase 3: Chain and reassociate
          Nxy‚Č§n : Nxy ‚Č§ n
          Nxy‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans Nxy‚Č§N N‚Č§n

          Nyz‚Č§n : Nyz ‚Č§ n
          Nyz‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans Nyz‚Č§N N‚Č§n

          xn : ‚Ąö
          xn = seq x n

          yn : ‚Ąö
          yn = seq y n

          zn : ‚Ąö
          zn = seq z n

          xn‚Č§yn+őĶq : xn ‚Č§‚Ąö (yn +‚Ąö őĶq)
          xn‚Č§yn+őĶq = NxyConv n Nxy‚Č§n

          yn‚Č§zn+őĶq : yn ‚Č§‚Ąö (zn +‚Ąö őĶq)
          yn‚Č§zn+őĶq = NyzConv n Nyz‚Č§n

          step‚āĀ : (yn +‚Ąö őĶq) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((zn +‚Ąö őĶq) +‚Ąö őĶq)
          step‚āĀ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-mono-right yn (zn +‚Ąö őĶq) őĶq yn‚Č§zn+őĶq

          step‚āā : xn ‚Č§‚Ąö ((zn +‚Ąö őĶq) +‚Ąö őĶq)
          step‚āā = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {xn} {(yn +‚Ąö őĶq)} {((zn +‚Ąö őĶq) +‚Ąö őĶq)} xn‚Č§yn+őĶq step‚āĀ

          step‚āÉ : ((zn +‚Ąö őĶq) +‚Ąö őĶq) ‚Č§‚Ąö (zn +‚Ąö (őĶq +‚Ąö őĶq))
          step‚āÉ = ‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąöň° {((zn +‚Ąö őĶq) +‚Ąö őĶq)} {(zn +‚Ąö (őĶq +‚Ąö őĶq))} (+‚Ąö-assoc zn őĶq őĶq)

          step‚āĄ : (zn +‚Ąö (őĶq +‚Ąö őĶq)) ‚Č§‚Ąö (zn +‚Ąö őĶ)
          step‚āĄ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-mono-left zn (őĶq +‚Ąö őĶq) őĶ (<‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö őĶq+őĶq<őĶ)

          done : xn ‚Č§‚Ąö (zn +‚Ąö őĶ)
          done = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {xn} {((zn +‚Ąö őĶq) +‚Ąö őĶq)} {(zn +‚Ąö őĶ)} step‚āā (‚Č§‚Ąö-trans step‚āÉ step‚āĄ)
        in
        done)
  }
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Antisymmetry of \texorpdfstring{$\le_{\mathbb{R}}$}{<=R}}
\label{sec:reals:leq-antisymmetry}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

If each of two real numbers is eventually bounded above by the other,
then their representatives differ by at most an arbitrary positive
tolerance on a tail. This is exactly the estimate required for
equivalence.

\subsection*{Law 23.9: Mutual non-strict order forces equivalence}

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Antisymmetry: mutual ‚Č§‚ĄĚ yields ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-antisym : {x y : ‚ĄĚ} ‚Üí x ‚Č§‚ĄĚ y ‚Üí y ‚Č§‚ĄĚ x ‚Üí x ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ y
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-antisym {x} {y} x‚Č§y y‚Č§x = record
  { conv0 = őĽ őĶ őĶpos ‚Üí
      let
        őĶq : ‚Ąö
        őĶq = őĶQuarter őĶ

        őĶqPos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶq
        őĶqPos = őĶQuarter-pos őĶ

        NxyPack : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq x n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶq))
        NxyPack = _‚Č§‚ĄĚ_.leReal x‚Č§y őĶq őĶqPos

        NyxPack : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq y n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq x n) +‚Ąö őĶq))
        NyxPack = _‚Č§‚ĄĚ_.leReal y‚Č§x őĶq őĶqPos

        Nxy : ‚Ąē
        Nxy = fst NxyPack

        Nyx : ‚Ąē
        Nyx = fst NyxPack

        NxyConv : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Nxy ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq x n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶq)
        NxyConv = snd NxyPack

        NyxConv : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Nyx ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq y n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq x n) +‚Ąö őĶq)
        NyxConv = snd NyxPack

        N : ‚Ąē
        N = Nxy +‚Ąē Nyx

        Nxy‚Č§N : Nxy ‚Č§ N
        Nxy‚Č§N =
          let
            mono : (Nxy +‚Ąē zero) ‚Č§ (Nxy +‚Ąē Nyx)
            mono = ‚Č§-+‚Ąē-monoň° {a = zero} {b = Nyx} z‚Č§n Nxy
          in
          subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ (Nxy +‚Ąē Nyx)) (+‚Ąē-zero-right Nxy) mono

        Nyx‚Č§N : Nyx ‚Č§ N
        Nyx‚Č§N =
          let
            mono : (Nyx +‚Ąē zero) ‚Č§ (Nyx +‚Ąē Nxy)
            mono = ‚Č§-+‚Ąē-monoň° {a = zero} {b = Nxy} z‚Č§n Nyx

            base : Nyx ‚Č§ (Nyx +‚Ąē Nxy)
            base = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ (Nyx +‚Ąē Nxy)) (+‚Ąē-zero-right Nyx) mono
          in
          subst (őĽ t ‚Üí Nyx ‚Č§ t) (+‚Ąē-comm Nyx Nxy) base
      in
      N , (őĽ n N‚Č§n ‚Üí
        let
          Nxy‚Č§n : Nxy ‚Č§ n
          Nxy‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans Nxy‚Č§N N‚Č§n

          Nyx‚Č§n : Nyx ‚Č§ n
          Nyx‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans Nyx‚Č§N N‚Č§n

          xn : ‚Ąö
          xn = seq x n

          yn : ‚Ąö
          yn = seq y n

          xn‚Č§yn+őĶq : xn ‚Č§‚Ąö (yn +‚Ąö őĶq)
          xn‚Č§yn+őĶq = NxyConv n Nxy‚Č§n

          yn‚Č§xn+őĶq : yn ‚Č§‚Ąö (xn +‚Ąö őĶq)
          yn‚Č§xn+őĶq = NyxConv n Nyx‚Č§n

          d‚Č§őĶq : dist‚Ąö xn yn ‚Č§‚Ąö őĶq
          d‚Č§őĶq = dist‚Ąö-bounded-by-őĶ xn yn őĶq xn‚Č§yn+őĶq yn‚Č§xn+őĶq

          őĶq<őĶ : őĶq <‚Ąö őĶ
          őĶq<őĶ = őĶQuarter<őĶ őĶ őĶpos
        in
        ‚Č§<‚Ąö‚Üí<‚Ąö {dist‚Ąö xn yn} {őĶq} {őĶ} d‚Č§őĶq őĶq<őĶ)
  }
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Congruence of \texorpdfstring{$\le_{\mathbb{R}}$}{<=R} under \texorpdfstring{$\simeq_{\mathbb{R}}$}{‚ČÉR}}
\label{sec:reals:leq-congruence}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The non-strict relation is easier to transport than the strict one.
Equivalence already provides comparisons in both directions, so one
composes those with the given order relation. Hence
$\leq_{\mathbb{R}}$ depends only on the represented real numbers.

\subsection*{Law 23.10: Non-strict order is invariant under equivalence}

The congruence law is obtained by composing the order bounds induced
by equivalence on the left and right.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Non-strict order respects ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-resp-‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ : {x x' y y' : ‚ĄĚ} ‚Üí x ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ x' ‚Üí y ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ y' ‚Üí x ‚Č§‚ĄĚ y ‚Üí x' ‚Č§‚ĄĚ y'
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-resp-‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ {x} {x'} {y} {y'} x‚ČÉx' y‚ČÉy' x‚Č§y =
  let
    x'‚Č§x : x' ‚Č§‚ĄĚ x
    x'‚Č§x = ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ‚Üí‚Č§‚ĄĚ (‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ-sym x‚ČÉx')

    y‚Č§y' : y ‚Č§‚ĄĚ y'
    y‚Č§y' = ‚ČÉ‚ĄĚ‚Üí‚Č§‚ĄĚ y‚ČÉy'
  in
  ‚Č§‚ĄĚ-trans (‚Č§‚ĄĚ-trans x'‚Č§x x‚Č§y) y‚Č§y'
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Right Additive Monotonicity of \texorpdfstring{$\le_{\mathbb{R}}$}{<=R}}
\label{sec:reals:right-additive-monotonicity}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

Order must be compatible with addition. If $x \leq y$, then adding the
same real number to both sides should preserve the comparison. On
representatives this is pointwise monotonicity of rational addition,
together with a simple reassociation of the error term. The
left-sided version is identical.

\subsection*{Law 23.11: Right addition preserves non-strict order}

Right monotonicity follows from the corresponding rational
monotonicity, with associativity used to place the
$\varepsilon$-term in the required form.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Right monotonicity of +‚ĄĚ under ‚Č§‚ĄĚ
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-+‚ĄĚ-mono-right : {x y z : ‚ĄĚ} ‚Üí x ‚Č§‚ĄĚ y ‚Üí (x +‚ĄĚ z) ‚Č§‚ĄĚ (y +‚ĄĚ z)
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-+‚ĄĚ-mono-right {x} {y} {z} x‚Č§y = record
  { leReal = őĽ őĶ őĶpos ‚Üí
      let
        pack : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq x n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶ))
        pack = _‚Č§‚ĄĚ_.leReal x‚Č§y őĶ őĶpos

        N : ‚Ąē
        N = fst pack

        conv : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq x n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶ)
        conv = snd pack
      in
      N , (őĽ n N‚Č§n ‚Üí
        let
          xn : ‚Ąö
          xn = seq x n

          yn : ‚Ąö
          yn = seq y n

          zn : ‚Ąö
          zn = seq z n

          step‚āĀ : (xn +‚Ąö zn) ‚Č§‚Ąö (((yn +‚Ąö őĶ) +‚Ąö zn))
          step‚āĀ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-mono-right xn (yn +‚Ąö őĶ) zn (conv n N‚Č§n)

          rhsEq : ((yn +‚Ąö őĶ) +‚Ąö zn) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö ((yn +‚Ąö zn) +‚Ąö őĶ)
          rhsEq =
            ‚ČÉ‚Ąö-trans
              (+‚Ąö-assoc yn őĶ zn)
              (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-trans
                (+‚Ąö-resp-‚ČÉ (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-refl yn) (+‚Ąö-comm őĶ zn))
                (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-sym (+‚Ąö-assoc yn zn őĶ)))

          step‚āā : (((yn +‚Ąö őĶ) +‚Ąö zn)) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((yn +‚Ąö zn) +‚Ąö őĶ)
          step‚āā = ‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąöň° {(((yn +‚Ąö őĶ) +‚Ąö zn))} {((yn +‚Ąö zn) +‚Ąö őĶ)} rhsEq
        in
        ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans step‚āĀ step‚āā)
  }
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Left Additive Monotonicity of \texorpdfstring{$\le_{\mathbb{R}}$}{<=R}}
\label{sec:reals:left-additive-monotonicity}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The left-sided version is identical. One again applies termwise
monotonicity of rational addition and carries the same tail estimate
through unchanged. Together, the two monotonicity laws show that
translation preserves asymptotic order on the reals.

\subsection*{Law 23.12: Left addition preserves non-strict order}

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Left monotonicity of +‚ĄĚ under ‚Č§‚ĄĚ
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-+‚ĄĚ-mono-left : {x y z : ‚ĄĚ} ‚Üí x ‚Č§‚ĄĚ y ‚Üí (z +‚ĄĚ x) ‚Č§‚ĄĚ (z +‚ĄĚ y)
‚Č§‚ĄĚ-+‚ĄĚ-mono-left {x} {y} {z} x‚Č§y = record
  { leReal = őĽ őĶ őĶpos ‚Üí
      let
        pack : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq x n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶ))
        pack = _‚Č§‚ĄĚ_.leReal x‚Č§y őĶ őĶpos

        N : ‚Ąē
        N = fst pack

        conv : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (seq x n) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((seq y n) +‚Ąö őĶ)
        conv = snd pack
      in
      N , (őĽ n N‚Č§n ‚Üí
        let
          xn : ‚Ąö
          xn = seq x n

          yn : ‚Ąö
          yn = seq y n

          zn : ‚Ąö
          zn = seq z n

          step‚āĀ : (zn +‚Ąö xn) ‚Č§‚Ąö (zn +‚Ąö (yn +‚Ąö őĶ))
          step‚āĀ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-mono-left zn xn (yn +‚Ąö őĶ) (conv n N‚Č§n)

          rhsEq : (zn +‚Ąö (yn +‚Ąö őĶ)) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö ((zn +‚Ąö yn) +‚Ąö őĶ)
          rhsEq = sym (+‚Ąö-assoc zn yn őĶ)

          step‚āā : (zn +‚Ąö (yn +‚Ąö őĶ)) ‚Č§‚Ąö ((zn +‚Ąö yn) +‚Ąö őĶ)
          step‚āā = ‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąöň° {(zn +‚Ąö (yn +‚Ąö őĶ))} {((zn +‚Ąö yn) +‚Ąö őĶ)} rhsEq
        in
        ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans step‚āĀ step‚āā)
  }
\end{code}

The required numerical machinery is now in place. Natural numbers,
integers, rationals, reals, order, distance, scaling, and the relevant
spectral operators have all been constructed within the same formal
framework. The next part uses this apparatus to evaluate the
invariants of $K_4$ explicitly.

What remains is therefore an organizational step. The invariants
already derived are assembled, their numerical values are computed,
and the formal closure of the chain is verified.


%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź
\chapter{Cauchy Completeness of \texorpdfstring{$\mathbb{R}$}{R}}
\label{ch:reals-cauchy-completeness}
%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź

The construction of $\mathbb{R}$ given so far supplies the completion
of $\mathbb{Q}$ as a setoid of Cauchy sequences. The next theorem must
also be made formal: every Cauchy sequence of real numbers itself
converges to a real number. The standard diagonal argument is carried
out below, using only the rational $\varepsilon$-machinery and the
embedding $\mathbb{Q} \hookrightarrow \mathbb{R}$ already constructed.

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Auxiliary natural number maximum}
\label{sec:reals-completeness:max-nat}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The diagonal argument repeatedly takes the maximum of finitely many
natural numbers. A small explicit definition with the two
inequality witnesses is sufficient.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Maximum on ‚Ąē.
max‚Ąē : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē
max‚Ąē zero    n       = n
max‚Ąē (suc m) zero    = suc m
max‚Ąē (suc m) (suc n) = suc (max‚Ąē m n)

-- ¬ß Left bound.
‚Č§-max‚Ąēň° : (m n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí m ‚Č§ max‚Ąē m n
‚Č§-max‚Ąēň° zero    n       = z‚Č§n
‚Č§-max‚Ąēň° (suc m) zero    = ‚Č§-refl (suc m)
‚Č§-max‚Ąēň° (suc m) (suc n) = s‚Č§s (‚Č§-max‚Ąēň° m n)

-- ¬ß Right bound.
‚Č§-max‚Ąē ≥ : (m n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí n ‚Č§ max‚Ąē m n
‚Č§-max‚Ąē ≥ zero    n       = ‚Č§-refl n
‚Č§-max‚Ąē ≥ (suc m) zero    = z‚Č§n
‚Č§-max‚Ąē ≥ (suc m) (suc n) = s‚Č§s (‚Č§-max‚Ąē ≥ m n)
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{The rational schedule \texorpdfstring{$1/(n+1)$}{1/(n+1)}}
\label{sec:reals-completeness:half-to}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The diagonal construction needs a vanishing sequence of positive
rational tolerances indexed by~$\mathbb{N}$. The rationals
$1/(n+1)$ are the natural choice. They are defined by directly using
the positive denominator \texttt{mk‚Ąē‚Āļ n} and inherit positivity from the
positive numerator \texttt{one‚Ą§}.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Schedule of rational tolerances 1/(n+1).
halfTo : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąö
halfTo n = one‚Ą§ / mk‚Ąē‚Āļ n

-- ¬ß Positivity of the schedule.
halfTo-pos : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö halfTo n
halfTo-pos n = 0<‚Ą§num‚Üí0<‚Ąö (halfTo n) (0<‚Ą§-pos zero)
\end{code}

The order on the schedule is decreasing in~$n$: a larger index
yields a smaller tolerance. The proof reduces to the integer
inequality $\mathord{+}\,\mathrm{suc}\,m \le \mathord{+}\,\mathrm{suc}\,n$ obtained from~$m \le n$.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Monotone decrease: m ‚Č§ n ‚Üí halfTo n ‚Č§ halfTo m.
halfTo-mono : (m n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí m ‚Č§ n ‚Üí halfTo n ‚Č§‚Ąö halfTo m
halfTo-mono m n m‚Č§n =
  let
    -- Goal: one‚Ą§ *‚Ą§ ‚Āļto‚Ą§ (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ m) ‚Č§‚Ą§ one‚Ą§ *‚Ą§ ‚Āļto‚Ą§ (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ n).
    base : (suc m) ‚Č§ (suc n)
    base = s‚Č§s m‚Č§n

    int-le : (+suc m) ‚Č§‚Ą§ (+suc n)
    int-le = base

    lhsEq : (one‚Ą§ *‚Ą§ ‚Āļto‚Ą§ (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ m)) ‚Č° (+suc m)
    lhsEq = *‚Ą§-one-left (+suc m)

    rhsEq : (one‚Ą§ *‚Ą§ ‚Āļto‚Ą§ (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ n)) ‚Č° (+suc n)
    rhsEq = *‚Ą§-one-left (+suc n)

    step : (+suc m) ‚Č§‚Ą§ (+suc n)
    step = int-le
  in
  ‚Č§‚Ą§-resp-‚Č°ň° (sym lhsEq) (‚Č§‚Ą§-resp-‚Č° ≥ (sym rhsEq) step)
\end{code}

The Archimedean property is the corresponding existence statement:
for every positive rational~$\varepsilon$, some index~$N$ makes
$1/(N+1)$ strictly smaller than~$\varepsilon$. The witness is read
directly off the denominator of~$\varepsilon$: choosing
$N = \mathtt{‚Āļto‚Ąē}\,b$ where $b$ is the denominator of~$\varepsilon$
suffices, because the resulting product~$a \cdot (b+1)$ strictly
exceeds~$b$ whenever $a$ is positive.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Mixed strict/weak transitivity on ‚Ą§.
<‚Č§‚Ą§‚Üí<‚Ą§-loc : {x y z : ‚Ą§} ‚Üí x <‚Ą§ y ‚Üí y ‚Č§‚Ą§ z ‚Üí x <‚Ą§ z
<‚Č§‚Ą§‚Üí<‚Ą§-loc {x} {y} {z} (x‚Č§y , y‚Čįx) y‚Č§z =
  ‚Č§‚Ą§-trans {x} {y} {z} x‚Č§y y‚Č§z ,
  (őĽ z‚Č§x ‚Üí y‚Čįx (‚Č§‚Ą§-trans {y} {z} {x} y‚Č§z z‚Č§x))

-- ¬ß Archimedean property of the schedule.
halfTo-arch : (őĶ : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ ‚Üí ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí halfTo N <‚Ąö őĶ)
halfTo-arch (a / mk‚Ąē‚Āļ k) őĶpos =
  let
    nWit : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ m ‚Üí a ‚Č° +suc m)
    nWit = positive-num-witness‚Ąö (a / mk‚Ąē‚Āļ k) őĶpos

    m : ‚Ąē
    m = fst nWit

    aEq : a ‚Č° +suc m
    aEq = snd nWit

    -- The chosen schedule index.
    N : ‚Ąē
    N = suc k

    -- ‚Āļto‚Ą§ (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ N) = +suc (suc k).
    -- The strict inequality on integers we ultimately need:
    --   one‚Ą§ *‚Ą§ +suc k <‚Ą§ a *‚Ą§ +suc (suc k).

    one‚Č§a : one‚Ą§ ‚Č§‚Ą§ a
    one‚Č§a = ‚Č§‚Ą§-resp-‚Č° ≥ (sym aEq) (s‚Č§s z‚Č§n)

    -- Step 1: one‚Ą§ *‚Ą§ +suc (suc k) ‚Č§‚Ą§ a *‚Ą§ +suc (suc k).
    step‚āĀ : (one‚Ą§ *‚Ą§ ‚Āļto‚Ą§ (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (suc k))) ‚Č§‚Ą§ (a *‚Ą§ ‚Āļto‚Ą§ (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (suc k)))
    step‚āĀ = ‚Č§‚Ą§-mul-pos-right one‚Ą§ a (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (suc k)) one‚Č§a

    -- Reduce the LHS of step‚āĀ to +suc (suc k).
    lhs‚āĀEq : (one‚Ą§ *‚Ą§ ‚Āļto‚Ą§ (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (suc k))) ‚Č° (+suc (suc k))
    lhs‚āĀEq = *‚Ą§-one-left (+suc (suc k))

    -- Strict step: +suc k <‚Ą§ +suc (suc k).
    sucStep : (+suc k) <‚Ą§ (+suc (suc k))
    sucStep = (s‚Č§s (‚Č§-step k)) , őĽ p ‚Üí suc‚Č§-impossible (suc k) p

    -- Reduce LHS of the goal to +suc k.
    goalLhsEq : (one‚Ą§ *‚Ą§ ‚Āļto‚Ą§ (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ k)) ‚Č° (+suc k)
    goalLhsEq = *‚Ą§-one-left (+suc k)

    -- Combine: +suc k <‚Ą§ +suc (suc k) ‚Č§‚Ą§ a *‚Ą§ +suc (suc k).
    chained : (+suc k) <‚Ą§ (a *‚Ą§ ‚Āļto‚Ą§ (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (suc k)))
    chained = <‚Č§‚Ą§‚Üí<‚Ą§-loc sucStep (‚Č§‚Ą§-resp-‚Č°ň° lhs‚āĀEq step‚āĀ)

    -- Rewrite via aEq on the RHS of the goal direction is not needed,
    -- since a appears on the right already.

    -- Rewrite LHS of the final goal.
    goal-int : (one‚Ą§ *‚Ą§ ‚Āļto‚Ą§ (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ k)) <‚Ą§ (a *‚Ą§ ‚Āļto‚Ą§ (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (suc k)))
    goal-int =
      <‚Ą§-resp-‚Č°ň° (sym goalLhsEq) chained
  in
  N , goal-int
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Cauchy sequences of reals and the diagonal}
\label{sec:reals:cauchyR-diag}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

A sequence of real numbers is Cauchy when, viewed through any rational
tolerance~$\varepsilon$, two sufficiently late members eventually agree
to within~$\varepsilon$ on every coordinate. The condition cannot
demand uniform agreement, since each real has its own internal
modulus; it must require eventual rational closeness on a tail.

The candidate limit is fixed by the same numerical material that
defines the sequence: from each $r_n$, choose the index that already
stabilises~$r_n$ to within~$1/(n+1)$, and read off that rational. The
resulting diagonal lives in~$\mathbb{Q}$ by construction; whether it is
Cauchy in~$\mathbb{Q}$ and whether the original sequence converges to
it, are the two propositions to be discharged.

\textbf{Constraint:} A real number is, by definition, a Cauchy
sequence of rationals. A sequence of reals is therefore a
two-parameter array of rationals, and ``convergence'' must be phrased
without invoking a metric on~$\mathbb{R}$ that has not yet been
constructed. The only tool available is the rational metric on each
column. \textbf{Construction:} The Cauchy condition demands eventual
$\varepsilon$-closeness coordinatewise; the candidate limit is the
diagonal sequence assembled from each row's own stabilisation index.
\textbf{Consequence:} No new metric structure on~$\mathbb{R}$ is
required to state completeness, and the limit is computed by the same
finite recipe that underlies the Cauchy data.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Cauchy condition for a sequence of reals (eventual őĶ-closeness on a tail).
record IsCauchy‚ĄĚ (r : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) : Set where
  field
    cauchy‚ĄĚ : (őĶ : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ ‚Üí
      ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (m n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ m ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí
        ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ K ‚Üí (k : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí K ‚Č§ k ‚Üí
          dist‚Ąö (seq (r m) k) (seq (r n) k) <‚Ąö őĶ))

-- ¬ß Convergence of a sequence of reals to a limit, again via tail closeness.
record _converges-to_ (r : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) (x : ‚ĄĚ) : Set where
  field
    conv‚ĄĚ : (őĶ : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őĶ ‚Üí
      ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ N ‚Üí (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí
        ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ K ‚Üí (k : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí K ‚Č§ k ‚Üí
          dist‚Ąö (seq (r n) k) (seq x k) <‚Ąö őĶ))

-- ¬ß Stable index of a real x at tolerance halfTo n.
diag-cauchyIdx : (x : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē
diag-cauchyIdx x n =
  fst (IsCauchy.cauchy (isCauchy x) (halfTo n) (halfTo-pos n))

-- ¬ß Diagonal sequence: nth value is x = (r n) sampled at its own stable index.
diagSeq : (‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąö
diagSeq r n = seq (r n) (diag-cauchyIdx (r n) n)

-- ¬ß Beyond the stable index, the sequence stays within halfTo n of the diagonal.
diagSeq-stable :
  (r : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (n k : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí diag-cauchyIdx (r n) n ‚Č§ k ‚Üí
  dist‚Ąö (diagSeq r n) (seq (r n) k) <‚Ąö halfTo n
diagSeq-stable r n k K‚Č§k =
  let
    pack = IsCauchy.cauchy (isCauchy (r n)) (halfTo n) (halfTo-pos n)
    K    = fst pack
    bnd  = snd pack
  in
    bnd K k (‚Č§-refl K) K‚Č§k
\end{code}

The diagonal is itself a rational Cauchy sequence. The standard
$\varepsilon/4$ accounting carries over: the schedule
$\eta = \mathtt{őĶQuarter}\,\delta$ controls the two ``side''
distances coming from the stabilising indices, while the middle
distance is bounded by~$\delta = \mathtt{őĶQuarter}\,\varepsilon$
through the Cauchy property of the source sequence of reals.

\textbf{Constraint:} The diagonal must converge in~$\mathbb{Q}$;
otherwise it cannot serve as a representative of a real limit.
\textbf{Construction:} Two triangle inequalities decompose the
distance between two diagonal terms into one stabilising error per
row plus one middle error; a single $\varepsilon/4$ budget closes
all three contributions. \textbf{Consequence:} Every Cauchy sequence
of reals produces a canonical rational Cauchy sequence, hence a
candidate real limit; nothing further than the Cauchy data is
required to construct that limit.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Diagonal of a Cauchy sequence of reals is itself Cauchy in ‚Ąö.
diagSeq-cauchy :
  (r : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí IsCauchy‚ĄĚ r ‚Üí IsCauchy (diagSeq r)
diagSeq-cauchy r icR = record
  { cauchy = őĽ őĶ őĶpos ‚Üí
      let
        őī : ‚Ąö
        őī = őĶQuarter őĶ

        őīpos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö őī
        őīpos = őĶQuarter-pos őĶ

        ő∑ : ‚Ąö
        ő∑ = őĶQuarter őī

        ő∑pos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö ő∑
        ő∑pos = őĶQuarter-pos őī

        ő∑Nonneg : 0‚Ąö ‚Č§‚Ąö ő∑
        ő∑Nonneg = <‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {0‚Ąö} {ő∑} ő∑pos

        őīNonneg : 0‚Ąö ‚Č§‚Ąö őī
        őīNonneg = <‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {0‚Ąö} {őī} őīpos

        -- Bounds at the ő∑ level: ő∑+ő∑ ‚Č§ őī.
        ő∑+ő∑‚ČÉőĶHalfőī : (ő∑ +‚Ąö ő∑) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö őĶHalf őī
        ő∑+ő∑‚ČÉőĶHalfőī = őĶQuarter+őĶQuarter‚ČÉőĶHalf őī

        ő∑+ő∑‚Č§őĶHalfőī : (ő∑ +‚Ąö ő∑) ‚Č§‚Ąö őĶHalf őī
        ő∑+ő∑‚Č§őĶHalfőī = ‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąöň° {ő∑ +‚Ąö ő∑} {őĶHalf őī} ő∑+ő∑‚ČÉőĶHalfőī

        őĶHalfőī<őī : őĶHalf őī <‚Ąö őī
        őĶHalfőī<őī = őĶHalf<őĶ őī őīpos

        őĶHalfőī‚Č§őī : őĶHalf őī ‚Č§‚Ąö őī
        őĶHalfőī‚Č§őī = <‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {őĶHalf őī} {őī} őĶHalfőī<őī

        ő∑+ő∑‚Č§őī : (ő∑ +‚Ąö ő∑) ‚Č§‚Ąö őī
        ő∑+ő∑‚Č§őī = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {ő∑ +‚Ąö ő∑} {őĶHalf őī} {őī} ő∑+ő∑‚Č§őĶHalfőī őĶHalfőī‚Č§őī

        őī+őī<őĶ : (őī +‚Ąö őī) <‚Ąö őĶ
        őī+őī<őĶ = őĶQuarter-double<őĶ őĶ őĶpos

        -- IsCauchy‚ĄĚ pack at ő∑ (mid bound).
        packR = IsCauchy‚ĄĚ.cauchy‚ĄĚ icR ő∑ ő∑pos
        N‚āā    = fst packR
        boundR = snd packR

        -- halfTo-arch at ő∑: halfTo N‚āĀ <‚Ąö ő∑.
        packH = halfTo-arch ő∑ ő∑pos
        N‚āĀ    = fst packH
        N‚āĀ<ő∑  : halfTo N‚āĀ <‚Ąö ő∑
        N‚āĀ<ő∑  = snd packH

        N‚āĀ‚Č§ő∑  : halfTo N‚āĀ ‚Č§‚Ąö ő∑
        N‚āĀ‚Č§ő∑  = <‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {halfTo N‚āĀ} {ő∑} N‚āĀ<ő∑

        N : ‚Ąē
        N = max‚Ąē N‚āĀ N‚āā
      in
        N , (őĽ m n N‚Č§m N‚Č§n ‚Üí
          let
            N‚āĀ‚Č§m : N‚āĀ ‚Č§ m
            N‚āĀ‚Č§m = ‚Č§-trans (‚Č§-max‚Ąēň° N‚āĀ N‚āā) N‚Č§m

            N‚āĀ‚Č§n : N‚āĀ ‚Č§ n
            N‚āĀ‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans (‚Č§-max‚Ąēň° N‚āĀ N‚āā) N‚Č§n

            N‚āā‚Č§m : N‚āā ‚Č§ m
            N‚āā‚Č§m = ‚Č§-trans (‚Č§-max‚Ąē ≥ N‚āĀ N‚āā) N‚Č§m

            N‚āā‚Č§n : N‚āā ‚Č§ n
            N‚āā‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans (‚Č§-max‚Ąē ≥ N‚āĀ N‚āā) N‚Č§n

            -- halfTo bounds.
            halfm‚Č§ő∑ : halfTo m ‚Č§‚Ąö ő∑
            halfm‚Č§ő∑ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {halfTo m} {halfTo N‚āĀ} {ő∑}
              (halfTo-mono N‚āĀ m N‚āĀ‚Č§m) N‚āĀ‚Č§ő∑

            halfn‚Č§ő∑ : halfTo n ‚Č§‚Ąö ő∑
            halfn‚Č§ő∑ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {halfTo n} {halfTo N‚āĀ} {ő∑}
              (halfTo-mono N‚āĀ n N‚āĀ‚Č§n) N‚āĀ‚Č§ő∑

            -- Cauchy‚ĄĚ middle bound: pick a common k below.
            packMid = boundR m n N‚āā‚Č§m N‚āā‚Č§n
            K‚āÉ      = fst packMid
            mid     : (k : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí K‚āÉ ‚Č§ k ‚Üí dist‚Ąö (seq (r m) k) (seq (r n) k) <‚Ąö ő∑
            mid     = snd packMid

            di_m = diag-cauchyIdx (r m) m
            di_n = diag-cauchyIdx (r n) n

            kInner : ‚Ąē
            kInner = max‚Ąē di_m di_n

            k : ‚Ąē
            k = max‚Ąē K‚āÉ kInner

            K‚āÉ‚Č§k : K‚āÉ ‚Č§ k
            K‚āÉ‚Č§k = ‚Č§-max‚Ąēň° K‚āÉ kInner

            kI‚Č§k : kInner ‚Č§ k
            kI‚Č§k = ‚Č§-max‚Ąē ≥ K‚āÉ kInner

            di_m‚Č§k : di_m ‚Č§ k
            di_m‚Č§k = ‚Č§-trans (‚Č§-max‚Ąēň° di_m di_n) kI‚Č§k

            di_n‚Č§k : di_n ‚Č§ k
            di_n‚Č§k = ‚Č§-trans (‚Č§-max‚Ąē ≥ di_m di_n) kI‚Č§k

            -- Three pieces.
            S1 : ‚Ąö
            S1 = dist‚Ąö (diagSeq r m) (seq (r m) k)

            S3 : ‚Ąö
            S3 = dist‚Ąö (seq (r m) k) (seq (r n) k)

            S4 : ‚Ąö
            S4 = dist‚Ąö (seq (r n) k) (diagSeq r n)

            S1< : S1 <‚Ąö halfTo m
            S1< = diagSeq-stable r m k di_m‚Č§k

            S1‚Č§ő∑ : S1 ‚Č§‚Ąö ő∑
            S1‚Č§ő∑ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {S1} {halfTo m} {ő∑}
              (<‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {S1} {halfTo m} S1<) halfm‚Č§ő∑

            S3<ő∑ : S3 <‚Ąö ő∑
            S3<ő∑ = mid k K‚āÉ‚Č§k

            S3‚Č§ő∑ : S3 ‚Č§‚Ąö ő∑
            S3‚Č§ő∑ = <‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {S3} {ő∑} S3<ő∑

            -- Symmetry on S4: diagSeq-stable gives the swapped form.
            S4-sym : dist‚Ąö (diagSeq r n) (seq (r n) k) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö S4
            S4-sym = dist‚Ąö-sym (diagSeq r n) (seq (r n) k)

            S4-sym-le : dist‚Ąö (diagSeq r n) (seq (r n) k) ‚Č§‚Ąö S4
            S4-sym-le = ‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąöň° {dist‚Ąö (diagSeq r n) (seq (r n) k)} {S4} S4-sym

            S4-sym-ge : S4 ‚Č§‚Ąö dist‚Ąö (diagSeq r n) (seq (r n) k)
            S4-sym-ge = ‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąöň° {S4} {dist‚Ąö (diagSeq r n) (seq (r n) k)}
              (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-sym {dist‚Ąö (diagSeq r n) (seq (r n) k)} {S4} S4-sym)

            S4< : S4 <‚Ąö halfTo n
            S4< = ‚Č§<‚Ąö‚Üí<‚Ąö {S4} {dist‚Ąö (diagSeq r n) (seq (r n) k)} {halfTo n}
              S4-sym-ge (diagSeq-stable r n k di_n‚Č§k)

            S4‚Č§ő∑ : S4 ‚Č§‚Ąö ő∑
            S4‚Č§ő∑ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {S4} {halfTo n} {ő∑}
              (<‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {S4} {halfTo n} S4<) halfn‚Č§ő∑

            -- Sum bounds.
            S1Nonneg : 0‚Ąö ‚Č§‚Ąö S1
            S1Nonneg = dist‚Ąö-nonneg (diagSeq r m) (seq (r m) k)

            S3Nonneg : 0‚Ąö ‚Č§‚Ąö S3
            S3Nonneg = dist‚Ąö-nonneg (seq (r m) k) (seq (r n) k)

            S4Nonneg : 0‚Ąö ‚Č§‚Ąö S4
            S4Nonneg = dist‚Ąö-nonneg (seq (r n) k) (diagSeq r n)

            S3+S4‚Č§ő∑+ő∑ : (S3 +‚Ąö S4) ‚Č§‚Ąö (ő∑ +‚Ąö ő∑)
            S3+S4‚Č§ő∑+ő∑ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-sum‚Č§double-nonneg S3 S4 ő∑
              S3Nonneg S4Nonneg ő∑Nonneg S3‚Č§ő∑ S4‚Č§ő∑

            S3+S4‚Č§őī : (S3 +‚Ąö S4) ‚Č§‚Ąö őī
            S3+S4‚Č§őī = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {S3 +‚Ąö S4} {ő∑ +‚Ąö ő∑} {őī}
              S3+S4‚Č§ő∑+ő∑ ő∑+ő∑‚Č§őī

            S3+S4Nonneg : 0‚Ąö ‚Č§‚Ąö (S3 +‚Ąö S4)
            S3+S4Nonneg = 0‚Č§‚Ąö-+‚Ąö S3 S4 S3Nonneg S4Nonneg

            S1‚Č§őī : S1 ‚Č§‚Ąö őī
            S1‚Č§őī = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {S1} {ő∑} {őī}
              S1‚Č§ő∑ (‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {ő∑} {ő∑ +‚Ąö ő∑} {őī}
                (‚Č§‚Ąö-add-nonneg-right ő∑ ő∑ ő∑Nonneg) ő∑+ő∑‚Č§őī)

            total‚Č§őī+őī : (S1 +‚Ąö (S3 +‚Ąö S4)) ‚Č§‚Ąö (őī +‚Ąö őī)
            total‚Č§őī+őī = ‚Č§‚Ąö-sum‚Č§double-nonneg S1 (S3 +‚Ąö S4) őī
              S1Nonneg S3+S4Nonneg őīNonneg S1‚Č§őī S3+S4‚Č§őī

            total<őĶ : (S1 +‚Ąö (S3 +‚Ąö S4)) <‚Ąö őĶ
            total<őĶ = ‚Č§<‚Ąö‚Üí<‚Ąö {S1 +‚Ąö (S3 +‚Ąö S4)} {őī +‚Ąö őī} {őĶ}
              total‚Č§őī+őī őī+őī<őĶ

            -- Triangle assembly.
            tri-inner : dist‚Ąö (seq (r m) k) (diagSeq r n) ‚Č§‚Ąö (S3 +‚Ąö S4)
            tri-inner = dist‚Ąö-triangle (seq (r m) k) (seq (r n) k) (diagSeq r n)

            tri-outer : dist‚Ąö (diagSeq r m) (diagSeq r n) ‚Č§‚Ąö
              (S1 +‚Ąö dist‚Ąö (seq (r m) k) (diagSeq r n))
            tri-outer = dist‚Ąö-triangle (diagSeq r m) (seq (r m) k) (diagSeq r n)

            -- Combine: S1 + dist‚Ąö(seq r m k, diagSeq r n) ‚Č§ S1 + (S3 + S4).
            outer-bnd : (S1 +‚Ąö dist‚Ąö (seq (r m) k) (diagSeq r n)) ‚Č§‚Ąö
              (S1 +‚Ąö (S3 +‚Ąö S4))
            outer-bnd = ‚Č§‚Ąö-+‚Ąö-mono-left S1
              (dist‚Ąö (seq (r m) k) (diagSeq r n)) (S3 +‚Ąö S4) tri-inner

            full‚Č§ : dist‚Ąö (diagSeq r m) (diagSeq r n) ‚Č§‚Ąö (S1 +‚Ąö (S3 +‚Ąö S4))
            full‚Č§ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans
              {dist‚Ąö (diagSeq r m) (diagSeq r n)}
              {S1 +‚Ąö dist‚Ąö (seq (r m) k) (diagSeq r n)}
              {S1 +‚Ąö (S3 +‚Ąö S4)}
              tri-outer outer-bnd
          in
            ‚Č§<‚Ąö‚Üí<‚Ąö {dist‚Ąö (diagSeq r m) (diagSeq r n)}
                    {S1 +‚Ąö (S3 +‚Ąö S4)} {őĶ}
              full‚Č§ total<őĶ)
  }
\end{code}

The diagonal already lives in~$\mathbb{R}$: package the rational
Cauchy sequence with its Cauchy proof to obtain the candidate limit.
Two further rounds of the same triangle bookkeeping then show that the
original sequence of reals converges to it, where convergence is
again read coordinatewise on the rational tails.

\textbf{Constraint:} The candidate limit must be inhabited by a real
number, and the original sequence must approach it in the only sense
available---rational closeness on a tail. \textbf{Construction:} The
limit is the record built from the diagonal sequence and its Cauchy
proof; convergence is obtained by inserting the diagonal as the middle
point of a single triangle, where one side is bounded by
\texttt{diagSeq-stable} and the other by the diagonal's own Cauchy
modulus. \textbf{Consequence:} $\mathbb{R}$, defined as Cauchy
sequences of rationals modulo eventual closeness, is itself
sequentially complete: every Cauchy sequence of reals has a real
limit.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß The diagonal packaged as a real number: the limit candidate.
diag‚ĄĚ : (r : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí IsCauchy‚ĄĚ r ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ
diag‚ĄĚ r ic = mk‚ĄĚ (diagSeq r) (diagSeq-cauchy r ic)

-- ¬ß Convergence: each row r n approximates the diagonal limit.
diag‚ĄĚ-converges :
  (r : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (ic : IsCauchy‚ĄĚ r) ‚Üí
  r converges-to diag‚ĄĚ r ic
diag‚ĄĚ-converges r ic = record
  { conv‚ĄĚ = őĽ őĶ őĶpos ‚Üí
      let
        ő∑ : ‚Ąö
        ő∑ = őĶQuarter őĶ

        ő∑pos : 0‚Ąö <‚Ąö ő∑
        ő∑pos = őĶQuarter-pos őĶ

        ő∑Nonneg : 0‚Ąö ‚Č§‚Ąö ő∑
        ő∑Nonneg = <‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {0‚Ąö} {ő∑} ő∑pos

        ő∑+ő∑<őĶ : (ő∑ +‚Ąö ő∑) <‚Ąö őĶ
        ő∑+ő∑<őĶ = őĶQuarter-double<őĶ őĶ őĶpos

        -- diagSeq is Cauchy; pack at ő∑.
        ic-diag : IsCauchy (diagSeq r)
        ic-diag = diagSeq-cauchy r ic

        packD = IsCauchy.cauchy ic-diag ő∑ ő∑pos
        N_d   : ‚Ąē
        N_d   = fst packD
        diagBnd : (m n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí N_d ‚Č§ m ‚Üí N_d ‚Č§ n ‚Üí
                  dist‚Ąö (diagSeq r m) (diagSeq r n) <‚Ąö ő∑
        diagBnd = snd packD

        -- halfTo schedule control.
        packH = halfTo-arch ő∑ ő∑pos
        N_h   : ‚Ąē
        N_h   = fst packH
        N_h<ő∑ : halfTo N_h <‚Ąö ő∑
        N_h<ő∑ = snd packH

        N_h‚Č§ő∑ : halfTo N_h ‚Č§‚Ąö ő∑
        N_h‚Č§ő∑ = <‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {halfTo N_h} {ő∑} N_h<ő∑

        N : ‚Ąē
        N = max‚Ąē N_d N_h
      in
        N , (őĽ n N‚Č§n ‚Üí
          let
            N_d‚Č§n : N_d ‚Č§ n
            N_d‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans (‚Č§-max‚Ąēň° N_d N_h) N‚Č§n

            N_h‚Č§n : N_h ‚Č§ n
            N_h‚Č§n = ‚Č§-trans (‚Č§-max‚Ąē ≥ N_d N_h) N‚Č§n

            di_n : ‚Ąē
            di_n = diag-cauchyIdx (r n) n

            K : ‚Ąē
            K = max‚Ąē di_n N_d

            di_n‚Č§K : di_n ‚Č§ K
            di_n‚Č§K = ‚Č§-max‚Ąēň° di_n N_d

            N_d‚Č§K : N_d ‚Č§ K
            N_d‚Č§K = ‚Č§-max‚Ąē ≥ di_n N_d

            halfn‚Č§ő∑ : halfTo n ‚Č§‚Ąö ő∑
            halfn‚Č§ő∑ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {halfTo n} {halfTo N_h} {ő∑}
              (halfTo-mono N_h n N_h‚Č§n) N_h‚Č§ő∑
          in
            K , (őĽ k K‚Č§k ‚Üí
              let
                di_n‚Č§k : di_n ‚Č§ k
                di_n‚Č§k = ‚Č§-trans di_n‚Č§K K‚Č§k

                N_d‚Č§k : N_d ‚Č§ k
                N_d‚Č§k = ‚Č§-trans N_d‚Č§K K‚Č§k

                S1 : ‚Ąö
                S1 = dist‚Ąö (seq (r n) k) (diagSeq r n)

                S2 : ‚Ąö
                S2 = dist‚Ąö (diagSeq r n) (diagSeq r k)

                -- S1 bound via symmetry of dist‚Ąö + diagSeq-stable.
                S1-sym : dist‚Ąö (diagSeq r n) (seq (r n) k) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö S1
                S1-sym = dist‚Ąö-sym (diagSeq r n) (seq (r n) k)

                S1-sym-ge : S1 ‚Č§‚Ąö dist‚Ąö (diagSeq r n) (seq (r n) k)
                S1-sym-ge = ‚ČÉ‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąöň° {S1} {dist‚Ąö (diagSeq r n) (seq (r n) k)}
                  (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-sym {dist‚Ąö (diagSeq r n) (seq (r n) k)} {S1} S1-sym)

                S1<halfn : S1 <‚Ąö halfTo n
                S1<halfn = ‚Č§<‚Ąö‚Üí<‚Ąö
                  {S1} {dist‚Ąö (diagSeq r n) (seq (r n) k)} {halfTo n}
                  S1-sym-ge (diagSeq-stable r n k di_n‚Č§k)

                S1‚Č§ő∑ : S1 ‚Č§‚Ąö ő∑
                S1‚Č§ő∑ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-trans {S1} {halfTo n} {ő∑}
                  (<‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {S1} {halfTo n} S1<halfn) halfn‚Č§ő∑

                S2<ő∑ : S2 <‚Ąö ő∑
                S2<ő∑ = diagBnd n k N_d‚Č§n N_d‚Č§k

                S2‚Č§ő∑ : S2 ‚Č§‚Ąö ő∑
                S2‚Č§ő∑ = <‚Ąö‚Üí‚Č§‚Ąö {S2} {ő∑} S2<ő∑

                S1Nonneg : 0‚Ąö ‚Č§‚Ąö S1
                S1Nonneg = dist‚Ąö-nonneg (seq (r n) k) (diagSeq r n)

                S2Nonneg : 0‚Ąö ‚Č§‚Ąö S2
                S2Nonneg = dist‚Ąö-nonneg (diagSeq r n) (diagSeq r k)

                sum‚Č§ : (S1 +‚Ąö S2) ‚Č§‚Ąö (ő∑ +‚Ąö ő∑)
                sum‚Č§ = ‚Č§‚Ąö-sum‚Č§double-nonneg S1 S2 ő∑
                  S1Nonneg S2Nonneg ő∑Nonneg S1‚Č§ő∑ S2‚Č§ő∑

                sum<őĶ : (S1 +‚Ąö S2) <‚Ąö őĶ
                sum<őĶ = ‚Č§<‚Ąö‚Üí<‚Ąö {S1 +‚Ąö S2} {ő∑ +‚Ąö ő∑} {őĶ} sum‚Č§ ő∑+ő∑<őĶ

                tri : dist‚Ąö (seq (r n) k) (diagSeq r k) ‚Č§‚Ąö (S1 +‚Ąö S2)
                tri = dist‚Ąö-triangle (seq (r n) k) (diagSeq r n) (diagSeq r k)
              in
                ‚Č§<‚Ąö‚Üí<‚Ąö {dist‚Ąö (seq (r n) k) (diagSeq r k)} {S1 +‚Ąö S2} {őĶ}
                  tri sum<őĶ))
  }

-- ¬ß Cauchy completeness of ‚ĄĚ: every Cauchy sequence of reals converges.
‚ĄĚ-cauchy-complete :
  (r : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí IsCauchy‚ĄĚ r ‚Üí ő£ ‚ĄĚ (őĽ x ‚Üí r converges-to x)
‚ĄĚ-cauchy-complete r ic = diag‚ĄĚ r ic , diag‚ĄĚ-converges r ic
\end{code}


%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź
\part{Derived Constants of \texorpdfstring{$K_4$}{K4}}
%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź

Part~VI collects the combinatorial and spectral invariants of $K_4$
into explicit verified records and computes their numerical values.
The aim is not to introduce new structure, but to evaluate, in closed
form, what has already been derived.

The vertex count, edge count, Euler characteristic, and Laplacian
eigenvalues are assembled into a single computation record, each field
being witnessed by definitional or proved equality in Agda. In
particular, the graph evaluation $\mathcal{C} = 137$ appears here as a
consequence of the earlier derivation together with the arithmetic of
Part~V.

The record is then shown to be unique: any two admissible invariant
records agree fieldwise. The final results return to the initial
distinction $D_0$ and show how the measurement-theoretic and
eliminative strands close the formal circle.

One should distinguish the internal and external readings of these
computations. Internally, the claim is that the stated equalities
reduce and the stated numbers are derivable in the formal system. Any
broader interpretation lies outside the formal argument.

%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź
\chapter{Natural-Number Division Infrastructure}
\label{ch:natural-number-division}
%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź

Several later combinatorial identities require exact division at the
level of natural numbers, but the safe base environment does not
provide the requisite primitives. This chapter supplies the small
amount of arithmetic infrastructure needed for those calculations while
preserving totality.

Two fuel-bounded procedures are introduced: a greatest common divisor
and an exact division routine. In each case recursion is controlled by
an explicit natural-number counter, because the present base offers no
general well-founded recursion principle. The fuel is chosen as the
sum of the inputs; each Euclidean subtraction step reduces that sum,
so this bound suffices.

The same idea governs exact division. The number of successful
subtractions is made explicit, and the resulting procedure remains
total.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Boolean comparison on ‚Ąē
_‚Č§‚Ąē_ : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí Bool
zero  ‚Č§‚Ąē _     = true
suc _ ‚Č§‚Ąē zero  = false
suc m ‚Č§‚Ąē suc n = m ‚Č§‚Ąē n

-- ¬ß Fuel-bounded GCD
gcd-fuel : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē
gcd-fuel zero    m _       = m
gcd-fuel (suc _) zero n    = n
gcd-fuel (suc _) m zero    = m
gcd-fuel (suc f) (suc m) (suc n) with (suc m) ‚Č§‚Ąē (suc n)
... | true  = gcd-fuel f (suc m) (n ‚ął m)
... | false = gcd-fuel f (m ‚ął n) (suc n)

-- ¬ß One-step exposure of gcd-fuel on positive inputs.
gcd-fuel-step : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí Bool ‚Üí ‚Ąē
gcd-fuel-step f m n true = gcd-fuel f m (n ‚ął m)
gcd-fuel-step f m n false = gcd-fuel f (m ‚ął n) n

gcd-fuel-suc-suc : (f m n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí gcd-fuel (suc f) (suc m) (suc n) ‚Č° gcd-fuel-step f (suc m) (suc n) (m ‚Č§‚Ąē n)
gcd-fuel-suc-suc f m n with m ‚Č§‚Ąē n
... | true = refl
... | false = refl

-- ¬ß GCD with canonical fuel
gcd : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē
gcd m n = gcd-fuel (m + n) m n
\end{code}

Once the gcd routine is available, the only remaining primitive is
exact division. When a divisor $d$ divides $n$, the quotient must be
computable at the level of natural numbers. Division is again
fuel-bounded, counting how many times the divisor can be subtracted
before the remainder falls below it.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Successor coercion to ‚Ąē‚Āļ
sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē‚Āļ
sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ zero = one‚Āļ
sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ (suc n) = suc‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ n)

-- ¬ß General ‚Ąē to ‚Ąē‚Āļ coercion
‚Ąē-to-‚Ąē‚Āļ : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē‚Āļ
‚Ąē-to-‚Ąē‚Āļ = mk‚Ąē‚Āļ

-- ¬ß Fuel-bounded integer division
div-fuel : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē‚Āļ ‚Üí ‚Ąē
div-fuel zero    _       _ = zero
div-fuel (suc f) n d with ‚Āļto‚Ąē d ‚Č§‚Ąē n
... | true  = suc (div-fuel f (n ‚ął ‚Āļto‚Ąē d) d)
... | false = zero

-- ¬ß One-step exposure of div-fuel on positive fuel.
div-fuel-step : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē‚Āļ ‚Üí Bool ‚Üí ‚Ąē
div-fuel-step f n d true = suc (div-fuel f (n ‚ął ‚Āļto‚Ąē d) d)
div-fuel-step f n d false = zero

div-fuel-suc : (f n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí (d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí div-fuel (suc f) n d ‚Č° div-fuel-step f n d (‚Āļto‚Ąē d ‚Č§‚Ąē n)
div-fuel-suc f n d with ‚Āļto‚Ąē d ‚Č§‚Ąē n
... | true = refl
... | false = refl

-- ¬ß Division by ‚Ąē‚Āļ
_div_ : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē‚Āļ ‚Üí ‚Ąē
n div d = div-fuel n n d

-- ¬ß Division by ‚Ąē (zero-safe)
_div‚Ąē_ : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē
_ div‚Ąē zero = zero
n div‚Ąē (suc d) = n div (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ d)
\end{code}

\section{Canonical Quotient Data and Reduced Witnesses}
\label{sec:rational-measurement:canonical-quotient-data}

The quotient structure of the rationals must also be made explicit at
the level of observable data. For the later normalization arguments,
numerator and denominator information cannot remain implicit in the
setoid presentation.

The gcd and division infrastructure developed above allows one to
extract reduced witnesses for every rational class, including zero and
negative values. The next definitions therefore make quotient data
visible, introduce the corresponding presentation records, and prepare
the normalization and uniqueness statements that follow.

\subsection{Quotient presentation and field uniqueness}

Before reduction can be discussed, the rational type must expose its
quotient fields as inspectable data. The lift into
$\mathit{Set}_\omega$ makes rationals available as values of
admissible observables, and the presentation record packages the
numerator and denominator together with the witnessing equality.

The construction is direct. The wrapper \texttt{‚ĄöŌČ} places rationals
in the ambient universe, and \texttt{RationalPresentation} records the
two constructor fields of a given rational. Since a fixed rational
already determines those fields, the resulting presentation is unique.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Lift rationals into SetŌČ so admissible observables can target them.
record ‚ĄöŌČ : SetŌČ where
  constructor wrap‚Ąö
  field
    lower‚Ąö : ‚Ąö

open ‚ĄöŌČ public

AdmissibleRationalObservable : SetŌČ
AdmissibleRationalObservable = AdmissibleObservable ‚ĄöŌČ

-- ¬ß Quotient presentation of a rational value.
record RationalPresentation (q : ‚Ąö) : Set where
  field
    pres-num : ‚Ą§
    pres-den : ‚Ąē‚Āļ
    presents-q : q ‚Č° pres-num / pres-den

-- ¬ß The constructor fields are already the canonical quotient data.
canonical-rational-presentation : (q : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí RationalPresentation q
canonical-rational-presentation (n / d) = record
  { pres-num = n
  ; pres-den = d
  ; presents-q = refl
  }

-- ¬ß Quotient fields are unique once a rational value is fixed.
rational-fields-unique :
  (q : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí
  (n‚āĀ n‚āā : ‚Ą§) ‚Üí
  (d‚āĀ d‚āā : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí
  q ‚Č° (n‚āĀ / d‚āĀ) ‚Üí
  q ‚Č° (n‚āā / d‚āā) ‚Üí
  (n‚āĀ ‚Č° n‚āā) √ó (d‚āĀ ‚Č° d‚āā)
rational-fields-unique (n / d) n‚āĀ n‚āā d‚āĀ d‚āā refl refl = refl , refl
\end{code}

\subsection{Reduced witnesses and irreducibility}

Once the quotient fields are visible, one can ask when a rational is
already in reduced form. The relevant condition is coprimality: a
quotient $n/d$ is \emph{irreducible} when
$\gcd(|n|, d) = 1$.

A \emph{reduced rational witness} packages a nonnegative numerator, a
positive denominator, the equation to the original rational, and a
proof of coprimality. Uniqueness of such witnesses uses two facts: the
uniqueness of quotient fields established above and the injectivity of
the embedding $\mathbb{N} \hookrightarrow \mathbb{Z}$, which is proved
here for later use.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß The ‚Ąē-embedding into ‚Ą§ is injective.
from‚Ąē‚Ą§-injective : {m n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí from‚Ąē‚Ą§ m ‚Č° from‚Ąē‚Ą§ n ‚Üí m ‚Č° n
from‚Ąē‚Ą§-injective {zero} {zero} refl = refl
from‚Ąē‚Ą§-injective {zero} {suc n} ()
from‚Ąē‚Ą§-injective {suc m} {zero} ()
from‚Ąē‚Ą§-injective {suc m} {suc n} refl = refl

-- ¬ß Irreducibility for rational quotients: numerator magnitude and denominator are coprime.
IrreducibleQuotient : ‚Ąö ‚Üí Set
IrreducibleQuotient q = gcd (mag‚Ą§ (num q)) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q)) ‚Č° 1

-- ¬ß Reduced witness for a rational quotient with nonnegative numerator.
record ReducedRationalWitness (q : ‚Ąö) : Set where
  field
    red-num : ‚Ąē
    red-den : ‚Ąē‚Āļ
    red-value : q ‚Č° from‚Ąē‚Ą§ red-num / red-den
    red-coprime : gcd red-num (‚Āļto‚Ąē red-den) ‚Č° 1

-- ¬ß Reduced witnesses are unique whenever they exist.
reduced-rational-witness-unique :
  {q : ‚Ąö} ‚Üí
  (r‚āĀ r‚āā : ReducedRationalWitness q) ‚Üí
  ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r‚āĀ ‚Č° ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r‚āā
  √ó ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r‚āĀ ‚Č° ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r‚āā
reduced-rational-witness-unique {q} r‚āĀ r‚āā =
  let
    pair : from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r‚āĀ)
         ‚Č° from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r‚āā)
         √ó (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r‚āĀ ‚Č° ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r‚āā)
    pair =
      rational-fields-unique q
        (from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r‚āĀ))
        (from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r‚āā))
        (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r‚āĀ)
        (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r‚āā)
        (ReducedRationalWitness.red-value r‚āĀ)
        (ReducedRationalWitness.red-value r‚āā)
  in
  from‚Ąē‚Ą§-injective (fst pair) , snd pair
\end{code}

\subsection{Arithmetic lemmas and exact division}

The reduction algorithm requires a short list of elementary arithmetic
lemmas about subtraction, comparison reflection, and exact division.
These results make the later normalization proofs fully explicit
inside the type theory.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Magnitude of the ‚Ąē-embedding is the original natural number.
mag‚Ą§-from‚Ąē‚Ą§ : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí mag‚Ą§ (from‚Ąē‚Ą§ n) ‚Č° n
mag‚Ą§-from‚Ąē‚Ą§ zero = refl
mag‚Ą§-from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (suc n) = refl

-- ¬ß Subtracting zero on the right does nothing.
‚ął-zero-right : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí n ‚ął zero ‚Č° n
‚ął-zero-right zero = refl
‚ął-zero-right (suc n) = refl

-- ¬ß Subtracting one from a successor recovers the predecessor.
‚ął-one-suc : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí suc n ‚ął 1 ‚Č° n
‚ął-one-suc n = ‚ął-zero-right n

-- ¬ß A left summand cancels against subtraction from the whole sum.
+‚Ąē-‚ął-cancel-left : (m n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí (m +‚Ąē n) ‚ął m ‚Č° n
+‚Ąē-‚ął-cancel-left zero n = ‚ął-zero-right n
+‚Ąē-‚ął-cancel-left (suc m) n = +‚Ąē-‚ął-cancel-left m n

-- ¬ß Every natural is ‚Č§‚Ąē its own right extension.
‚Č§‚Ąē-self-+‚Ąē-right : (m n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí m ‚Č§‚Ąē (m +‚Ąē n) ‚Č° true
‚Č§‚Ąē-self-+‚Ąē-right zero n = refl
‚Č§‚Ąē-self-+‚Ąē-right (suc m) n = ‚Č§‚Ąē-self-+‚Ąē-right m n

-- ¬ß Boolean ‚Č§‚Ąē reflects propositional ‚Č§.
‚Č§‚Ąē-true‚Üí‚Č§ : {m n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí m ‚Č§‚Ąē n ‚Č° true ‚Üí m ‚Č§ n
‚Č§‚Ąē-true‚Üí‚Č§ {zero} {n} _ = z‚Č§n
‚Č§‚Ąē-true‚Üí‚Č§ {suc m} {zero} ()
‚Č§‚Ąē-true‚Üí‚Č§ {suc m} {suc n} eq = s‚Č§s (‚Č§‚Ąē-true‚Üí‚Č§ eq)

-- ¬ß Boolean failure of ‚Č§‚Ąē forces the reverse propositional order.
‚Č§‚Ąē-false‚Üí‚Č§ : {m n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí m ‚Č§‚Ąē n ‚Č° false ‚Üí n ‚Č§ m
‚Č§‚Ąē-false‚Üí‚Č§ {zero} {n} ()
‚Č§‚Ąē-false‚Üí‚Č§ {suc m} {zero} _ = z‚Č§n
‚Č§‚Ąē-false‚Üí‚Č§ {suc m} {suc n} eq = s‚Č§s (‚Č§‚Ąē-false‚Üí‚Č§ eq)

-- ¬ß Ordered subtraction splits the larger number into base plus remainder.
‚Č§‚Üí+‚Ąē-‚ął-split : {m n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí m ‚Č§ n ‚Üí m +‚Ąē (n ‚ął m) ‚Č° n
‚Č§‚Üí+‚Ąē-‚ął-split {zero} {n} z‚Č§n = ‚ął-zero-right n
‚Č§‚Üí+‚Ąē-‚ął-split {suc m} {suc n} (s‚Č§s p) = cong suc (‚Č§‚Üí+‚Ąē-‚ął-split p)

-- ¬ß Packing the predecessor of a positive natural recovers the same ‚Ąē‚Āļ.
mk‚Ąē‚Āļ-‚Āļto‚Ąē-minus-one : (d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (‚Āļto‚Ąē d ‚ął 1) ‚Č° d
mk‚Ąē‚Āļ-‚Āļto‚Ąē-minus-one (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p) = cong mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (‚ął-zero-right p)

-- ¬ß Division by one leaves naturals unchanged.
div-one‚Āļ : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí n div one‚Āļ ‚Č° n
div-one‚Āļ zero = refl
div-one‚Āļ (suc n) =
  cong suc
    (trans
      (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí div-fuel n t one‚Āļ) (‚ął-zero-right n))
      (div-one‚Āļ n))

\end{code}

The exact-division theorem states that the fuel parameter has no
semantic effect on an exact multiple. The proof is a simple induction
on the quotient: one subtraction step removes one copy of the divisor,
passes the remaining slack forward, and invokes the inductive
hypothesis. Thus, whenever divisibility is known, the bounded division
procedure computes the correct quotient.

\begin{code}

-- ¬ß Division recovers the quotient on exact multiples, even with extra fuel.
div-fuel-exact-multiple :
  (q slack : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí (d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí
  div-fuel ((q *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d) +‚Ąē slack) (q *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d) d ‚Č° q
div-fuel-exact-multiple zero zero (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p) = refl
div-fuel-exact-multiple zero (suc slack) (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p) = refl
div-fuel-exact-multiple (suc q) slack (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p) =
  let
    qk : ‚Ąē
    qk = q *‚Ąē suc p

    fuelShape : ((suc q *‚Ąē suc p) +‚Ąē slack) ‚Č° suc (p +‚Ąē (qk +‚Ąē slack))
    fuelShape = cong suc (+‚Ąē-assoc p qk slack)

    cmpEq : suc p ‚Č§‚Ąē (suc q *‚Ąē suc p) ‚Č° true
    cmpEq = ‚Č§‚Ąē-self-+‚Ąē-right p qk

    subEq : (suc q *‚Ąē suc p) ‚ął suc p ‚Č° qk
    subEq = +‚Ąē-‚ął-cancel-left (suc p) qk

    fuelEq : p +‚Ąē (qk +‚Ąē slack) ‚Č° qk +‚Ąē (p +‚Ąē slack)
    fuelEq =
      trans
        (sym (+‚Ąē-assoc p qk slack))
        (trans
          (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t +‚Ąē slack) (+‚Ąē-comm p qk))
          (+‚Ąē-assoc qk p slack))
  in
  trans
    (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí div-fuel t (suc q *‚Ąē suc p) (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p)) fuelShape)
    (trans
      (div-fuel-suc (p +‚Ąē (qk +‚Ąē slack)) (suc q *‚Ąē suc p) (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p))
      (trans
        (cong (div-fuel-step (p +‚Ąē (qk +‚Ąē slack)) (suc q *‚Ąē suc p) (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p)) cmpEq)
        (cong suc
          (trans
            (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí div-fuel (p +‚Ąē (qk +‚Ąē slack)) t (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p)) subEq)
            (trans
              (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí div-fuel t qk (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p)) fuelEq)
              (div-fuel-exact-multiple q (p +‚Ąē slack) (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p)))))))

-- ¬ß Division by a positive factor recovers the quotient on exact multiples.
div-exact-multiple : (q : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí (d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí (q *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d) div d ‚Č° q
div-exact-multiple q d =
  trans
    (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí div-fuel t (q *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d) d) (sym (+‚Ąē-zero-right (q *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d))))
    (div-fuel-exact-multiple q zero d)
\end{code}

\subsection{Positive divisibility theory}

A constructive divisibility notion is now needed for the gcd
development. The statement that $d$ divides $n$ must carry an explicit
quotient and factorization proof, because later arguments extract that
quotient to compute exact division.

The record \texttt{Divides‚Āļ} packages precisely this data. From it one
derives the basic closure properties required later: addition,
subtraction, multiplication, cancellation, order reflection, and
extraction of exact quotients.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Positive divisibility packages an explicit quotient witness.
record Divides‚Āļ (d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) (n : ‚Ąē) : Set where
  field
    quotient‚Āļ : ‚Ąē
    factor‚Āļ : n ‚Č° quotient‚Āļ *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d

open Divides‚Āļ public

-- ¬ß Every exact multiple yields a positive divisibility witness.
divides‚Āļ-multiple : (q : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí (d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí Divides‚Āļ d (q *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d)
divides‚Āļ-multiple q d = record
  { quotient‚Āļ = q
  ; factor‚Āļ = refl
  }

-- ¬ß The divisor divides itself.
divides‚Āļ-self : (d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí Divides‚Āļ d (‚Āļto‚Ąē d)
divides‚Āļ-self d = record
  { quotient‚Āļ = oneNat
  ; factor‚Āļ = sym (*‚Ąē-one-left (‚Āļto‚Ąē d))
  }

-- ¬ß Zero is divisible by every positive natural.
divides‚Āļ-zero : (d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí Divides‚Āļ d zero
divides‚Āļ-zero d = record
  { quotient‚Āļ = zero
  ; factor‚Āļ = refl
  }

-- ¬ß Positive divisibility is closed under addition.
divides‚Āļ-+‚Ąē : {d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ} {m n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí Divides‚Āļ d m ‚Üí Divides‚Āļ d n ‚Üí Divides‚Āļ d (m +‚Ąē n)
divides‚Āļ-+‚Ąē {d} {m} {n} dm dn = record
  { quotient‚Āļ = quotient‚Āļ dm +‚Ąē quotient‚Āļ dn
  ; factor‚Āļ =
      trans
        (cong‚āā _+‚Ąē_ (factor‚Āļ dm) (factor‚Āļ dn))
        (sym (*‚Ąē-distrib-left-+‚Ąē (quotient‚Āļ dm) (quotient‚Āļ dn) (‚Āļto‚Ąē d)))
  }

-- ¬ß Exact division extracts the stored quotient from a positive divisibility witness.
divides‚Āļ‚Üídiv : {d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ} {n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí (dvd : Divides‚Āļ d n) ‚Üí n div d ‚Č° quotient‚Āļ dvd
divides‚Āļ‚Üídiv {d} {n} dvd =
  trans
    (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t div d) (factor‚Āļ dvd))
    (div-exact-multiple (quotient‚Āļ dvd) d)

-- ¬ß A positive divisible natural has a positive stored quotient.
divides‚Āļ-positive-quotient : {d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ} {n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí (dvd : Divides‚Āļ d (suc n)) ‚Üí ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ k ‚Üí quotient‚Āļ dvd ‚Č° suc k)
divides‚Āļ-positive-quotient {d} {n} dvd with quotient‚Āļ dvd | factor‚Āļ dvd
... | zero | ()
... | suc k | _ = k , refl

-- ¬ß Built-in addition and manuscript addition coincide on naturals.
+-to-+‚Ąē : (m n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí (m + n) ‚Č° (m +‚Ąē n)
+-to-+‚Ąē zero n = refl
+-to-+‚Ąē (suc m) n = cong suc (+-to-+‚Ąē m n)

-- ¬ß The ‚Ąē-view of ‚Ąē‚Āļ multiplication is ordinary natural multiplication.
‚Āļto‚Ąē-*‚Āļ : (x y : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí ‚Āļto‚Ąē (x *‚Āļ y) ‚Č° ‚Āļto‚Ąē x *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē y
‚Āļto‚Ąē-*‚Āļ (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ a) (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ b) = cong suc (+‚Ąē-comm (a *‚Ąē suc b) b)

-- ¬ß Divisibility lifts through multiplication by a positive factor on the right.
divides‚Āļ-mul-right : {e : ‚Ąē‚Āļ} {n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí Divides‚Āļ e n ‚Üí (d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí Divides‚Āļ (e *‚Āļ d) (n *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d)
divides‚Āļ-mul-right {e} {n} dvd d = record
  { quotient‚Āļ = quotient‚Āļ dvd
  ; factor‚Āļ =
      trans
        (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d) (factor‚Āļ dvd))
        (trans
          (*‚Ąē-assoc (quotient‚Āļ dvd) (‚Āļto‚Ąē e) (‚Āļto‚Ąē d))
          (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí quotient‚Āļ dvd *‚Ąē t) (sym (‚Āļto‚Ąē-*‚Āļ e d))))
  }

-- ¬ß Equality after multiplying by the same positive factor cancels on the right.
*‚Ąē-cancel ≥-suc : {a b : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí (k : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí a *‚Ąē suc k ‚Č° b *‚Ąē suc k ‚Üí a ‚Č° b
*‚Ąē-cancel ≥-suc {a} {b} k eq =
  ‚Č§-antisym
    (‚Č§-*‚Ąē-cancel ≥-suc k (subst (őĽ t ‚Üí (a *‚Ąē suc k) ‚Č§ t) eq (‚Č§-refl (a *‚Ąē suc k))))
    (‚Č§-*‚Ąē-cancel ≥-suc k (subst (őĽ t ‚Üí (b *‚Ąē suc k) ‚Č§ t) (sym eq) (‚Č§-refl (b *‚Ąē suc k))))

-- ¬ß A product of two positive naturals equals one only in the trivial case.
positive-product-one : (a b : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí suc a *‚Ąē suc b ‚Č° oneNat ‚Üí (a ‚Č° zero) √ó (b ‚Č° zero)
positive-product-one a zero eq =
  let
    aEq : suc a ‚Č° oneNat
    aEq = trans (sym (*‚Ąē-one-right (suc a))) eq
  in
  suc-injective aEq , refl
positive-product-one a (suc b) ()

-- ¬ß If a product divisor of d also divides d, the extra factor must be one.
divides‚Āļ-product-factor-left-one : (e d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí Divides‚Āļ (e *‚Āļ d) (‚Āļto‚Ąē d) ‚Üí e ‚Č° one‚Āļ
divides‚Āļ-product-factor-left-one e d dvd =
  let
    qPos : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ k ‚Üí quotient‚Āļ dvd ‚Č° suc k)
    qPos = divides‚Āļ-positive-quotient dvd

    k : ‚Ąē
    k = fst qPos

    qEq : quotient‚Āļ dvd ‚Č° suc k
    qEq = snd qPos

    factored : ‚Āļto‚Ąē d ‚Č° ((suc k *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē e) *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d)
    factored =
      trans
        (factor‚Āļ dvd)
        (trans
          (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē (e *‚Āļ d)) qEq)
          (trans
            (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí suc k *‚Ąē t) (‚Āļto‚Ąē-*‚Āļ e d))
            (sym (*‚Ąē-assoc (suc k) (‚Āļto‚Ąē e) (‚Āļto‚Ąē d)))))

    prodOne : suc k *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē e ‚Č° oneNat
    prodOne =
      *‚Ąē-cancel ≥-suc
        (pred d)
        (trans
          (sym factored)
          (sym (*‚Ąē-one-left (‚Āļto‚Ąē d))))

    ePredZero : pred e ‚Č° zero
    ePredZero = snd (positive-product-one k (pred e) prodOne)
  in
  cong mk‚Ąē‚Āļ ePredZero
\end{code}

These factor lemmas allow comparison of stored quotients. If $d$
divides both $m$ and $n$ and $m \le n$, then the quotient associated
with $m$ is at most the quotient associated with $n$.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Order on divisible multiples reflects to the stored quotients.
divides‚Āļ-quotient-‚Č§ :
  {d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ} {m n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí
  m ‚Č§ n ‚Üí
  (dm : Divides‚Āļ d m) ‚Üí
  (dn : Divides‚Āļ d n) ‚Üí
  Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm ‚Č§ Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn
divides‚Āļ-quotient-‚Č§ {mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p} {m} {n} m‚Č§n dm dn =
  let
    step‚āĀ : m ‚Č§ (Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn *‚Ąē (suc p))
    step‚āĀ = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí m ‚Č§ t) (Divides‚Āļ.factor‚Āļ dn) m‚Č§n

    step‚āā : (Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm *‚Ąē (suc p)) ‚Č§ (Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn *‚Ąē (suc p))
    step‚āā = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ (Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn *‚Ąē (suc p))) (Divides‚Āļ.factor‚Āļ dm) step‚āĀ
  in
  ‚Č§-*‚Ąē-cancel ≥-suc p step‚āā

-- ¬ß Positive divisibility survives subtraction of a smaller divisible multiple.
divides‚Āļ-‚ął-‚Č§ :
  {d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ} {m n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí
  m ‚Č§ n ‚Üí
  Divides‚Āļ d m ‚Üí
  Divides‚Āļ d n ‚Üí
  Divides‚Āļ d (n ‚ął m)
divides‚Āļ-‚ął-‚Č§ {d} {m} {n} m‚Č§n dm dn = record
  { quotient‚Āļ = Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn ‚ął Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm
  ; factor‚Āļ = factor
  }
  where
    q‚Č§ : Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm ‚Č§ Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn
    q‚Č§ = divides‚Āļ-quotient-‚Č§ m‚Č§n dm dn

    qSplit : Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm +‚Ąē (Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn ‚ął Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm) ‚Č° Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn
    qSplit = ‚Č§‚Üí+‚Ąē-‚ął-split q‚Č§

    nAsSum : n ‚Č° m +‚Ąē ((Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn ‚ął Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm) *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d)
    nAsSum =
      trans
        (Divides‚Āļ.factor‚Āļ dn)
        (trans
          (sym (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d) qSplit))
          (trans
            (*‚Ąē-distrib-left-+‚Ąē (Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm) (Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn ‚ął Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm) (‚Āļto‚Ąē d))
            (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t +‚Ąē ((Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn ‚ął Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm) *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d)) (sym (Divides‚Āļ.factor‚Āļ dm)))))

    factor : n ‚ął m ‚Č° (Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn ‚ął Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm) *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d
    factor = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚ął m ‚Č° (Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn ‚ął Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm) *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d) (sym nAsSum)
      (+‚Ąē-‚ął-cancel-left m ((Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dn ‚ął Divides‚Āļ.quotient‚Āļ dm) *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d))

-- ¬ß Adding back a divisible remainder recovers divisibility of the larger number.
divides‚Āļ-restore-‚ął :
  {d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ} {m n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí
  m ‚Č§ n ‚Üí
  Divides‚Āļ d m ‚Üí
  Divides‚Āļ d (n ‚ął m) ‚Üí
  Divides‚Āļ d n
divides‚Āļ-restore-‚ął {d} {m} {n} m‚Č§n dm dnm =
  subst
    (Divides‚Āļ d)
    (‚Č§‚Üí+‚Ąē-‚ął-split m‚Č§n)
    (divides‚Āļ-+‚Ąē dm dnm)

-- ¬ß The ‚Ąē-view of sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ is the expected successor.
‚Āļto‚Ąē-sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí ‚Āļto‚Ąē (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ n) ‚Č° suc n
‚Āļto‚Ąē-sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ zero = refl
‚Āļto‚Ąē-sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ (suc n) = cong suc (‚Āļto‚Ąē-sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ n)

-- ¬ß The successor-coded positive natural divides its own ‚Ąē-view.
divides‚Āļ-suc-self : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Divides‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ n) (suc n)
divides‚Āļ-suc-self n =
  subst
    (Divides‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ n))
    (‚Āļto‚Ąē-sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ n)
    (divides‚Āļ-self (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ n))

-- ¬ß Zero/zero is a stable fixed point of gcd-fuel.
gcd-fuel-zero-zero : (f : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí gcd-fuel f zero zero ‚Č° zero
gcd-fuel-zero-zero zero = refl
gcd-fuel-zero-zero (suc f) = refl

-- ¬ß Positive fuel with a zero left input returns the right input.
gcd-fuel-zero-left-suc : (f n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí gcd-fuel (suc f) zero (suc n) ‚Č° suc n
gcd-fuel-zero-left-suc f n = refl

-- ¬ß Positive fuel with a zero right input returns the left input.
gcd-fuel-zero-right-suc : (f m : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí gcd-fuel (suc f) (suc m) zero ‚Č° suc m
gcd-fuel-zero-right-suc f m = refl

-- ¬ß One Euclidean subtraction step preserves the total positive mass in the true branch.
euclid-sum-true : {m n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí m ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (suc m) +‚Ąē (n ‚ął m) ‚Č° suc n
euclid-sum-true p = cong suc (‚Č§‚Üí+‚Ąē-‚ął-split p)
