-- ¬ß One Euclidean subtraction step preserves the total positive mass in the false branch.
euclid-sum-false : {m n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí n ‚Č§ m ‚Üí (m ‚ął n) +‚Ąē (suc n) ‚Č° suc m
euclid-sum-false {m} {n} p =
  trans
    (+‚Ąē-comm (m ‚ął n) (suc n))
    (cong suc (‚Č§‚Üí+‚Ąē-‚ął-split p))
\end{code}

\subsection{GCD engine: common-divisor preservation, output divisibility, and positivity}

To use the Euclidean routine in the reduction pipeline, three facts
are needed. First, any common positive divisor of the inputs remains a
divisor of the output. Second, whenever the output is positive, it
divides each input. Third, if at least one input is positive, then the
output is positive as well.

Together these statements show that the recursive routine behaves as
the usual greatest common divisor for the purposes needed later: it
produces a positive common divisor of the inputs, and every other
common divisor factors through it.

The proofs follow the recursive subtraction steps and transport the
necessary divisibility data through them.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Every natural is bounded by its own right extension.
‚Č§-self+‚Ąē : (m n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí m ‚Č§ (m +‚Ąē n)
‚Č§-self+‚Ąē zero n = z‚Č§n
‚Č§-self+‚Ąē (suc m) n = s‚Č§s (‚Č§-self+‚Ąē m n)

mutual
  -- ¬ß One recursive Euclidean step preserves any common positive divisor.
  gcd-fuel-common-divisor-step :
    {d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ} ‚Üí
    (f m n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí
    (b : Bool) ‚Üí
    m ‚Č§‚Ąē n ‚Č° b ‚Üí
    Divides‚Āļ d (suc m) ‚Üí
    Divides‚Āļ d (suc n) ‚Üí
    Divides‚Āļ d (gcd-fuel-step f (suc m) (suc n) b)
  gcd-fuel-common-divisor-step f m n true eq dm dn =
    gcd-fuel-common-divisor f (suc m) (n ‚ął m) dm
      (divides‚Āļ-‚ął-‚Č§ (s‚Č§s (‚Č§‚Ąē-true‚Üí‚Č§ eq)) dm dn)
  gcd-fuel-common-divisor-step f m n false eq dm dn =
    gcd-fuel-common-divisor f (m ‚ął n) (suc n)
      (divides‚Āļ-‚ął-‚Č§ (s‚Č§s (‚Č§‚Ąē-false‚Üí‚Č§ eq)) dn dm)
      dn

  -- ¬ß Any common positive divisor survives every Euclidean gcd-fuel step.
  gcd-fuel-common-divisor :
    {d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ} ‚Üí
    (f m n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí
    Divides‚Āļ d m ‚Üí
    Divides‚Āļ d n ‚Üí
    Divides‚Āļ d (gcd-fuel f m n)
  gcd-fuel-common-divisor zero m n dm dn = dm
  gcd-fuel-common-divisor (suc f) zero zero dm dn = divides‚Āļ-zero _
  gcd-fuel-common-divisor (suc f) zero (suc n) dm dn = dn
  gcd-fuel-common-divisor (suc f) (suc m) zero dm dn = dm
  gcd-fuel-common-divisor {d} (suc f) (suc m) (suc n) dm dn =
    subst
      (Divides‚Āļ d)
      (sym (gcd-fuel-suc-suc f m n))
      (gcd-fuel-common-divisor-step f m n (m ‚Č§‚Ąē n) refl dm dn)

    -- ¬ß Phase 2: Positive gcd outputs divide both original inputs

-- ¬ß Under sufficient fuel, every positive gcd-fuel output divides both inputs.
gcd-fuel-positive-output-divides‚Č§ :
  (fuel m n g : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí
  ((m +‚Ąē n) ‚Č§ fuel) ‚Üí
  (gcd-fuel fuel m n ‚Č° suc g) ‚Üí
  ((Divides‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g) m) √ó (Divides‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g) n))
gcd-fuel-positive-output-divides‚Č§ fuel zero zero g enough eq with trans (sym (gcd-fuel-zero-zero fuel)) eq
... | ()
gcd-fuel-positive-output-divides‚Č§ zero zero (suc n) g () eq
gcd-fuel-positive-output-divides‚Č§ (suc fuel) zero (suc n) g enough eq =
  divides‚Āļ-zero (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g) ,
  subst
    (Divides‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g))
    (sym eq')
    (divides‚Āļ-suc-self g)
  where
    eq' : suc n ‚Č° suc g
    eq' = trans (sym (gcd-fuel-zero-left-suc fuel n)) eq
gcd-fuel-positive-output-divides‚Č§ zero (suc m) zero g () eq
gcd-fuel-positive-output-divides‚Č§ (suc fuel) (suc m) zero g enough eq =
  subst
    (Divides‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g))
    (sym eq')
    (divides‚Āļ-suc-self g)
  , divides‚Āļ-zero (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g)
  where
    eq' : suc m ‚Č° suc g
    eq' = trans (sym (gcd-fuel-zero-right-suc fuel m)) eq
gcd-fuel-positive-output-divides‚Č§ (suc fuel) (suc m) (suc n) g (s‚Č§s enough') eq with inspect (m ‚Č§‚Ąē n)
... | reveal true bEq =
  let
    p : m ‚Č§ n
    p = ‚Č§‚Ąē-true‚Üí‚Č§ bEq

    sucn‚Č§sum : suc n ‚Č§ (m +‚Ąē suc n)
    sucn‚Č§sum = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí suc n ‚Č§ t) (+‚Ąē-comm (suc n) m) (‚Č§-self+‚Ąē (suc n) m)

    subEnough : ((suc m) +‚Ąē (n ‚ął m)) ‚Č§ fuel
    subEnough = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ fuel) (sym (euclid-sum-true p)) (‚Č§-trans sucn‚Č§sum enough')

    stepEq‚āÄ : gcd-fuel-step fuel (suc m) (suc n) (m ‚Č§‚Ąē n) ‚Č° suc g
    stepEq‚āÄ = trans (sym (gcd-fuel-suc-suc fuel m n)) eq

    stepEq : gcd-fuel fuel (suc m) (n ‚ął m) ‚Č° suc g
    stepEq = trans (sym (cong (gcd-fuel-step fuel (suc m) (suc n)) bEq)) stepEq‚āÄ

    rec : Divides‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g) (suc m) √ó Divides‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g) (n ‚ął m)
    rec = gcd-fuel-positive-output-divides‚Č§ fuel (suc m) (n ‚ął m) g subEnough stepEq

    dm : Divides‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g) (suc m)
    dm = fst rec

    dn-rest : Divides‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g) (n ‚ął m)
    dn-rest = snd rec
  in
  dm , divides‚Āļ-restore-‚ął (s‚Č§s p) dm dn-rest
... | reveal false bEq =
  let
    p : n ‚Č§ m
    p = ‚Č§‚Ąē-false‚Üí‚Č§ bEq

    sucm‚Č§sum : suc m ‚Č§ (m +‚Ąē suc n)
    sucm‚Č§sum = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí suc m ‚Č§ t) (sym (+‚Ąē-suc-right m n)) (‚Č§-self+‚Ąē (suc m) n)

    subEnough : ((m ‚ął n) +‚Ąē (suc n)) ‚Č§ fuel
    subEnough = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ fuel) (sym (euclid-sum-false p)) (‚Č§-trans sucm‚Č§sum enough')

    stepEq‚āÄ : gcd-fuel-step fuel (suc m) (suc n) (m ‚Č§‚Ąē n) ‚Č° suc g
    stepEq‚āÄ = trans (sym (gcd-fuel-suc-suc fuel m n)) eq

    stepEq : gcd-fuel fuel (m ‚ął n) (suc n) ‚Č° suc g
    stepEq = trans (sym (cong (gcd-fuel-step fuel (suc m) (suc n)) bEq)) stepEq‚āÄ

    rec : Divides‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g) (m ‚ął n) √ó Divides‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g) (suc n)
    rec = gcd-fuel-positive-output-divides‚Č§ fuel (m ‚ął n) (suc n) g subEnough stepEq

    dm-rest : Divides‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g) (m ‚ął n)
    dm-rest = fst rec

    dn : Divides‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g) (suc n)
    dn = snd rec
  in
  divides‚Āļ-restore-‚ął (s‚Č§s p) dn dm-rest , dn
\end{code}

The inductive core of the gcd engine is established: for any fuel level sufficient to reach termination, the positive output divides both inputs. The remaining step generalises this to the canonical fuel formula $\texttt{slack} + (m + n)$, which always provides enough iterations. This is the interface consumed by the rational reduction machinery above.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Canonical-or-greater fuel is enough to turn any positive output into a common divisor.
gcd-fuel-positive-output-divides :
  (slack m n g : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí
  (gcd-fuel (slack +‚Ąē (m +‚Ąē n)) m n ‚Č° suc g) ‚Üí
  ((Divides‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g) m) √ó (Divides‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g) n))
gcd-fuel-positive-output-divides slack m n g eq =
  gcd-fuel-positive-output-divides‚Č§
    (slack +‚Ąē (m +‚Ąē n))
    m
    n
    g
    (subst
      (őĽ t ‚Üí (m +‚Ąē n) ‚Č§ t)
      (+‚Ąē-comm (m +‚Ąē n) slack)
      (‚Č§-self+‚Ąē (m +‚Ąē n) slack))
    eq

  -- ¬ß Phase 3: Positive total mass forces a positive gcd

-- ¬ß Any common positive divisor of m and n divides gcd m n.
common-divisor-divides-gcd :
  {d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ} ‚Üí
  (m n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí
  Divides‚Āļ d m ‚Üí
  Divides‚Āļ d n ‚Üí
  Divides‚Āļ d (gcd m n)
common-divisor-divides-gcd {d} m n dm dn =
  subst
    (Divides‚Āļ d)
    (sym (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí gcd-fuel t m n) (+-to-+‚Ąē m n)))
    (gcd-fuel-common-divisor (m +‚Ąē n) m n dm dn)

-- ¬ß Under sufficient fuel, a positive total input mass forces a positive gcd-fuel output.
gcd-fuel-positive-sum‚Č§ :
  (fuel m n s : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí
  (m +‚Ąē n ‚Č° suc s) ‚Üí
  ((m +‚Ąē n) ‚Č§ fuel) ‚Üí
  ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ g ‚Üí gcd-fuel fuel m n ‚Č° suc g)
gcd-fuel-positive-sum‚Č§ zero zero zero s () enough
gcd-fuel-positive-sum‚Č§ zero zero (suc n) s sumEq ()
gcd-fuel-positive-sum‚Č§ zero (suc m) zero s sumEq ()
gcd-fuel-positive-sum‚Č§ zero (suc m) (suc n) s sumEq ()
gcd-fuel-positive-sum‚Č§ (suc fuel) zero zero s () enough
gcd-fuel-positive-sum‚Č§ (suc fuel) zero (suc n) s sumEq enough = n , gcd-fuel-zero-left-suc fuel n
gcd-fuel-positive-sum‚Č§ (suc fuel) (suc m) zero s sumEq enough = m , gcd-fuel-zero-right-suc fuel m
gcd-fuel-positive-sum‚Č§ (suc fuel) (suc m) (suc n) s sumEq (s‚Č§s enough') with inspect (m ‚Č§‚Ąē n)
... | reveal true bEq =
  let
    p : m ‚Č§ n
    p = ‚Č§‚Ąē-true‚Üí‚Č§ bEq

    sucn‚Č§sum : suc n ‚Č§ (m +‚Ąē suc n)
    sucn‚Č§sum = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí suc n ‚Č§ t) (+‚Ąē-comm (suc n) m) (‚Č§-self+‚Ąē (suc n) m)

    subEnough : ((suc m) +‚Ąē (n ‚ął m)) ‚Č§ fuel
    subEnough = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ fuel) (sym (euclid-sum-true p)) (‚Č§-trans sucn‚Č§sum enough')

    subSum : (suc m) +‚Ąē (n ‚ął m) ‚Č° suc n
    subSum = euclid-sum-true p

    stepEq‚āÄ : gcd-fuel-step fuel (suc m) (suc n) (m ‚Č§‚Ąē n) ‚Č° gcd-fuel fuel (suc m) (n ‚ął m)
    stepEq‚āÄ = cong (gcd-fuel-step fuel (suc m) (suc n)) bEq

    rec : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ g ‚Üí gcd-fuel fuel (suc m) (n ‚ął m) ‚Č° suc g)
    rec = gcd-fuel-positive-sum‚Č§ fuel (suc m) (n ‚ął m) n subSum subEnough
  in
  fst rec , trans (gcd-fuel-suc-suc fuel m n) (trans stepEq‚āÄ (snd rec))
... | reveal false bEq =
  let
    p : n ‚Č§ m
    p = ‚Č§‚Ąē-false‚Üí‚Č§ bEq

    sucm‚Č§sum : suc m ‚Č§ (m +‚Ąē suc n)
    sucm‚Č§sum = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí suc m ‚Č§ t) (sym (+‚Ąē-suc-right m n)) (‚Č§-self+‚Ąē (suc m) n)

    subEnough : ((m ‚ął n) +‚Ąē (suc n)) ‚Č§ fuel
    subEnough = subst (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ fuel) (sym (euclid-sum-false p)) (‚Č§-trans sucm‚Č§sum enough')

    subSum : (m ‚ął n) +‚Ąē (suc n) ‚Č° suc m
    subSum = euclid-sum-false p

    stepEq‚āÄ : gcd-fuel-step fuel (suc m) (suc n) (m ‚Č§‚Ąē n) ‚Č° gcd-fuel fuel (m ‚ął n) (suc n)
    stepEq‚āÄ = cong (gcd-fuel-step fuel (suc m) (suc n)) bEq

    rec : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ g ‚Üí gcd-fuel fuel (m ‚ął n) (suc n) ‚Č° suc g)
    rec = gcd-fuel-positive-sum‚Č§ fuel (m ‚ął n) (suc n) m subSum subEnough
  in
  fst rec , trans (gcd-fuel-suc-suc fuel m n) (trans stepEq‚āÄ (snd rec))

-- ¬ß The canonical gcd of a positive denominator is itself positive.
gcd-positive-right : (m : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí (d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ g ‚Üí gcd m (‚Āļto‚Ąē d) ‚Č° suc g)
gcd-positive-right m d =
  let
    pack : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ g ‚Üí gcd-fuel (m +‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d) m (‚Āļto‚Ąē d) ‚Č° suc g)
    pack =
      gcd-fuel-positive-sum‚Č§
        (m +‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d)
        m
        (‚Āļto‚Ąē d)
        (m +‚Ąē pred d)
        (+‚Ąē-suc-right m (pred d))
        (‚Č§-refl (m +‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē d))
  in
  fst pack ,
    trans
      (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí gcd-fuel t m (‚Āļto‚Ąē d)) (+-to-+‚Ąē m (‚Āļto‚Ąē d)))
      (snd pack)
\end{code}

\subsection{Canonical reduction candidate and divisibility}

The gcd engine and the divisibility theory are now available. For a
rational $q = n/d$, the natural reduction candidate is obtained by
computing $g = \gcd(|n|, d)$ and dividing both visible fields by $g$.

Three basic facts are needed. The divisor $g$ is positive because the
denominator is positive, its $\mathbb{N}$-view coincides with the
computed gcd, and it divides both the numerator magnitude and the
denominator. With these bookkeeping lemmas in place, the candidate can
be defined directly from the quotient data.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß The successor-coded positive natural is definitionally mk‚Ąē‚Āļ.
sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ-mk‚Ąē‚Āļ : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ n ‚Č° mk‚Ąē‚Āļ n
sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ-mk‚Ąē‚Āļ zero = refl
sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ-mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (suc n) = cong suc‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ-mk‚Ąē‚Āļ n)

-- ¬ß The gcd used by the canonical reduction candidate.
canonical-reduction-divisor : ‚Ąö ‚Üí ‚Ąē‚Āļ
canonical-reduction-divisor q = mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (gcd (mag‚Ą§ (num q)) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q)) ‚ął 1)

-- ¬ß Phase 2: Divide both fields by the forced gcd

-- ¬ß Candidate reduced numerator obtained by dividing out the computed gcd.
canonical-reduction-num : ‚Ąö ‚Üí ‚Ąē
canonical-reduction-num q = mag‚Ą§ (num q) div canonical-reduction-divisor q

-- ¬ß Candidate reduced denominator obtained by dividing out the computed gcd.
canonical-reduction-den : ‚Ąö ‚Üí ‚Ąē‚Āļ
canonical-reduction-den q = mk‚Ąē‚Āļ ((‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q) div canonical-reduction-divisor q) ‚ął 1)

-- ¬ß Package the raw gcd/division reduction candidate.
record CanonicalReductionCandidate (q : ‚Ąö) : Set where
  field
    cand-divisor : ‚Ąē‚Āļ
    cand-num : ‚Ąē
    cand-den : ‚Ąē‚Āļ

canonical-reduction-candidate : (q : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí CanonicalReductionCandidate q
canonical-reduction-candidate q = record
  { cand-divisor = canonical-reduction-divisor q
  ; cand-num = canonical-reduction-num q
  ; cand-den = canonical-reduction-den q
  }

-- ¬ß The canonical reduction divisor is the positive gcd itself.
canonical-reduction-divisor-positive :
  (q : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí
  ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ g ‚Üí canonical-reduction-divisor q ‚Č° sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g)
canonical-reduction-divisor-positive q =
  let
    gPack : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ g ‚Üí gcd (mag‚Ą§ (num q)) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q)) ‚Č° suc g)
    gPack = gcd-positive-right (mag‚Ą§ (num q)) (den q)

    g : ‚Ąē
    g = fst gPack

    gcdEq : gcd (mag‚Ą§ (num q)) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q)) ‚Č° suc g
    gcdEq = snd gPack
  in
  g ,
    trans
      (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (t ‚ął 1)) gcdEq)
      (trans
        (cong mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (‚ął-one-suc g))
        (sym (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ-mk‚Ąē‚Āļ g)))

-- ¬ß The ‚Ąē-view of the canonical reduction divisor is the computed gcd.
canonical-reduction-divisor-value :
  (q : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí
  ‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-divisor q) ‚Č° gcd (mag‚Ą§ (num q)) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q))
canonical-reduction-divisor-value q with gcd-positive-right (mag‚Ą§ (num q)) (den q)
... | g , gcdEq =
  let
    divEq : canonical-reduction-divisor q ‚Č° sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g
    divEq =
      trans
        (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (t ‚ął 1)) gcdEq)
        (trans
          (cong mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (‚ął-one-suc g))
          (sym (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ-mk‚Ąē‚Āļ g)))
  in
  trans
    (cong ‚Āļto‚Ąē divEq)
    (trans
      (‚Āļto‚Ąē-sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g)
      (sym gcdEq))

-- ¬ß The canonical reduction divisor divides the visible numerator magnitude.
canonical-reduction-divides-num :
  (q : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí
  Divides‚Āļ (canonical-reduction-divisor q) (mag‚Ą§ (num q))
canonical-reduction-divides-num q with gcd-positive-right (mag‚Ą§ (num q)) (den q)
... | g , gcdEq =
  let
    divEq : canonical-reduction-divisor q ‚Č° sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g
    divEq =
      trans
        (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (t ‚ął 1)) gcdEq)
        (trans
          (cong mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (‚ął-one-suc g))
          (sym (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ-mk‚Ąē‚Āļ g)))

    gcdEq+‚Ąē : gcd-fuel (mag‚Ą§ (num q) +‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q)) (mag‚Ą§ (num q)) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q)) ‚Č° suc g
    gcdEq+‚Ąē =
      trans
        (sym (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí gcd-fuel t (mag‚Ą§ (num q)) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q))) (+-to-+‚Ąē (mag‚Ą§ (num q)) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q)))))
        gcdEq
  in
  subst
    (őĽ t ‚Üí Divides‚Āļ t (mag‚Ą§ (num q)))
    (sym divEq)
    (fst
      (gcd-fuel-positive-output-divides
        zero
        (mag‚Ą§ (num q))
        (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q))
        g
        gcdEq+‚Ąē))

-- ¬ß The canonical reduction divisor divides the visible denominator.
canonical-reduction-divides-den :
  (q : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí
  Divides‚Āļ (canonical-reduction-divisor q) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q))
canonical-reduction-divides-den q with gcd-positive-right (mag‚Ą§ (num q)) (den q)
... | g , gcdEq =
  let
    divEq : canonical-reduction-divisor q ‚Č° sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g
    divEq =
      trans
        (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (t ‚ął 1)) gcdEq)
        (trans
          (cong mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (‚ął-one-suc g))
          (sym (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ-mk‚Ąē‚Āļ g)))

    gcdEq+‚Ąē : gcd-fuel (mag‚Ą§ (num q) +‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q)) (mag‚Ą§ (num q)) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q)) ‚Č° suc g
    gcdEq+‚Ąē =
      trans
        (sym (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí gcd-fuel t (mag‚Ą§ (num q)) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q))) (+-to-+‚Ąē (mag‚Ą§ (num q)) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q)))))
        gcdEq
  in
  subst
    (őĽ t ‚Üí Divides‚Āļ t (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q)))
    (sym divEq)
    (snd
      (gcd-fuel-positive-output-divides
        zero
        (mag‚Ą§ (num q))
        (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q))
        g
        gcdEq+‚Ąē))
\end{code}

\subsection{Factorization, coprimality, and witness stabilization}

The next step is to show that this candidate is genuinely reduced.
Because the gcd divides both fields, exact division yields
factorizations of the original numerator magnitude and denominator
through the candidate and the divisor.

Coprimality is then obtained by showing that any common divisor of the
candidate pair would induce a larger common divisor of the original
pair, contrary to maximality of the gcd. If the input is already
irreducible, the divisor is $1$, so the candidate agrees with the
original data. This yields the stabilization and idempotence results
needed later.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß The candidate numerator factorization follows from exact division.
canonical-reduction-num-factor :
  (q : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí
  mag‚Ą§ (num q)
  ‚Č° canonical-reduction-num q *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-divisor q)
canonical-reduction-num-factor q =
  let
    dvd : Divides‚Āļ (canonical-reduction-divisor q) (mag‚Ą§ (num q))
    dvd = canonical-reduction-divides-num q
  in
  trans
    (Divides‚Āļ.factor‚Āļ dvd)
    (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-divisor q)) (sym (divides‚Āļ‚Üídiv dvd)))

-- ¬ß The candidate denominator factorization follows from exact division.
canonical-reduction-den-factor :
  (q : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí
  ‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q)
  ‚Č° ‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-den q) *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-divisor q)
canonical-reduction-den-factor q =
  let
    dvd : Divides‚Āļ (canonical-reduction-divisor q) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q))
    dvd = canonical-reduction-divides-den q

    qPos : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ k ‚Üí quotient‚Āļ dvd ‚Č° suc k)
    qPos = divides‚Āļ-positive-quotient dvd

    k : ‚Ąē
    k = fst qPos

    qEq : quotient‚Āļ dvd ‚Č° suc k
    qEq = snd qPos

    denQuotEq : ‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-den q) ‚Č° quotient‚Āļ dvd
    denQuotEq =
      let
        denEq : canonical-reduction-den q ‚Č° sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ k
        denEq =
          trans
            (cong mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚ął 1) (trans (divides‚Āļ‚Üídiv dvd) qEq)))
            (trans
              (cong mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (‚ął-one-suc k))
              (sym (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ-mk‚Ąē‚Āļ k)))
      in
      trans
        (cong ‚Āļto‚Ąē denEq)
        (trans
          (‚Āļto‚Ąē-sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ k)
          (sym qEq))
  in
  trans
    (Divides‚Āļ.factor‚Āļ dvd)
    (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-divisor q)) (sym denQuotEq))

-- ¬ß Any common positive divisor of the canonical reduced pair is forced to be one.
canonical-reduction-common-divisor-one :
  (q : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí
  {e : ‚Ąē‚Āļ} ‚Üí
  Divides‚Āļ e (canonical-reduction-num q) ‚Üí
  Divides‚Āļ e (‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-den q)) ‚Üí
  e ‚Č° one‚Āļ
canonical-reduction-common-divisor-one q {e} dnum dden =
  let
    liftedNum : Divides‚Āļ (e *‚Āļ canonical-reduction-divisor q) (mag‚Ą§ (num q))
    liftedNum =
      subst
        (Divides‚Āļ (e *‚Āļ canonical-reduction-divisor q))
          (sym (canonical-reduction-num-factor q))
        (divides‚Āļ-mul-right dnum (canonical-reduction-divisor q))

    liftedDen : Divides‚Āļ (e *‚Āļ canonical-reduction-divisor q) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q))
    liftedDen =
      subst
        (Divides‚Āļ (e *‚Āļ canonical-reduction-divisor q))
          (sym (canonical-reduction-den-factor q))
        (divides‚Āļ-mul-right dden (canonical-reduction-divisor q))

    gcdDiv : Divides‚Āļ (e *‚Āļ canonical-reduction-divisor q) (gcd (mag‚Ą§ (num q)) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q)))
    gcdDiv = common-divisor-divides-gcd (mag‚Ą§ (num q)) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q)) liftedNum liftedDen

    divisorDiv : Divides‚Āļ (e *‚Āļ canonical-reduction-divisor q) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-divisor q))
    divisorDiv =
      subst
        (Divides‚Āļ (e *‚Āļ canonical-reduction-divisor q))
          (sym (canonical-reduction-divisor-value q))
        gcdDiv
  in
  divides‚Āļ-product-factor-left-one e (canonical-reduction-divisor q) divisorDiv

-- ¬ß Phase 2: Eliminate every leftover common factor

-- ¬ß The canonical reduced numerator and denominator are coprime.
canonical-reduction-candidate-coprime :
  (q : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí
  gcd (canonical-reduction-num q) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-den q)) ‚Č° 1
canonical-reduction-candidate-coprime q with gcd-positive-right (canonical-reduction-num q) (canonical-reduction-den q)
... | g , gcdEq =
  let
    gcdEq+‚Ąē : gcd-fuel (canonical-reduction-num q +‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-den q)) (canonical-reduction-num q) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-den q)) ‚Č° suc g
    gcdEq+‚Ąē =
      trans
        (sym (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí gcd-fuel t (canonical-reduction-num q) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-den q))) (+-to-+‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-num q) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-den q)))))
        gcdEq

    divisorPair : Divides‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g) (canonical-reduction-num q) √ó Divides‚Āļ (sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-den q))
    divisorPair =
      gcd-fuel-positive-output-divides
        zero
        (canonical-reduction-num q)
        (‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-den q))
        g
        gcdEq+‚Ąē

    oneEq : sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g ‚Č° one‚Āļ
    oneEq = canonical-reduction-common-divisor-one q (fst divisorPair) (snd divisorPair)
  in
  trans
    gcdEq
    (trans
      (sym (‚Āļto‚Ąē-sucTo‚Ąē‚Āļ g))
      (cong ‚Āļto‚Ąē oneEq))

-- ¬ß A reduced witness fixes the visible quotient fields of q.
reduced-witness-fields :
  {q : ‚Ąö} ‚Üí
  (r : ReducedRationalWitness q) ‚Üí
  num q ‚Č° from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r)
  √ó den q ‚Č° ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r
reduced-witness-fields {n / d} r =
  rational-fields-unique (n / d)
    n
    (from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r))
    d
    (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r)
    refl
    (ReducedRationalWitness.red-value r)
\end{code}

The preceding lemmas identify the canonical divisor with $1$ in the
reduced case and relate the fields of a reduced witness to the
original quotient data. These bookkeeping results prepare the
irreducibility and stabilization statements that follow.

A reduced witness can now be turned into an irreducibility certificate
for the represented rational.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Every reduced witness certifies irreducibility of the represented quotient.
reduced-witness-irred : {q : ‚Ąö} ‚Üí (r : ReducedRationalWitness q) ‚Üí IrreducibleQuotient q
reduced-witness-irred {q} r =
  let
    numEq = fst (reduced-witness-fields r)
    denEq = snd (reduced-witness-fields r)
    magEq : mag‚Ą§ (num q) ‚Č° ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r
    magEq = trans (cong mag‚Ą§ numEq) (mag‚Ą§-from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r))

    denNatEq : ‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q) ‚Č° ‚Āļto‚Ąē (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r)
    denNatEq = cong ‚Āļto‚Ąē denEq

    gcdEq : gcd (mag‚Ą§ (num q)) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q)) ‚Č° gcd (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r))
    gcdEq =
      trans
        (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí gcd t (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q))) magEq)
        (cong (gcd (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r)) denNatEq)
  in
  trans gcdEq (ReducedRationalWitness.red-coprime r)

-- ¬ß On a reduced witness, the canonical gcd collapses to one.
canonical-reduction-divisor-from-reduced :
  {q : ‚Ąö} ‚Üí
  (r : ReducedRationalWitness q) ‚Üí
  canonical-reduction-divisor q ‚Č° one‚Āļ
canonical-reduction-divisor-from-reduced r =
  trans
    (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (t ‚ął 1)) (reduced-witness-irred r))
    refl

-- ¬ß Phase 3: Stabilization on already reduced input

-- ¬ß The canonical candidate numerator stabilizes on any reduced witness.
canonical-reduction-num-from-reduced :
  {q : ‚Ąö} ‚Üí
  (r : ReducedRationalWitness q) ‚Üí
  canonical-reduction-num q ‚Č° ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r
canonical-reduction-num-from-reduced {q} r =
  let
    numEq = fst (reduced-witness-fields r)
    magEq : mag‚Ą§ (num q) ‚Č° ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r
    magEq = trans (cong mag‚Ą§ numEq) (mag‚Ą§-from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r))

    divEq :
      mag‚Ą§ (num q) div canonical-reduction-divisor q
      ‚Č° mag‚Ą§ (num q) div one‚Āļ
    divEq = cong (őĽ t ‚Üí mag‚Ą§ (num q) div t) (canonical-reduction-divisor-from-reduced r)
  in
  trans divEq (trans (div-one‚Āļ (mag‚Ą§ (num q))) magEq)

-- ¬ß The canonical candidate denominator stabilizes on any reduced witness.
canonical-reduction-den-from-reduced :
  {q : ‚Ąö} ‚Üí
  (r : ReducedRationalWitness q) ‚Üí
  canonical-reduction-den q ‚Č° ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r
canonical-reduction-den-from-reduced {q} r =
  let
    denEq = snd (reduced-witness-fields r)
    denNatEq : ‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q) ‚Č° ‚Āļto‚Ąē (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r)
    denNatEq = cong ‚Āļto‚Ąē denEq

    step :
      (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q) div canonical-reduction-divisor q)
      ‚Č° ‚Āļto‚Ąē (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r)
    step =
      trans
        (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí ‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q) div t) (canonical-reduction-divisor-from-reduced r))
        (trans (div-one‚Āļ (‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q))) denNatEq)
  in
  trans
    (cong mk‚Ąē‚Āļ (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚ął 1) step))
    (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ-‚Āļto‚Ąē-minus-one (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r))

-- ¬ß The full canonical reduction candidate stabilizes on any reduced witness.
canon-red-candidate-from-reduced :
  {q : ‚Ąö} ‚Üí
  (r : ReducedRationalWitness q) ‚Üí
  CanonicalReductionCandidate.cand-divisor
    (canonical-reduction-candidate q) ‚Č° one‚Āļ
  √ó CanonicalReductionCandidate.cand-num
      (canonical-reduction-candidate q) ‚Č°
      ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r
  √ó CanonicalReductionCandidate.cand-den
      (canonical-reduction-candidate q) ‚Č°
      ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r
canon-red-candidate-from-reduced r =
  canonical-reduction-divisor-from-reduced r
  , ( canonical-reduction-num-from-reduced r
    , canonical-reduction-den-from-reduced r
    )

-- ¬ß Nonnegative rationals expose a natural numerator presentation.
nonnegative-rational-presentation :
  (q : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí
  0‚Ą§ ‚Č§‚Ą§ num q ‚Üí
  ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ n ‚Üí q ‚Č° from‚Ąē‚Ą§ n / den q)
nonnegative-rational-presentation (a / d) a‚Č•0 with 0‚Č§‚Ą§‚Üífrom‚Ąē‚Ą§ a a‚Č•0
... | n , a‚Č° = n , cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t / d) a‚Č°

-- ¬ß Irreducible nonnegative quotients already carry a global reduced witness.
irreducible-nonnegative-witness :
  (q : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí
  IrreducibleQuotient q ‚Üí
  0‚Ą§ ‚Č§‚Ą§ num q ‚Üí
  ReducedRationalWitness q
irreducible-nonnegative-witness (a / d) irred a‚Č•0 with 0‚Č§‚Ą§‚Üífrom‚Ąē‚Ą§ a a‚Č•0
... | n , a‚Č° = record
  { red-num = n
  ; red-den = d
  ; red-value = cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t / d) a‚Č°
  ; red-coprime =
      trans
        (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí gcd t (‚Āļto‚Ąē d)) (sym (trans (cong mag‚Ą§ a‚Č°) (mag‚Ą§-from‚Ąē‚Ą§ n))))
        irred
  }

-- ¬ß The canonical candidate becomes a concrete reduced witness on irreducible nonnegative input.
canonical-reduction-on-irreducible-nonnegative :
  (q : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí
  (irred : IrreducibleQuotient q) ‚Üí
  (q‚Č•0 : 0‚Ą§ ‚Č§‚Ą§ num q) ‚Üí
  CanonicalReductionCandidate.cand-divisor (canonical-reduction-candidate q) ‚Č° one‚Āļ
  √ó CanonicalReductionCandidate.cand-num (canonical-reduction-candidate q)
      ‚Č° ReducedRationalWitness.red-num (irreducible-nonnegative-witness q irred q‚Č•0)
  √ó CanonicalReductionCandidate.cand-den (canonical-reduction-candidate q)
      ‚Č° ReducedRationalWitness.red-den (irreducible-nonnegative-witness q irred q‚Č•0)
canonical-reduction-on-irreducible-nonnegative q irred q‚Č•0 =
  canon-red-candidate-from-reduced
    (irreducible-nonnegative-witness q irred q‚Č•0)
\end{code}

\subsection{Normal forms and sign handling}

Up to this point the reduction procedure works with numerator
magnitudes, so it first produces normal forms for the nonnegative
sector. The record \texttt{ReducedRationalForm} captures this
quotient-level notion: a natural numerator, a positive denominator,
equality up to $\simeq_{\mathbb{Q}}$, and coprimality.

To treat arbitrary rationals, one extends this to
\texttt{SignedReducedRationalForm}, allowing an integer numerator. The
bridge is provided by negation: it preserves rational equivalence and
leaves the magnitude of an embedded natural unchanged. A negative
rational is therefore reduced by reducing its positive counterpart and
then transporting the result back through negation.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Positive denominators are exactly natural embeddings.
‚Āļto‚Ą§-from‚Ąē‚Ą§ : (d : ‚Ąē‚Āļ) ‚Üí ‚Āļto‚Ą§ d ‚Č° from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (‚Āļto‚Ąē d)
‚Āļto‚Ą§-from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (mk‚Ąē‚Āļ p) = refl

-- ¬ß Quotient-level reduced normal form for an arbitrary rational class.
record ReducedRationalForm (q : ‚Ąö) : Set where
  field
    form-num : ‚Ąē
    form-den : ‚Ąē‚Āļ
    form-value : q ‚ČÉ‚Ąö (from‚Ąē‚Ą§ form-num / form-den)
    form-coprime : gcd form-num (‚Āļto‚Ąē form-den) ‚Č° 1

-- ¬ß Signed quotient-level reduced normal form for a genuinely arbitrary rational class.
record SignedReducedRationalForm (q : ‚Ąö) : Set where
  field
    signed-num : ‚Ą§
    signed-den : ‚Ąē‚Āļ
    signed-value : q ‚ČÉ‚Ąö (signed-num / signed-den)
    signed-coprime : gcd (mag‚Ą§ signed-num) (‚Āļto‚Ąē signed-den) ‚Č° 1

-- ¬ß Every strict reduced witness yields a quotient-level reduced form.
reduced-witness‚Üíform : {q : ‚Ąö} ‚Üí ReducedRationalWitness q ‚Üí ReducedRationalForm q
reduced-witness‚Üíform {q} r = record
  { form-num = ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r
  ; form-den = ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r
  ; form-value =
      subst
        (őĽ t ‚Üí t ‚ČÉ‚Ąö (from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r) / ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r))
      (sym (ReducedRationalWitness.red-value r))
        (‚ČÉ‚Ąö-refl (from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r) / ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r))
  ; form-coprime = ReducedRationalWitness.red-coprime r
  }

-- ¬ß Negation preserves rational equivalence.
-‚Ąö-resp-‚ČÉ : {p q : ‚Ąö} ‚Üí p ‚ČÉ‚Ąö q ‚Üí (-‚Ąö p) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö (-‚Ąö q)
-‚Ąö-resp-‚ČÉ {a / b} {c / d} eq =
  trans
    (*‚Ą§-neg-left a (‚Āļto‚Ą§ d))
    (trans
      (cong neg‚Ą§ eq)
      (sym (*‚Ą§-neg-left c (‚Āļto‚Ą§ b))))

-- ¬ß Magnitude ignores the transported sign on embedded naturals.
mag‚Ą§-neg-from‚Ąē‚Ą§ : (n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí mag‚Ą§ (neg‚Ą§ (from‚Ąē‚Ą§ n)) ‚Č° n
mag‚Ą§-neg-from‚Ąē‚Ą§ zero = refl
mag‚Ą§-neg-from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (suc n) = refl

-- ¬ß Any nonnegative quotient-level reduced form is already a signed one.
reduced-form‚Üísigned : {q : ‚Ąö} ‚Üí ReducedRationalForm q ‚Üí SignedReducedRationalForm q
reduced-form‚Üísigned {q} r = record
  { signed-num = from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (ReducedRationalForm.form-num r)
  ; signed-den = ReducedRationalForm.form-den r
  ; signed-value = ReducedRationalForm.form-value r
  ; signed-coprime =
      trans
        (cong
          (őĽ t ‚Üí gcd t (‚Āļto‚Ąē (ReducedRationalForm.form-den r)))
          (mag‚Ą§-from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (ReducedRationalForm.form-num r)))
        (ReducedRationalForm.form-coprime r)
  }
\end{code}

\subsection{Global reduction theorems}

All components are now available for the global reduction theorem.
Nonnegative rationals use the factorization and coprimality results
already established; negative rationals are handled by reduction of
the corresponding positive class and transport through negation.

The final theorem is therefore an assembly statement. The three sign
cases are combined into a single construction showing that every
rational class admits a canonical signed reduced form.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Explicit factorization data needed to make the canonical candidate sound globally.
record CanonicalReductionFactorization (q : ‚Ąö) : Set where
  field
    num-nonnegative : 0‚Ą§ ‚Č§‚Ą§ num q
    num-factor :
      mag‚Ą§ (num q)
      ‚Č° canonical-reduction-num q *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-divisor q)
    den-factor :
      ‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q)
      ‚Č° ‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-den q) *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-divisor q)
    candidate-coprime :
      gcd (canonical-reduction-num q) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-den q)) ‚Č° 1

-- ¬ß With gcd-factorization laws established, the canonical candidate is the quotient-level reduced form.
canonical-reduction-form-from-factorization :
  (q : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí
  CanonicalReductionFactorization q ‚Üí
  ReducedRationalForm q
canonical-reduction-form-from-factorization (a / dq) f = record
  { form-num = canonical-reduction-num (a / dq)
  ; form-den = canonical-reduction-den (a / dq)
  ; form-value = valueProof
  ; form-coprime = CanonicalReductionFactorization.candidate-coprime f
  }
  where
    divr : ‚Ąē‚Āļ
    divr = canonical-reduction-divisor (a / dq)

    cnum : ‚Ąē
    cnum = canonical-reduction-num (a / dq)

    cden : ‚Ąē‚Āļ
    cden = canonical-reduction-den (a / dq)

    aNatPack : ő£ ‚Ąē (őĽ n ‚Üí a ‚Č° from‚Ąē‚Ą§ n)
    aNatPack = 0‚Č§‚Ą§‚Üífrom‚Ąē‚Ą§ a (CanonicalReductionFactorization.num-nonnegative f)

    aNat : ‚Ąē
    aNat = fst aNatPack

    a‚Č° : a ‚Č° from‚Ąē‚Ą§ aNat
    a‚Č° = snd aNatPack

    numFactorEq : aNat ‚Č° cnum *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē divr
    numFactorEq =
      trans
        (sym (mag‚Ą§-from‚Ąē‚Ą§ aNat))
        (trans
          (cong mag‚Ą§ (sym a‚Č°))
          (CanonicalReductionFactorization.num-factor f))

    lhsNum : a *‚Ą§ ‚Āļto‚Ą§ cden ‚Č° (from‚Ąē‚Ą§ cnum *‚Ą§ ‚Āļto‚Ą§ divr) *‚Ą§ ‚Āļto‚Ą§ cden
    lhsNum =
      trans
        (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t *‚Ą§ ‚Āļto‚Ą§ cden) a‚Č°)
        (trans
          (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí from‚Ąē‚Ą§ t *‚Ą§ ‚Āļto‚Ą§ cden) numFactorEq)
          (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t *‚Ą§ ‚Āļto‚Ą§ cden) (sym (from‚Ąē‚Ą§-mul-‚Āļ cnum divr))))

    lhsRegroup : a *‚Ą§ ‚Āļto‚Ą§ cden ‚Č° from‚Ąē‚Ą§ cnum *‚Ą§ ((‚Āļto‚Ą§ divr) *‚Ą§ (‚Āļto‚Ą§ cden))
    lhsRegroup =
      trans lhsNum (*‚Ą§-assoc (from‚Ąē‚Ą§ cnum) (‚Āļto‚Ą§ divr) (‚Āļto‚Ą§ cden))

    rhsDen : ‚Āļto‚Ą§ dq ‚Č° (‚Āļto‚Ą§ cden) *‚Ą§ (‚Āļto‚Ą§ divr)
    rhsDen =
      trans
        (‚Āļto‚Ą§-from‚Ąē‚Ą§ dq)
        (trans
          (cong from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (CanonicalReductionFactorization.den-factor f))
          (trans
            (sym (from‚Ąē‚Ą§-mul-‚Āļ (‚Āļto‚Ąē cden) divr))
            (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí t *‚Ą§ ‚Āļto‚Ą§ divr) (‚Āļto‚Ą§-from‚Ąē‚Ą§ cden))))

    rhsRegroup : from‚Ąē‚Ą§ cnum *‚Ą§ ‚Āļto‚Ą§ dq ‚Č° from‚Ąē‚Ą§ cnum *‚Ą§ ((‚Āļto‚Ą§ cden) *‚Ą§ (‚Āļto‚Ą§ divr))
    rhsRegroup = cong (őĽ t ‚Üí from‚Ąē‚Ą§ cnum *‚Ą§ t) rhsDen

    middleComm : from‚Ąē‚Ą§ cnum *‚Ą§ ((‚Āļto‚Ą§ divr) *‚Ą§ (‚Āļto‚Ą§ cden)) ‚Č° from‚Ąē‚Ą§ cnum *‚Ą§ ((‚Āļto‚Ą§ cden) *‚Ą§ (‚Āļto‚Ą§ divr))
    middleComm = cong (őĽ t ‚Üí from‚Ąē‚Ą§ cnum *‚Ą§ t) (*‚Ą§-comm (‚Āļto‚Ą§ divr) (‚Āļto‚Ą§ cden))

    valueProof : (a / dq) ‚ČÉ‚Ąö (from‚Ąē‚Ą§ cnum / cden)
    valueProof = trans lhsRegroup (trans middleComm (sym rhsRegroup))

-- ¬ß The canonical reduction candidate carries full factorization data for nonnegative rationals.
canonical-reduction-factorization-nonnegative :
  (q : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí
  0‚Ą§ ‚Č§‚Ą§ num q ‚Üí
  CanonicalReductionFactorization q
canonical-reduction-factorization-nonnegative q q‚Č•0 = record
  { num-nonnegative = q‚Č•0
  ; num-factor = canonical-reduction-num-factor q
  ; den-factor = canonical-reduction-den-factor q
  ; candidate-coprime = canonical-reduction-candidate-coprime q
  }

-- ¬ß Every nonnegative rational class has a canonical quotient-level reduced form.
canonical-reduction-form-nonnegative :
  (q : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí
  0‚Ą§ ‚Č§‚Ą§ num q ‚Üí
  ReducedRationalForm q
canonical-reduction-form-nonnegative q q‚Č•0 =
  canonical-reduction-form-from-factorization q (canonical-reduction-factorization-nonnegative q q‚Č•0)
\end{code}

The nonnegative case is fully resolved: every nonnegative rational has a unique factorization through the canonical candidate, and that candidate yields a quotient-level reduced form. What remains is the sign barrier---negative rationals must be handled by reducing their magnitude and then transporting the result through negation.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Canonical reduction yields a signed quotient-level reduced form for every rational.
canonical-reduction-form :
  (q : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí
  SignedReducedRationalForm q
canonical-reduction-form (0‚Ą§ / d) =
  reduced-form‚Üísigned (canonical-reduction-form-nonnegative (0‚Ą§ / d) tt)
canonical-reduction-form ((+suc n) / d) =
  reduced-form‚Üísigned (canonical-reduction-form-nonnegative ((+suc n) / d) tt)
canonical-reduction-form ((-suc n) / d) = record
  { signed-num = neg‚Ą§ (from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (ReducedRationalForm.form-num negForm))
  ; signed-den = ReducedRationalForm.form-den negForm
  ; signed-value = -‚Ąö-resp-‚ČÉ (ReducedRationalForm.form-value negForm)
  ; signed-coprime =
      trans
        (cong
          (őĽ t ‚Üí gcd t (‚Āļto‚Ąē (ReducedRationalForm.form-den negForm)))
          (mag‚Ą§-neg-from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (ReducedRationalForm.form-num negForm)))
        (ReducedRationalForm.form-coprime negForm)
  }
  where
    negForm : ReducedRationalForm ((+suc n) / d)
    negForm = canonical-reduction-form-nonnegative ((+suc n) / d) tt

-- ¬ß In the irreducible nonnegative sector, the conditional factorization data is already forced.
canonical-reduction-factorization-on-irreducible-nonnegative :
  (q : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí
  (irred : IrreducibleQuotient q) ‚Üí
  (q‚Č•0 : 0‚Ą§ ‚Č§‚Ą§ num q) ‚Üí
  CanonicalReductionFactorization q
canonical-reduction-factorization-on-irreducible-nonnegative q irred q‚Č•0 = record
  { num-nonnegative = q‚Č•0
  ; num-factor = numFactor
  ; den-factor = denFactor
  ; candidate-coprime = candidateCoprime
  }
  where
    rw : ReducedRationalWitness q
    rw = irreducible-nonnegative-witness q irred q‚Č•0

    numWitnessEq : mag‚Ą§ (num q) ‚Č° ReducedRationalWitness.red-num rw
    numWitnessEq =
      trans
        (cong mag‚Ą§ (fst (reduced-witness-fields rw)))
        (mag‚Ą§-from‚Ąē‚Ą§ (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num rw))

    denWitnessEq : ‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q) ‚Č° ‚Āļto‚Ąē (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den rw)
    denWitnessEq = cong ‚Āļto‚Ąē (snd (reduced-witness-fields rw))

    divNatEq : ‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-divisor q) ‚Č° 1
    divNatEq = cong ‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-divisor-from-reduced rw)

    numCandEq : canonical-reduction-num q ‚Č° ReducedRationalWitness.red-num rw
    numCandEq = canonical-reduction-num-from-reduced rw

    denCandNatEq : ‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-den q) ‚Č° ‚Āļto‚Ąē (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den rw)
    denCandNatEq = cong ‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-den-from-reduced rw)

    numFactor : mag‚Ą§ (num q) ‚Č° canonical-reduction-num q *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-divisor q)
    numFactor =
      trans
        (trans numWitnessEq (sym numCandEq))
        (sym
          (trans
            (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí canonical-reduction-num q *‚Ąē t) divNatEq)
            (*‚Ąē-one-right (canonical-reduction-num q))))

    denFactor : ‚Āļto‚Ąē (den q) ‚Č° ‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-den q) *‚Ąē ‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-divisor q)
    denFactor =
      trans
        (trans denWitnessEq (sym denCandNatEq))
        (sym
          (trans
            (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí ‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-den q) *‚Ąē t) divNatEq)
            (*‚Ąē-one-right (‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-den q)))))

    candidateCoprime : gcd (canonical-reduction-num q) (‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-den q)) ‚Č° 1
    candidateCoprime =
      trans
        (cong (őĽ t ‚Üí gcd t (‚Āļto‚Ąē (canonical-reduction-den q))) numCandEq)
        (trans
          (cong (gcd (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num rw)) denCandNatEq)
          (ReducedRationalWitness.red-coprime rw))

-- ¬ß The canonical candidate already yields a quotient-level reduced form on irreducible nonnegative input.
canonical-reduction-form-on-irreducible-nonnegative :
  (q : ‚Ąö) ‚Üí
  (irred : IrreducibleQuotient q) ‚Üí
  (q‚Č•0 : 0‚Ą§ ‚Č§‚Ą§ num q) ‚Üí
  ReducedRationalForm q
canonical-reduction-form-on-irreducible-nonnegative q irred q‚Č•0 =
  canonical-reduction-form-from-factorization q
    (canonical-reduction-factorization-on-irreducible-nonnegative q irred q‚Č•0)
\end{code}

\subsection{Observable quotient layer}

The class-level reduction is complete: every rational carries a signed reduced form.  But the invariant chain does not touch rationals in isolation.  It touches \emph{admissible rational observables}---functions from drift states into $\mathbb{Q}$ that already survive the admissibility filter.  If quotient data were not lifted pointwise along these observables, the bridge chapter would be forced to re-derive the presentation at every call site, and the uniqueness guarantee would dissolve into ad hoc casework.

The lift is immediate.  An admissible rational observable already assigns a rational value at each drift state.  Reading off the constructor fields at each state gives the canonical pointwise presentation; the uniqueness of those fields then propagates pointwise.  Reduced witnesses are similarly lifted: a reduced observable carries a reduced witness at every state, and whenever two such witnesses exist, they agree pointwise because the class-level uniqueness theorem already forces agreement field by field.

The same information must now be available pointwise for admissible
observables. Since such an observable assigns a rational value to each
drift state, one simply reads off the quotient fields and reduced
witnesses state by state. Their uniqueness is inherited immediately
from the corresponding class-level theorems. This closes the observable
version of the quotient layer and makes the reduction machinery usable
throughout the rest of the development.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Pointwise quotient presentation for an admissible rational observable.
record RationalObservablePresentation (a : AdmissibleRationalObservable) : SetŌČ where
  field
    presented-num : DriftState ‚Üí ‚Ą§
    presented-den : DriftState ‚Üí ‚Ąē‚Āļ
    presents-observable :
      (s : DriftState) ‚Üí
      lower‚Ąö (observe a s) ‚Č° presented-num s / presented-den s

-- ¬ß Canonical pointwise presentation reads off the quotient fields directly.
canonical-rational-observable-presentation :
  (a : AdmissibleRationalObservable) ‚Üí RationalObservablePresentation a
canonical-rational-observable-presentation a = record
  { presented-num = őĽ s ‚Üí num (lower‚Ąö (observe a s))
  ; presented-den = őĽ s ‚Üí den (lower‚Ąö (observe a s))
  ; presents-observable = őĽ s ‚Üí refl
  }

-- ¬ß Pointwise quotient fields are unique for every admissible rational observable.
observable-quotient-fields-unique :
  (a : AdmissibleRationalObservable) ‚Üí
  (P‚āĀ P‚āā : RationalObservablePresentation a) ‚Üí
  (s : DriftState) ‚Üí
  RationalObservablePresentation.presented-num P‚āĀ s ‚Č° RationalObservablePresentation.presented-num P‚āā s
  √ó RationalObservablePresentation.presented-den P‚āĀ s ‚Č° RationalObservablePresentation.presented-den P‚āā s
observable-quotient-fields-unique a P‚āĀ P‚āā s =
  rational-fields-unique (lower‚Ąö (observe a s))
    (RationalObservablePresentation.presented-num P‚āĀ s)
    (RationalObservablePresentation.presented-num P‚āā s)
    (RationalObservablePresentation.presented-den P‚āĀ s)
    (RationalObservablePresentation.presented-den P‚āā s)
    (RationalObservablePresentation.presents-observable P‚āĀ s)
    (RationalObservablePresentation.presents-observable P‚āā s)

-- ¬ß Pointwise reduced witnesses on an admissible rational observable.
record ReducedRationalObservable (a : AdmissibleRationalObservable) : SetŌČ where
  field
    reduced-at : (s : DriftState) ‚Üí ReducedRationalWitness (lower‚Ąö (observe a s))

-- ¬ß Pointwise reduced witnesses are unique whenever they exist.
reduced-rational-observable-unique :
  (a : AdmissibleRationalObservable) ‚Üí
  (R‚āĀ R‚āā : ReducedRationalObservable a) ‚Üí
  (s : DriftState) ‚Üí
  ReducedRationalWitness.red-num (ReducedRationalObservable.reduced-at R‚āĀ s)
    ‚Č° ReducedRationalWitness.red-num (ReducedRationalObservable.reduced-at R‚āā s)
  √ó ReducedRationalWitness.red-den (ReducedRationalObservable.reduced-at R‚āĀ s)
    ‚Č° ReducedRationalWitness.red-den (ReducedRationalObservable.reduced-at R‚āā s)
reduced-rational-observable-unique a R‚āĀ R‚āā s =
  reduced-rational-witness-unique
    (ReducedRationalObservable.reduced-at R‚āĀ s)
    (ReducedRationalObservable.reduced-at R‚āā s)
\end{code}


%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź
\chapter{\texorpdfstring{$K_4$}{K4} Forced Constants Layer}
\label{ch:k4-unit-constants}
%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź

Once the vertex number is fixed at $V = 4$, the basic combinatorial
constants of $K_4$ are determined. This chapter computes edge counts,
face counts, spectral constants, and cycle data directly from that
value, with the resulting identities verified inside the formal
system.

It is useful to separate internal computation from external
interpretation. The results proved here concern only the formal
development: given the already established structure of $K_4$, the
stated invariants reduce to the stated numbers. Any broader
interpretation lies outside the present argument.

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Simplex invariants}
\label{sec:k4-constants:simplex-invariants}
\phantomsection\label{sec:K4-vertex-count}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

This section evaluates the basic tetrahedral invariants determined by
$V = 4$. The standard simplex formulas give the closed values $E=6$,
$F=4$, $\deg=3$, and $\chi=2$, and the same computations identify them
with the abstract simplex quantities introduced earlier.

\phantomsection\label{law:k4:simplex-degree-match}

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Vertex count of K‚āĄ (= simplex-vertices from Tier 5)
vertexCountK4 : ‚Ąē
vertexCountK4 = simplex-vertices

-- ¬ß Edge count: E = V(V‚ąí1)/2
edgeCountK4 : ‚Ąē
edgeCountK4 = (vertexCountK4 * (vertexCountK4 ‚ął 1)) div‚Ąē 2

-- ¬ß Face count: F = V(V‚ąí1)(V‚ąí2)/6
faceCountK4 : ‚Ąē
faceCountK4 = (vertexCountK4 * (vertexCountK4 ‚ął 1) * (vertexCountK4 ‚ął 2)) div‚Ąē 6

-- ¬ß Vertex degree: deg = V ‚ąí 1
degree-K4 : ‚Ąē
degree-K4 = vertexCountK4 ‚ął 1

-- ¬ß Euler characteristic: Ōá = V + F ‚ąí E
eulerChar-computed : ‚Ąē
eulerChar-computed = (vertexCountK4 + faceCountK4) ‚ął edgeCountK4

-- ¬ß Law 16B-0: E = 6
law16B-0-edges : edgeCountK4 ‚Č° 6
law16B-0-edges = refl

-- ¬ß Law 16B-1: F = 4
law16B-1-faces : faceCountK4 ‚Č° 4
law16B-1-faces = refl

-- ¬ß Law 16B-2: deg = 3
law16B-2-degree : degree-K4 ‚Č° 3
law16B-2-degree = refl

-- ¬ß Law 16B-3: Ōá = 2
law16B-3-euler : eulerChar-computed ‚Č° 2
law16B-3-euler = refl

-- ¬ß Law 16B-4: edge count matches simplex definition
law16B-4-edges-match : edgeCountK4 ‚Č° simplex-edges
law16B-4-edges-match = refl

-- ¬ß Law 16B-5: degree matches simplex definition
law16B-5-degree-match : degree-K4 ‚Č° simplex-degree
law16B-5-degree-match = refl

-- ¬ß Law 16B-6: Euler characteristic matches simplex definition
law16B-6-euler-match : eulerChar-computed ‚Č° simplex-chi
law16B-6-euler-match = refl
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Clifford and spectral constants}
\label{sec:k4-constants:clifford-spectral-constants}
\phantomsection\label{sec:fermat-prime}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The next constants are derived from the same tetrahedral data and
introduce no additional parameters. Clifford dimension, hierarchy
exponent, and the discrete evaluation data are all fixed polynomial or
exponential expressions in the previously computed invariants.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Clifford algebra dimension: 2^V
clifford-dimension : ‚Ąē
clifford-dimension = 2 ^ vertexCountK4

-- ¬ß Law 16B-7: Cl(‚ĄĚ^4) has dimension 16
law16B-7-clifford : clifford-dimension ‚Č° 16
law16B-7-clifford = refl

-- ¬ß Clifford mode count (alias)
clifford-modes : ‚Ąē
clifford-modes = clifford-dimension

-- ¬ß Fermat-like combinatorial strata
F‚āā : ‚Ąē
F‚āā = suc clifford-modes

F‚āÉ : ‚Ąē
F‚āÉ = suc (clifford-modes * clifford-modes)

-- ¬ß Discrete őļ: spectral width √ó 2
őļ-discrete : ‚Ąē
őļ-discrete = 2 * (degree-K4 + 1)

-- ¬ß Law 16B-8: őļ = 8
law16B-8-kappa : őļ-discrete ‚Č° 8
law16B-8-kappa = refl

\end{code}

The Bailey--Borwein--Plouffe identity for $\pi$ can be read here only
at the level of its integer coefficient pattern. In that pattern the
base, step, offsets, and numerators are all recovered from $K_4$
invariants: $\chi^V = 16$, $\kappa = 8$, $V-d=1$, $V=4$,
$d+\chi=5$, and $E=6$. The record below does not analyze convergence;
it identifies the BBP coefficient ledger as native to the already fixed
tetrahedral data.

\begin{code}

-- ¬ß BBP coefficient ledger as K‚āĄ data.

bbp-base : ‚Ąē
bbp-base = eulerChar-computed ^ vertexCountK4  -- Ōá^V = 2‚Āī = 16

bbp-step : ‚Ąē
bbp-step = őļ-discrete  -- őļ = 8

bbp-őī : ‚Ąē
bbp-őī = vertexCountK4 ‚ął degree-K4  -- V ‚ąí d = 1

bbp-offset‚āĀ : ‚Ąē
bbp-offset‚āĀ = bbp-őī  -- V ‚ąí d = 1

bbp-offset‚āā : ‚Ąē
bbp-offset‚āā = vertexCountK4  -- V = 4

bbp-offset‚āÉ : ‚Ąē
bbp-offset‚āÉ = degree-K4 + eulerChar-computed  -- d + Ōá = 5

bbp-offset‚āĄ : ‚Ąē
bbp-offset‚āĄ = edgeCountK4  -- E = 6

bbp-numer‚āĀ : ‚Ąē
bbp-numer‚āĀ = vertexCountK4  -- V = 4

bbp-numer‚āā : ‚Ąē
bbp-numer‚āā = eulerChar-computed  -- Ōá = 2

bbp-numer‚āÉ : ‚Ąē
bbp-numer‚āÉ = bbp-őī  -- őī = 1

bbp-numer‚āĄ : ‚Ąē
bbp-numer‚āĄ = bbp-őī  -- őī = 1

record BBP-K4-Identification : Set where
  field
    base-is-ŌáV    : bbp-base ‚Č° 16
    step-is-őļ     : bbp-step ‚Č° 8
    offset‚āĀ-is-őī  : bbp-offset‚āĀ ‚Č° 1
    offset‚āā-is-V  : bbp-offset‚āā ‚Č° 4
    offset‚āÉ-is-dŌá : bbp-offset‚āÉ ‚Č° 5
    offset‚āĄ-is-E  : bbp-offset‚āĄ ‚Č° 6
    numer‚āĀ-is-V   : bbp-numer‚āĀ ‚Č° 4
    numer‚āā-is-Ōá   : bbp-numer‚āā ‚Č° 2
    numer‚āÉ-is-őī   : bbp-numer‚āÉ ‚Č° 1
    numer‚āĄ-is-őī   : bbp-numer‚āĄ ‚Č° 1

-- ¬ß Law 16B-8a: BBP identification
law16B-8a-bbp-k4 : BBP-K4-Identification
law16B-8a-bbp-k4 = record
  { base-is-ŌáV    = refl
  ; step-is-őļ     = refl
  ; offset‚āĀ-is-őī  = refl
  ; offset‚āā-is-V  = refl
  ; offset‚āÉ-is-dŌá = refl
  ; offset‚āĄ-is-E  = refl
  ; numer‚āĀ-is-V   = refl
  ; numer‚āā-is-Ōá   = refl
  ; numer‚āÉ-is-őī   = refl
  ; numer‚āĄ-is-őī   = refl }

-- ¬ß Hierarchy exponent: V¬∑E ‚ąí Ōá
hierarchy-exponent : ‚Ąē
hierarchy-exponent = vertexCountK4 * edgeCountK4 ‚ął eulerChar-computed

-- ¬ß Law 16B-9: hierarchy exponent = 22
law16B-9-hierarchy : hierarchy-exponent ‚Č° 22
law16B-9-hierarchy = refl

-- ¬ß Simplex denominator: d ¬∑ (1 + E¬≤)
simplex-denom-K4 : ‚Ąē
simplex-denom-K4 = degree-K4 * suc (edgeCountK4 * edgeCountK4)

-- ¬ß Law 16B-10: simplex denominator = 111
law16B-10-simplex-denom : simplex-denom-K4 ‚Č° 111
law16B-10-simplex-denom = refl

-- ¬ß Edge pair count: E¬≤
EdgePairCount-early : ‚Ąē
EdgePairCount-early = edgeCountK4 * edgeCountK4

-- ¬ß Law 16B-11: E¬≤ = 36
law16B-11-edge-pairs : EdgePairCount-early ‚Č° 36
law16B-11-edge-pairs = refl

-- ¬ß Law 16B-12: F‚āā = 17
law16B-12-F2-is-17 : F‚āā ‚Č° 17
law16B-12-F2-is-17 = refl

-- ¬ß Law 16B-13: F‚āÉ = 257
law16B-13-F3-is-257 : F‚āÉ ‚Č° 257
law16B-13-F3-is-257 = refl

-- ¬ß Law 16B-14: compactification triple (5, 17, 37)
law16B-14-compactification-triple :
  (suc vertexCountK4 ‚Č° 5) √ó ((suc clifford-dimension ‚Č° 17) √ó (suc EdgePairCount-early ‚Č° 37))
law16B-14-compactification-triple = refl , (refl , refl)
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{\texorpdfstring{$K_4$}{K4} aliases and cycle counts}
\label{sec:k4-constants:aliases-cycle-counts}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The remaining definitions synchronize notation used in different parts
of the manuscript and record the cycle counts needed later. No new
structure is introduced here; the aliases and derived quantities are
read off from the same previously computed tetrahedral data.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß K‚āĄ aliases for K‚āĄ usage
K4-V K4-E K4-F K4-deg K4-chi : ‚Ąē
K4-V   = vertexCountK4
K4-E   = edgeCountK4
K4-F   = faceCountK4
K4-deg = degree-K4
K4-chi = eulerChar-computed

-- ¬ß Redundant aliases for K‚āĄ invariant fields
K‚āĄ-vertices-count K‚āĄ-edges-count K‚āĄ-degree-count K‚āĄ-triangles : ‚Ąē
K‚āĄ-vertices-count = vertexCountK4
K‚āĄ-edges-count    = edgeCountK4
K‚āĄ-degree-count   = degree-K4
K‚āĄ-triangles      = faceCountK4

-- ¬ß Simplex evaluation alias for K‚āĄ
simplex-eval-K4 : ‚Ąē
simplex-eval-K4 = simplex-eval

-- ¬ß Law 16B-15: simplex evaluation = 137
law16B-15-simplex-eval-bare : simplex-eval-K4 ‚Č° 137
law16B-15-simplex-eval-bare = refl

\end{code}

The least non-trivial named invariant in the $K_4$ ledger is
$\chi=2$. The degree is $3$, the vertex and face counts are $4$, the
edge count is $6$, and $\kappa=8$. Consequently any ratio of the form
$n/\mathcal{C}^2$ whose numerator is drawn from this invariant basis is
bounded below by $\chi/\mathcal{C}^2$, once the numerator is required
to be non-trivial.

\begin{code}

-- ¬ß Law 16B-15a: Ōá is the minimum non-trivial K‚āĄ invariant.
law-chi-below-deg : suc K4-chi ‚Č§ K4-deg
law-chi-below-deg = s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s z‚Č§n))               -- 3 ‚Č§ 3

law-chi-below-V : suc K4-chi ‚Č§ K4-V
law-chi-below-V = s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s z‚Č§n))                  -- 3 ‚Č§ 4

law-chi-below-F : suc K4-chi ‚Č§ K4-F
law-chi-below-F = s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s z‚Č§n))                  -- 3 ‚Č§ 4

law-chi-below-E : suc K4-chi ‚Č§ K4-E
law-chi-below-E = s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s z‚Č§n))                  -- 3 ‚Č§ 6

law-chi-below-őļ : suc K4-chi ‚Č§ őļ-discrete
law-chi-below-őļ = s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s z‚Č§n))                  -- 3 ‚Č§ 8

-- ¬ß Ōá is non-trivial: Ōá > 1.
law-chi-nontrivial : suc 1 ‚Č§ K4-chi
law-chi-nontrivial = s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s z‚Č§n)                     -- 2 ‚Č§ 2

-- ¬ß Combined Ōá-minimality record.
record ChiMinimality : Set where
  field
    chi-nontrivial : suc 1 ‚Č§ K4-chi
    chi-below-deg  : suc K4-chi ‚Č§ K4-deg
    chi-below-V    : suc K4-chi ‚Č§ K4-V
    chi-below-F    : suc K4-chi ‚Č§ K4-F
    chi-below-E    : suc K4-chi ‚Č§ K4-E
    chi-below-őļ    : suc K4-chi ‚Č§ őļ-discrete

law16B-15a-chi-minimality : ChiMinimality
law16B-15a-chi-minimality = record
  { chi-nontrivial = law-chi-nontrivial
  ; chi-below-deg  = law-chi-below-deg
  ; chi-below-V    = law-chi-below-V
  ; chi-below-F    = law-chi-below-F
  ; chi-below-E    = law-chi-below-E
  ; chi-below-őļ    = law-chi-below-őļ
  }

-- ¬ß Triangle and square cycle counts
count-triangles : ‚Ąē
count-triangles = simplex-vertices

count-squares : ‚Ąē
count-squares = simplex-degree

total-nontrivial-cycles : ‚Ąē
total-nontrivial-cycles = count-triangles + count-squares

-- ¬ß Law 16B-16: total non-trivial cycles = 7
law16B-16-total-cycles : total-nontrivial-cycles ‚Č° 7
law16B-16-total-cycles = refl

-- ¬ß Loop and propagation orders
triangle-loop-order : ‚Ąē
triangle-loop-order = simplex-vertices ‚ął simplex-degree  -- V ‚ąí d = 4 ‚ąí 3 = 1

square-loop-order : ‚Ąē
square-loop-order = simplex-vertices ‚ął simplex-degree + (simplex-vertices ‚ął simplex-degree)  -- 1 + 1 = 2

max-propagation-per-edge : ‚Ąē
max-propagation-per-edge = simplex-vertices ‚ął simplex-degree

-- ¬ß Law 16B-17: max propagation per edge = 1
law16B-17-max-prop : max-propagation-per-edge ‚Č° 1
law16B-17-max-prop = refl

-- ¬ß Lattice spacing at natural scale
lattice-spacing-natural : ‚Ąē
lattice-spacing-natural = simplex-vertices ‚ął simplex-degree

-- ¬ß Law 16B-18: lattice spacing = 1
law16B-18-lattice-natural : lattice-spacing-natural ‚Č° 1
law16B-18-lattice-natural = refl

-- ¬ß K‚āÖ edge count for comparison
K5-total-edges : ‚Ąē
K5-total-edges = ((vertexCountK4 + 1) * vertexCountK4) div‚Ąē 2

-- ¬ß Law 16B-19: K‚āÖ has 10 edges
law16B-19-K5-edges : K5-total-edges ‚Č° 10
law16B-19-K5-edges = refl
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Generation index exhaustion}
\label{sec:k4-constants:generation-exhaustion}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The non-zero spectrum of $L(K_4)$ carries multiplicity $d = 3$. Three eigenmodes demand three indices, and the indices cannot be imported from outside: every label that ranks them must already be a $K_4$ invariant, or the ranking smuggles in foreign content. The question is therefore not which labels to choose, but which labels survive the constraint that they live in the natural ranking interval $[1, d]$.

The atomic alphabet of $K_4$ is $\{V, E, F, d, \chi, \kappa\} = \{4, 6, 4, 3, 2, 8\}$. Of its six members, exactly two fall in $[1, d]$: $\chi = 2$ and $d = 3$. The remaining slot $\{1\}$ is realised by no atom, but by the unique atomic shortfall: $V \dot{-} d = F \dot{-} d = d \dot{-} \chi = 1$. Three independent atomic differences collapse onto the same value, and that value closes the interval.

Once the interval $[1, d]$ is closed by $(V \dot{-} d, \chi, d) = (1, 2, 3)$, no other strictly monotone triple of basis atoms in $[1, d]$ can occupy it---every alternative either repeats a value, leaves a gap, or exceeds $d$. The three eigenmode indices are therefore not chosen but forced within the basis.

This leaves a simple exhaustion argument. Among the basic invariants,
only $\chi=2$ and $d=3$ already lie in the interval $[1,d]$.
Everything larger than $d$ is excluded at once. The remaining value $1$
is supplied by the common shortfall $V\dot{-}d = F\dot{-}d = d\dot{-}\chi$.
Hence the increasing triple $(1,2,3)$ is obtained internally from the
$K_4$ ledger and no alternative strictly monotone triple of the same
kind remains.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Generation indices, named by their K‚āĄ origin
gen-index-1 : ‚Ąē
gen-index-1 = vertexCountK4 ‚ął degree-K4   -- V ‚ął d = 1

gen-index-2 : ‚Ąē
gen-index-2 = eulerChar-computed          -- Ōá = 2

gen-index-3 : ‚Ąē
gen-index-3 = degree-K4                   -- d = 3

-- ¬ß Numerical values
law16B-20-gen-index-1 : gen-index-1 ‚Č° 1
law16B-20-gen-index-1 = refl

law16B-21-gen-index-2 : gen-index-2 ‚Č° 2
law16B-21-gen-index-2 = refl

law16B-22-gen-index-3 : gen-index-3 ‚Č° 3
law16B-22-gen-index-3 = refl

-- ¬ß Strict monotonicity of the index triple
law16B-23-gen-mono-12 : suc gen-index-1 ‚Č§ gen-index-2
law16B-23-gen-mono-12 = s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s z‚Č§n)                      -- 2 ‚Č§ 2

law16B-24-gen-mono-23 : suc gen-index-2 ‚Č§ gen-index-3
law16B-24-gen-mono-23 = s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s z‚Č§n))                -- 3 ‚Č§ 3

-- ¬ß The four atoms outside [1, d] all strictly exceed d.
law16B-25-V-above-d : suc degree-K4 ‚Č§ vertexCountK4
law16B-25-V-above-d = s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s z‚Č§n)))            -- 4 ‚Č§ 4

law16B-26-F-above-d : suc degree-K4 ‚Č§ faceCountK4
law16B-26-F-above-d = s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s z‚Č§n)))            -- 4 ‚Č§ 4

law16B-27-E-above-d : suc degree-K4 ‚Č§ edgeCountK4
law16B-27-E-above-d = s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s z‚Č§n)))            -- 4 ‚Č§ 6

law16B-28-őļ-above-d : suc degree-K4 ‚Č§ őļ-discrete
law16B-28-őļ-above-d = s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s (s‚Č§s z‚Č§n)))            -- 4 ‚Č§ 8

-- ¬ß The atomic shortfall: three independent atomic differences all collapse onto 1.
law16B-29-Vd-is-1 : vertexCountK4 ‚ął degree-K4 ‚Č° 1
law16B-29-Vd-is-1 = refl

law16B-30-Fd-is-1 : faceCountK4 ‚ął degree-K4 ‚Č° 1
law16B-30-Fd-is-1 = refl

law16B-31-dŌá-is-1 : degree-K4 ‚ął eulerChar-computed ‚Č° 1
law16B-31-dŌá-is-1 = refl

-- ¬ß The exhaustion record for the three generation indices.
record GenerationExhaustion : Set where
  field
    -- indices are canonical K‚āĄ witnesses
    index-1-from-K4 : gen-index-1 ‚Č° vertexCountK4 ‚ął degree-K4
    index-2-from-K4 : gen-index-2 ‚Č° eulerChar-computed
    index-3-from-K4 : gen-index-3 ‚Č° degree-K4
    -- their numerical values
    val-1 : gen-index-1 ‚Č° 1
    val-2 : gen-index-2 ‚Č° 2
    val-3 : gen-index-3 ‚Č° 3
    -- strictly monotone enumeration of [1, d]
    mono-12 : suc gen-index-1 ‚Č§ gen-index-2
    mono-23 : suc gen-index-2 ‚Č§ gen-index-3
    -- the four atoms outside the interval
    V-above : suc degree-K4 ‚Č§ vertexCountK4
    F-above : suc degree-K4 ‚Č§ faceCountK4
    E-above : suc degree-K4 ‚Č§ edgeCountK4
    őļ-above : suc degree-K4 ‚Č§ őļ-discrete
    -- the three coincident atomic shortfalls
    Vd-is-1 : vertexCountK4 ‚ął degree-K4 ‚Č° 1
    Fd-is-1 : faceCountK4 ‚ął degree-K4 ‚Č° 1
    dŌá-is-1 : degree-K4 ‚ął eulerChar-computed ‚Č° 1

law16B-32-generation-exhaustion : GenerationExhaustion
law16B-32-generation-exhaustion = record
  { index-1-from-K4 = refl
  ; index-2-from-K4 = refl
  ; index-3-from-K4 = refl
  ; val-1   = law16B-20-gen-index-1
  ; val-2   = law16B-21-gen-index-2
  ; val-3   = law16B-22-gen-index-3
  ; mono-12 = law16B-23-gen-mono-12
  ; mono-23 = law16B-24-gen-mono-23
  ; V-above = law16B-25-V-above-d
  ; F-above = law16B-26-F-above-d
  ; E-above = law16B-27-E-above-d
  ; őļ-above = law16B-28-őļ-above-d
  ; Vd-is-1 = law16B-29-Vd-is-1
  ; Fd-is-1 = law16B-30-Fd-is-1
  ; dŌá-is-1 = law16B-31-dŌá-is-1
  }
\end{code}

Both shortfall identities reduce to the same value $1$, so the
interval is closed internally by the tetrahedral data. That is the
combinatorial conclusion needed in what follows.

This completes the combinatorial ledger needed in the next chapter. The
remaining task is to show that these constants determine a unique
representation record.

\paragraph{The computed invariants.}
At this stage the formal development has fixed, among other values,
$V = 4$, $E = 6$, the eigenvalue ratio $3{:}1$, and the graph
evaluation $\mathcal{C} = 137$. The next chapter uses these computed
quantities only as inputs to the uniqueness argument for the
representation record.

%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź
\chapter{\texorpdfstring{$K_4$}{K4} Representation Theory}
\label{ch:k4-representation-theory}
%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź

The aim of this chapter is to show that the invariant representation
of $K_4$ is unique. One field is anchored directly to the vertex count,
and the remaining fields are linked to it by explicit equations. The
result is a rigidity statement: every admissible record collapses to
the same data.

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Structural infrastructure}
\label{sec:k4-representation:structural-infrastructure}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

This section fixes the combinatorial carrier on which the
representation theory acts. The vertex set, adjacency relation, basis
states, and neighbor summation are given explicitly so that later
arguments refer to a concrete graph rather than an abstract
placeholder. The lemma \texttt{cover-necessary} records the
corresponding logical fact about the eliminator schema.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß K‚āĄ vertex type
data K4Vertex : Set where
  v‚āÄ v‚āĀ v‚āā v‚āÉ : K4Vertex
\end{code}

\phantomsection\label{lem:cover-necessary}

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Lemma: coverage is necessary for the Distinction eliminator schema.
cover-necessary :
  ¬¨ ((S‚āÄ : Set) (‚Ąď‚āÄ r‚āÄ : S‚āÄ) ‚Üí ‚Ąď‚āÄ ‚Č† r‚āÄ ‚Üí
     (P : S‚āÄ ‚Üí Set) ‚Üí P ‚Ąď‚āÄ ‚Üí P r‚āÄ ‚Üí (x : S‚āÄ) ‚Üí P x)
cover-necessary elim = elim K4Vertex v‚āÄ v‚āĀ v‚āÄ‚Č†v‚āĀ P-vertex tt tt v‚āā
  where
    v‚āÄ‚Č†v‚āĀ : v‚āÄ ‚Č† v‚āĀ
    v‚āÄ‚Č†v‚āĀ ()
    P-vertex : K4Vertex ‚Üí Set
    P-vertex v‚āÄ = ‚ä§
    P-vertex v‚āĀ = ‚ä§
    P-vertex v‚āā = ‚ä•
    P-vertex v‚āÉ = ‚ä§

-- ¬ß K‚āĄ state: assignment of ‚Ąē to each vertex
K4State : Set
K4State = K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē

-- ¬ß K‚āĄ adjacency (complete graph minus diagonal)
adjacent : K4Vertex ‚Üí K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē
adjacent v‚āÄ v‚āÄ = 0
adjacent v‚āÄ _  = 1
adjacent v‚āĀ v‚āĀ = 0
adjacent v‚āĀ _  = 1
adjacent v‚āā v‚āā = 0
adjacent v‚āā _  = 1
adjacent v‚āÉ v‚āÉ = 0
adjacent v‚āÉ _  = 1

-- ¬ß Kronecker delta on K‚āĄ
őī : K4Vertex ‚Üí K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē
őī v‚āÄ v‚āÄ = 1
őī v‚āÄ _  = 0
őī v‚āĀ v‚āĀ = 1
őī v‚āĀ _  = 0
őī v‚āā v‚āā = 1
őī v‚āā _  = 0
őī v‚āÉ v‚āÉ = 1
őī v‚āÉ _  = 0

-- ¬ß Basis states (= Kronecker delta)
K4-basis : K4Vertex ‚Üí K4State
K4-basis = őī

-- ¬ß Sum of neighbor values (graph Laplacian action)
sum-neighbors : K4State ‚Üí K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē
sum-neighbors Ōą v = adjacent v v‚āÄ * Ōą v‚āÄ + adjacent v v‚āĀ * Ōą v‚āĀ
                  + adjacent v v‚āā * Ōą v‚āā + adjacent v v‚āÉ * Ōą v‚āÉ
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Vertex-invariance laws}
\label{sec:k4-representation:vertex-invariance-laws}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

$K_4$ is vertex-transitive, so quantities defined purely from
adjacency should not depend on a chosen label. The following lemmas
verify this directly by case analysis on the four vertices.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Adjacency row sum equals vertex degree
law-adjacency-degree : (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí
  adjacent v v‚āÄ + adjacent v v‚āĀ + adjacent v v‚āā + adjacent v v‚āÉ ‚Č° degree-K4
law-adjacency-degree v‚āÄ = refl
law-adjacency-degree v‚āĀ = refl
law-adjacency-degree v‚āā = refl
law-adjacency-degree v‚āÉ = refl

-- ¬ß Each basis state is normalized (total weight 1)
law-basis-normalized : (u : K4Vertex) ‚Üí
  K4-basis u v‚āÄ + K4-basis u v‚āĀ + K4-basis u v‚āā + K4-basis u v‚āÉ ‚Č° 1
law-basis-normalized v‚āÄ = refl
law-basis-normalized v‚āĀ = refl
law-basis-normalized v‚āā = refl
law-basis-normalized v‚āÉ = refl

-- ¬ß Basis propagation equals adjacency (16 cases)
law-basis-spreads : (u v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí
  sum-neighbors (K4-basis u) v ‚Č° adjacent v u
law-basis-spreads v‚āÄ v‚āÄ = refl
law-basis-spreads v‚āÄ v‚āĀ = refl
law-basis-spreads v‚āÄ v‚āā = refl
law-basis-spreads v‚āÄ v‚āÉ = refl
law-basis-spreads v‚āĀ v‚āÄ = refl
law-basis-spreads v‚āĀ v‚āĀ = refl
law-basis-spreads v‚āĀ v‚āā = refl
law-basis-spreads v‚āĀ v‚āÉ = refl
law-basis-spreads v‚āā v‚āÄ = refl
law-basis-spreads v‚āā v‚āĀ = refl
law-basis-spreads v‚āā v‚āā = refl
law-basis-spreads v‚āā v‚āÉ = refl
law-basis-spreads v‚āÉ v‚āÄ = refl
law-basis-spreads v‚āÉ v‚āĀ = refl
law-basis-spreads v‚āÉ v‚āā = refl
law-basis-spreads v‚āÉ v‚āÉ = refl
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{The representation record}
\label{sec:k4-representation:representation-record}
\phantomsection\label{sec:spacetime-index}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The record packages the numerical quantities used later together with
the equations that connect them. Its design is triangular: the anchor
$\dim \equiv V$ fixes the first field, and the remaining constraints
determine the others successively.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß The K‚āĄ invariant representation: 17 unknowns + 1 anchor + 16 chain constraints
record K4Record : Set where
  field
    dim          : ‚Ąē     -- total simplex dimension (V)
    n-spatial    : ‚Ąē     -- codimension-1 count (V ‚ął 1)
    n-temporal   : ‚Ąē     -- residual dimension (V ‚ął n-spatial)
    edge-count   : ‚Ąē     -- edge count (E)
    face-count   : ‚Ąē     -- 2-simplex count (F)
    euler        : ‚Ąē     -- Euler characteristic (V + F ‚ął E)
    graph-eval : ‚Ąē     -- graph evaluation (V^d ¬∑ Ōá + d¬≤)
    codim        : ‚Ąē     -- codimension count (= d)
    clifford-dim   : ‚Ąē     -- Clifford dimension (2^V)
    hierarchy    : ‚Ąē     -- hierarchy exponent (V¬∑E ‚ął Ōá)
    auto-count   : ‚Ąē     -- automorphism count (|S_V|)
    vertex-euler-product : ‚Ąē     -- discrete spectral product V¬∑Ōá
    norm-index      : ‚Ąē     -- normalization index (= V)
    frac-num   : ‚Ąē     -- fraction numerator (= n-temporal)
    frac-den   : ‚Ąē     -- fraction denominator (= E)
    res-index    : ‚Ąē     -- residual atom count (V ‚ął d)
    min-loop     : ‚Ąē     -- triangle-cycle order (V ‚ął d)

    -- The anchor: exactly ONE connection to K‚āĄ
    anchor : dim ‚Č° vertexCountK4

    -- Graph structure chain (from complete graph K_dim)
    cg-deg      : n-spatial ‚Č° dim ‚ął 1
    cg-temporal : n-temporal ‚Č° dim ‚ął n-spatial
    cg-edges    : edge-count ‚Č° (dim * n-spatial) div‚Ąē 2
    cg-faces    : face-count ‚Č° dim
    cg-euler    : euler ‚Č° (dim + face-count) ‚ął edge-count

    -- Structural chain (invariant correspondences)
    ph-clifford   : clifford-dim ‚Č° 2 ^ dim
    ph-eval : graph-eval ‚Č° (dim ^ n-spatial) * euler + n-spatial * n-spatial
    ph-codim      : codim ‚Č° n-spatial
    ph-hierarchy : hierarchy ‚Č° dim * edge-count ‚ął euler
    ph-auto     : auto-count ‚Č° dim * n-spatial * (n-spatial ‚ął 1) * (n-spatial ‚ął 2)
    ph-vertex-euler     : vertex-euler-product ‚Č° dim * euler
    ph-norm       : norm-index ‚Č° dim
    ph-res       : res-index ‚Č° n-temporal
    ph-loop     : min-loop ‚Č° n-temporal
    ph-frac-num : frac-num ‚Č° n-temporal
    ph-frac-den : frac-den ‚Č° edge-count
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Existence: the canonical witness}
\label{sec:k4-representation:existence-canonical-witness}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The first task is to exhibit an inhabitant. The canonical witness
assigns to each field the value already computed in the combinatorial
chapters, and every proof field closes by \texttt{refl} because those
values already satisfy the defining equations.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß The canonical K‚āĄ invariant representation
canonical-rep : K4Record
canonical-rep = record
  { dim          = 4
  ; n-spatial    = 3
  ; n-temporal   = 1
  ; edge-count   = 6
  ; face-count   = 4
  ; euler        = 2
  ; graph-eval = 137
  ; codim        = 3
  ; clifford-dim   = 16
  ; hierarchy    = 22
  ; auto-count   = 24
  ; vertex-euler-product      = 8
  ; norm-index      = 4
  ; frac-num   = 1
  ; frac-den   = 6
  ; res-index    = 1
  ; min-loop     = 1
  ; anchor       = refl   -- 4 ‚Č° vertexCountK4
  ; cg-deg       = refl   -- 3 ‚Č° 4 ‚ął 1
  ; cg-temporal  = refl   -- 1 ‚Č° 4 ‚ął 3
  ; cg-edges     = refl   -- 6 ‚Č° (4 * 3) div‚Ąē 2
  ; cg-faces     = refl   -- 4 ‚Č° 4
  ; cg-euler     = refl   -- 2 ‚Č° (4 + 4) ‚ął 6
  ; ph-clifford    = refl   -- 16 ‚Č° 2‚Āī
  ; ph-eval  = refl   -- 137 ‚Č° 4¬≥¬∑2 + 3¬≤
  ; ph-codim       = refl   -- 3 ‚Č° 3
  ; ph-hierarchy = refl   -- 22 ‚Č° 4¬∑6 ‚ął 2
  ; ph-auto      = refl   -- 24 ‚Č° 4¬∑3¬∑2¬∑1
  ; ph-vertex-euler      = refl   -- 8 ‚Č° 4¬∑2
  ; ph-norm        = refl   -- 4 ‚Č° 4
  ; ph-res        = refl   -- 1 ‚Č° 1
  ; ph-loop      = refl   -- 1 ‚Č° 1
  ; ph-frac-num = refl  -- 1 ‚Č° 1
  ; ph-frac-den = refl  -- 6 ‚Č° 6
  }
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Determination: the forcing chain resolves every field}
\label{sec:k4-representation:determination-forcing-chain}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

To prove rigidity, one starts with an arbitrary admissible record and
evaluates the constraints outward from the anchor. The equations first
determine $\dim$, then $n\text{-spatial}$ and $n\text{-temporal}$,
and from there the remaining fields follow by substitution.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Forcing chain: every field is determined by the constraints
module ForcedValues (r : K4Record) where
  open K4Record r

  dim-is-4 : dim ‚Č° 4
  dim-is-4 = anchor

  spatial-is-3 : n-spatial ‚Č° 3
  spatial-is-3 = trans cg-deg (cong (őĽ d ‚Üí d ‚ął 1) dim-is-4)

  temporal-is-1 : n-temporal ‚Č° 1
  temporal-is-1 = trans cg-temporal
    (trans (cong (őĽ d ‚Üí d ‚ął n-spatial) dim-is-4)
           (cong (őĽ s ‚Üí 4 ‚ął s) spatial-is-3))

  edge-is-6 : edge-count ‚Č° 6
  edge-is-6 = trans cg-edges
    (trans (cong (őĽ d ‚Üí (d * n-spatial) div‚Ąē 2) dim-is-4)
           (cong (őĽ s ‚Üí (4 * s) div‚Ąē 2) spatial-is-3))

  faces-is-4 : face-count ‚Č° 4
  faces-is-4 = trans cg-faces dim-is-4

  euler-is-2 : euler ‚Č° 2
  euler-is-2 = trans cg-euler
    (trans (cong (őĽ d ‚Üí (d + face-count) ‚ął edge-count) dim-is-4)
    (trans (cong (őĽ f ‚Üí (4 + f) ‚ął edge-count) faces-is-4)
           (cong (őĽ g ‚Üí 8 ‚ął g) edge-is-6)))

  clifford-is-16 : clifford-dim ‚Č° 16
  clifford-is-16 = trans ph-clifford (cong (őĽ d ‚Üí 2 ^ d) dim-is-4)

  eval-is-137 : graph-eval ‚Č° 137
  eval-is-137 = trans ph-eval
    (trans (cong (őĽ d ‚Üí (d ^ n-spatial) * euler + n-spatial * n-spatial) dim-is-4)
    (trans (cong (őĽ s ‚Üí (4 ^ s) * euler + s * s) spatial-is-3)
           (cong (őĽ e ‚Üí (4 ^ 3) * e + 3 * 3) euler-is-2)))

  codim-is-3 : codim ‚Č° 3
  codim-is-3 = trans ph-codim spatial-is-3

  hierarchy-is-22 : hierarchy ‚Č° 22
  hierarchy-is-22 = trans ph-hierarchy
    (trans (cong (őĽ d ‚Üí d * edge-count ‚ął euler) dim-is-4)
    (trans (cong (őĽ g ‚Üí 4 * g ‚ął euler) edge-is-6)
           (cong (őĽ e ‚Üí 24 ‚ął e) euler-is-2)))

  auto-is-24 : auto-count ‚Č° 24
  auto-is-24 = trans ph-auto
    (trans (cong (őĽ d ‚Üí d * n-spatial * (n-spatial ‚ął 1) * (n-spatial ‚ął 2)) dim-is-4)
           (cong (őĽ s ‚Üí 4 * s * (s ‚ął 1) * (s ‚ął 2)) spatial-is-3))

  vertex-euler-is-8 : vertex-euler-product ‚Č° 8
  vertex-euler-is-8 = trans ph-vertex-euler
    (trans (cong (őĽ d ‚Üí d * euler) dim-is-4)
           (cong (őĽ e ‚Üí 4 * e) euler-is-2))

  norm-is-4 : norm-index ‚Č° 4
  norm-is-4 = trans ph-norm dim-is-4

  res-is-1 : res-index ‚Č° 1
  res-is-1 = trans ph-res temporal-is-1

  loop-is-1 : min-loop ‚Č° 1
  loop-is-1 = trans ph-loop temporal-is-1

  frac-num-is-1 : frac-num ‚Č° 1
  frac-num-is-1 = trans ph-frac-num temporal-is-1

  frac-den-is-6 : frac-den ‚Č° 6
  frac-den-is-6 = trans ph-frac-den edge-is-6
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Uniqueness: any two representations agree}
\label{sec:k4-representation:uniqueness}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

Once the forcing calculation has been carried out for arbitrary
records, fieldwise uniqueness is immediate. Any two admissible
representations have corresponding fields equal to the same forced
values, so they agree one component at a time.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Uniqueness: any two K4Record agree on all 17 fields
module RepUniqueness (r‚āĀ r‚āā : K4Record) where
  private
    module F‚āĀ = ForcedValues r‚āĀ
    module F‚āā = ForcedValues r‚āā
  open K4Record

  dim-‚Č°          : dim r‚āĀ ‚Č° dim r‚āā
  dim-‚Č°          = trans F‚āĀ.dim-is-4 (sym F‚āā.dim-is-4)

  spatial-‚Č°      : n-spatial r‚āĀ ‚Č° n-spatial r‚āā
  spatial-‚Č°      = trans F‚āĀ.spatial-is-3 (sym F‚āā.spatial-is-3)

  temporal-‚Č°     : n-temporal r‚āĀ ‚Č° n-temporal r‚āā
  temporal-‚Č°     = trans F‚āĀ.temporal-is-1 (sym F‚āā.temporal-is-1)

  edge-‚Č°        : edge-count r‚āĀ ‚Č° edge-count r‚āā
  edge-‚Č°        = trans F‚āĀ.edge-is-6 (sym F‚āā.edge-is-6)

  faces-‚Č°        : face-count r‚āĀ ‚Č° face-count r‚āā
  faces-‚Č°        = trans F‚āĀ.faces-is-4 (sym F‚āā.faces-is-4)

  euler-‚Č°        : euler r‚āĀ ‚Č° euler r‚āā
  euler-‚Č°        = trans F‚āĀ.euler-is-2 (sym F‚āā.euler-is-2)

  eval-‚Č°     : graph-eval r‚āĀ ‚Č° graph-eval r‚āā
  eval-‚Č°     = trans F‚āĀ.eval-is-137 (sym F‚āā.eval-is-137)

  codim-‚Č°          : codim r‚āĀ ‚Č° codim r‚āā
  codim-‚Č°          = trans F‚āĀ.codim-is-3 (sym F‚āā.codim-is-3)

  clifford-‚Č°       : clifford-dim r‚āĀ ‚Č° clifford-dim r‚āā
  clifford-‚Č°       = trans F‚āĀ.clifford-is-16 (sym F‚āā.clifford-is-16)

  hierarchy-‚Č°    : hierarchy r‚āĀ ‚Č° hierarchy r‚āā
  hierarchy-‚Č°    = trans F‚āĀ.hierarchy-is-22 (sym F‚āā.hierarchy-is-22)

  auto-‚Č°         : auto-count r‚āĀ ‚Č° auto-count r‚āā
  auto-‚Č°         = trans F‚āĀ.auto-is-24 (sym F‚āā.auto-is-24)

  vertex-euler-‚Č°         : vertex-euler-product r‚āĀ ‚Č° vertex-euler-product r‚āā
  vertex-euler-‚Č°         = trans F‚āĀ.vertex-euler-is-8 (sym F‚āā.vertex-euler-is-8)

  norm-‚Č°           : norm-index r‚āĀ ‚Č° norm-index r‚āā
  norm-‚Č°           = trans F‚āĀ.norm-is-4 (sym F‚āā.norm-is-4)

  res-‚Č°           : res-index r‚āĀ ‚Č° res-index r‚āā
  res-‚Č°           = trans F‚āĀ.res-is-1 (sym F‚āā.res-is-1)

  loop-‚Č°         : min-loop r‚āĀ ‚Č° min-loop r‚āā
  loop-‚Č°         = trans F‚āĀ.loop-is-1 (sym F‚āā.loop-is-1)

  frac-num-‚Č°   : frac-num r‚āĀ ‚Č° frac-num r‚āā
  frac-num-‚Č°   = trans F‚āĀ.frac-num-is-1 (sym F‚āā.frac-num-is-1)

  frac-den-‚Č°   : frac-den r‚āĀ ‚Č° frac-den r‚āā
  frac-den-‚Č°   = trans F‚āĀ.frac-den-is-6 (sym F‚āā.frac-den-is-6)
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Singleton: the representation has exactly one inhabitant}
\label{sec:k4-representation:singleton}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

Fieldwise agreement is not yet record equality because \texttt{K4Record}
also contains proof fields. Since these proof fields are equations
