between natural numbers, Hedberg's theorem on decidable equality of
$\mathbb{N}$ provides UIP for them. Record equality then follows from
agreement of both data and proof components.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Decidable equality on ‚Ąē.

data Dec‚Ąē (A : Set) : Set where
  decided-yes : A   ‚Üí Dec‚Ąē A
  decided-no  : ¬¨ A ‚Üí Dec‚Ąē A

_‚Čü‚Ąē_ : (m n : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Dec‚Ąē (m ‚Č° n)
zero  ‚Čü‚Ąē zero  = decided-yes refl
zero  ‚Čü‚Ąē suc n = decided-no (őĽ ())
suc m ‚Čü‚Ąē zero  = decided-no (őĽ ())
suc m ‚Čü‚Ąē suc n with m ‚Čü‚Ąē n
... | decided-yes refl = decided-yes refl
... | decided-no  m‚ČĘn  = decided-no (őĽ p ‚Üí m‚ČĘn (suc-injective p))

-- ¬ß Hedberg's theorem for ‚Ąē.

-- ¬ß Canonical proof selected by decidable equality.
private
  ‚Ąē-canonical : {m n : ‚Ąē} ‚Üí m ‚Č° n ‚Üí m ‚Č° n
  ‚Ąē-canonical {m} {n} p with m ‚Čü‚Ąē n
  ... | decided-yes c = c
  ... | decided-no  f = ‚ä•-elim (f p)

  ‚Ąē-canonical-const : {m n : ‚Ąē} (p q : m ‚Č° n) ‚Üí ‚Ąē-canonical p ‚Č° ‚Ąē-canonical q
  ‚Ąē-canonical-const {m} {n} p q with m ‚Čü‚Ąē n
  ... | decided-yes _ = refl
  ... | decided-no  f = ‚ä•-elim (f p)

  -- ¬ß General cancellation: trans (sym p) p reduces to refl.
  trans-sym-cancel : {m n : ‚Ąē} (p : m ‚Č° n) ‚Üí trans (sym p) p ‚Č° refl
  trans-sym-cancel refl = refl

  -- ¬ß Cancel: any proof p is equal to the canonical one composed with a correction.
  ‚Ąē-cancel : {m n : ‚Ąē} (p : m ‚Č° n) ‚Üí
    p ‚Č° trans (sym (‚Ąē-canonical refl)) (‚Ąē-canonical p)
  ‚Ąē-cancel refl = sym (trans-sym-cancel (‚Ąē-canonical refl))

-- ¬ß Hedberg: any two proofs of m ‚Č° n in ‚Ąē are equal.
‚Ąē-UIP : {m n : ‚Ąē} (p q : m ‚Č° n) ‚Üí p ‚Č° q
‚Ąē-UIP p q =
  trans (‚Ąē-cancel p)
    (trans (cong (trans (sym (‚Ąē-canonical refl))) (‚Ąē-canonical-const p q))
           (sym (‚Ąē-cancel q)))

\end{code}

The singleton proof decomposes an arbitrary record into its data fields
and proof fields. The forced-value equations identify every data field
with its canonical value; then UIP for equations in $\mathbb{N}$
collapses the remaining proof fields to the canonical witnesses. Record
eta closes the final equality.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß K4Record singleton.

private
  singleton-lemma :
    ‚ąÄ d s t g f e c ng sp h a b bh bn bd u ml
    ‚Üí d ‚Č° 4 ‚Üí s ‚Č° 3 ‚Üí t ‚Č° 1 ‚Üí g ‚Č° 6 ‚Üí f ‚Č° 4 ‚Üí e ‚Č° 2
    ‚Üí c ‚Č° 137 ‚Üí ng ‚Č° 3 ‚Üí sp ‚Č° 16 ‚Üí h ‚Č° 22 ‚Üí a ‚Č° 24 ‚Üí b ‚Č° 8
    ‚Üí bh ‚Č° 4 ‚Üí bn ‚Č° 1 ‚Üí bd ‚Č° 6 ‚Üí u ‚Č° 1 ‚Üí ml ‚Č° 1
    ‚Üí (panc  : d ‚Č° vertexCountK4)      ‚Üí (pdeg  : s ‚Č° d ‚ął 1)
    ‚Üí (ptemp : t ‚Č° d ‚ął s)              ‚Üí (pedge : g ‚Č° (d * s) div‚Ąē 2)
    ‚Üí (pface : f ‚Č° d)                  ‚Üí (pchi  : e ‚Č° (d + f) ‚ął g)
    ‚Üí (pspin : sp ‚Č° 2 ^ d)             ‚Üí (pcoup : c ‚Č° (d ^ s) * e + s * s)
    ‚Üí (pgen  : ng ‚Č° s)                 ‚Üí (phier : h ‚Č° d * g ‚ął e)
    ‚Üí (pauto : a ‚Č° d * s * (s ‚ął 1) * (s ‚ął 2))
    ‚Üí (pbell : b ‚Č° d * e)              ‚Üí (pbh   : bh ‚Č° d)
    ‚Üí (puv   : u ‚Č° t)                  ‚Üí (ploop : ml ‚Č° t)
    ‚Üí (pbnum : bn ‚Č° t)                 ‚Üí (pbden : bd ‚Č° g)
    ‚Üí record
        { dim = d ; n-spatial = s ; n-temporal = t ; edge-count = g
        ; face-count = f ; euler = e ; graph-eval = c ; codim = ng
        ; clifford-dim = sp ; hierarchy = h ; auto-count = a ; vertex-euler-product = b
        ; norm-index = bh ; frac-num = bn ; frac-den = bd
        ; res-index = u ; min-loop = ml
        ; anchor = panc ; cg-deg = pdeg ; cg-temporal = ptemp
        ; cg-edges = pedge ; cg-faces = pface ; cg-euler = pchi
        ; ph-clifford = pspin ; ph-eval = pcoup ; ph-codim = pgen
        ; ph-hierarchy = phier ; ph-auto = pauto ; ph-vertex-euler = pbell
        ; ph-norm = pbh ; ph-res = puv ; ph-loop = ploop
        ; ph-frac-num = pbnum ; ph-frac-den = pbden
        } ‚Č° canonical-rep
  singleton-lemma .4 .3 .1 .6 .4 .2 .137 .3 .16 .22 .24 .8 .4 .1 .6 .1 .1
    refl refl refl refl refl refl refl refl refl refl refl refl refl refl refl refl refl
    panc pdeg ptemp pedge pface pchi pspin pcoup pgen phier pauto pbell pbh puv ploop pbnum pbden
    rewrite ‚Ąē-UIP panc refl  | ‚Ąē-UIP pdeg refl  | ‚Ąē-UIP ptemp refl
    | ‚Ąē-UIP pedge refl | ‚Ąē-UIP pface refl | ‚Ąē-UIP pchi refl
    | ‚Ąē-UIP pspin refl | ‚Ąē-UIP pcoup refl | ‚Ąē-UIP pgen refl
    | ‚Ąē-UIP phier refl | ‚Ąē-UIP pauto refl | ‚Ąē-UIP pbell refl
    | ‚Ąē-UIP pbh refl   | ‚Ąē-UIP puv refl   | ‚Ąē-UIP ploop refl
    | ‚Ąē-UIP pbnum refl | ‚Ąē-UIP pbden refl
    = refl

K4Record-is-canonical : (r : K4Record) ‚Üí r ‚Č° canonical-rep
K4Record-is-canonical r = singleton-lemma
  dim n-spatial n-temporal edge-count face-count euler
  graph-eval codim clifford-dim hierarchy auto-count vertex-euler-product
  norm-index frac-num frac-den res-index min-loop
  dim-is-4 spatial-is-3 temporal-is-1 edge-is-6 faces-is-4 euler-is-2
  eval-is-137 codim-is-3 clifford-is-16 hierarchy-is-22 auto-is-24 vertex-euler-is-8
  norm-is-4 frac-num-is-1 frac-den-is-6 res-is-1 loop-is-1
  anchor cg-deg cg-temporal cg-edges cg-faces cg-euler
  ph-clifford ph-eval ph-codim ph-hierarchy ph-auto ph-vertex-euler
  ph-norm ph-res ph-loop ph-frac-num ph-frac-den
  where
    open K4Record r
    open ForcedValues r

-- ¬ß Corollary: any two representations are propositionally equal.
K4Record-singleton : (r‚āĀ r‚āā : K4Record) ‚Üí r‚āĀ ‚Č° r‚āā
K4Record-singleton r‚āĀ r‚āā =
  trans (K4Record-is-canonical r‚āĀ) (sym (K4Record-is-canonical r‚āā))
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Cross-constraints: inter-layer consistency}
\label{sec:k4-representation:cross-constraints}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The same numerical quantities were obtained along several routes. The
equalities collected here verify that the graph-theoretic, algebraic,
spectral, and combinatorial descriptions agree on the canonical
record.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Cross-layer bridges between forced values
module CrossConstraints where
  open K4Record

  -- ¬ß Spatial directions = codimension count
  cross-spatial-is-gen : n-spatial canonical-rep ‚Č° codim canonical-rep
  cross-spatial-is-gen = refl

  -- ¬ß Boundary normalization index = vertex count
  cross-norm-is-dim : faceCountK4 ‚Č° vertexCountK4
  cross-norm-is-dim = refl

  -- ¬ß Triangle-cycle order = residual atom count = V ‚ął d
  cross-loop-is-residual : triangle-loop-order ‚Č° vertexCountK4 ‚ął degree-K4
  cross-loop-is-residual = refl

  -- ¬ß Fraction denominator = edge count
  cross-frac-is-edge : frac-den canonical-rep ‚Č° edge-count canonical-rep
  cross-frac-is-edge = refl

  -- ¬ß Automorphism count = dim √ó edge-count (= V √ó E = 4 √ó 6 = 24)
  cross-auto-is-dim-edge :
    vertexCountK4 * degree-K4 * (degree-K4 ‚ął 1) * (degree-K4 ‚ął 2) ‚Č° 24
  cross-auto-is-dim-edge = refl

  -- ¬ß Hierarchy + Euler = V ¬∑ E (22 + 2 = 24)
  cross-hierarchy-plus-euler :
    hierarchy-exponent + eulerChar-computed ‚Č°
    vertexCountK4 * edgeCountK4
  cross-hierarchy-plus-euler = refl
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Constraint derivation: from named invariants to canonical witness}
\label{sec:k4-representation:constraint-derivation}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The canonical record can also be reconstructed directly from the named
$K_4$ invariants. In this form no bare numeral is inserted
independently; the representation is assembled entirely from
previously computed quantities.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Derivation: named invariants assemble into a representation
module ConstraintDerivation where

  -- ¬ß Graph-theoretic laws (K‚āĄ as complete graph)
  law-cg-deg : degree-K4 ‚Č° vertexCountK4 ‚ął 1
  law-cg-deg = refl

  law-cg-edges : edgeCountK4 ‚Č° (vertexCountK4 * degree-K4) div‚Ąē 2
  law-cg-edges = refl

  law-cg-faces : faceCountK4 ‚Č° vertexCountK4
  law-cg-faces = refl

  law-cg-euler : eulerChar-computed ‚Č° (vertexCountK4 + faceCountK4) ‚ął edgeCountK4
  law-cg-euler = refl

  -- ¬ß Algebraic laws (representation theory)
  law-ph-clifford : clifford-dimension ‚Č° 2 ^ vertexCountK4
  law-ph-clifford = refl

  law-ph-auto :
    vertexCountK4 * degree-K4 * (degree-K4 ‚ął 1) * (degree-K4 ‚ął 2) ‚Č° 24
  law-ph-auto = refl

  -- ¬ß Simplex-Euler evaluation law (K‚āĄ invariant basis)
  law-ph-eval :
    simplex-eval ‚Č°
    (vertexCountK4 ^ degree-K4) * eulerChar-computed + degree-K4 * degree-K4
  law-ph-eval = refl

  -- ¬ß Combinatorial law (K‚āĄ arithmetic)
  law-ph-hierarchy :
    hierarchy-exponent ‚Č° vertexCountK4 * edgeCountK4 ‚ął eulerChar-computed
  law-ph-hierarchy = refl

  -- ¬ß Structural assembly: K4Record from named invariants only
  derived-rep : K4Record
  derived-rep = record
    { dim          = vertexCountK4
    ; n-spatial    = degree-K4
    ; n-temporal   = vertexCountK4 ‚ął degree-K4
    ; edge-count   = edgeCountK4
    ; face-count   = faceCountK4
    ; euler        = eulerChar-computed
    ; graph-eval = simplex-eval
    ; codim        = degree-K4
    ; clifford-dim   = clifford-dimension
    ; hierarchy    = hierarchy-exponent
    ; auto-count   = vertexCountK4 * degree-K4
                   * (degree-K4 ‚ął 1) * (degree-K4 ‚ął 2)
    ; vertex-euler-product      = vertexCountK4 * eulerChar-computed
    ; norm-index      = vertexCountK4
    ; frac-num   = vertexCountK4 ‚ął degree-K4
    ; frac-den   = edgeCountK4
    ; res-index    = vertexCountK4 ‚ął degree-K4
    ; min-loop     = vertexCountK4 ‚ął degree-K4
    ; anchor       = refl
    ; cg-deg       = refl
    ; cg-temporal  = refl
    ; cg-edges     = refl
    ; cg-faces     = refl
    ; cg-euler     = refl
    ; ph-clifford    = refl
    ; ph-auto      = refl
    ; ph-eval  = refl
    ; ph-hierarchy = refl
    ; ph-codim       = refl
    ; ph-vertex-euler      = refl
    ; ph-norm        = refl
    ; ph-res        = refl
    ; ph-loop      = refl
    ; ph-frac-num = refl
    ; ph-frac-den = refl
    }

  -- ¬ß derived-rep IS canonical-rep (definitional equality)
  derivation-is-canonical : derived-rep ‚Č° canonical-rep
  derivation-is-canonical = refl
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Represented quantities as computable \texorpdfstring{$K_4$}{K4} invariants}
\label{sec:k4-representation:computable-invariants}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The record entries may now be read as explicit computable functions of
the concrete $K_4$ carrier. This rephrasing adds no new data; it makes
the relation between the representation record and graph calculations
direct.

\subsection{Representation Completeness}
\label{sec:k4-representation:representation-completeness}

The completeness statement says that every admissible representation
already has the same observable values as the canonical one. The
formal bridge into the observable language is therefore independent of
presentation.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß RepresentationComplete: every K4Record agrees with canonical-rep on all values.
module RepresentationComplete (r : K4Record) where
  private module F = ForcedValues r
  open K4Record

  val-dim        : dim r          ‚Č° dim canonical-rep
  val-dim        = F.dim-is-4

  val-spatial    : n-spatial r    ‚Č° n-spatial canonical-rep
  val-spatial    = F.spatial-is-3

  val-temporal   : n-temporal r   ‚Č° n-temporal canonical-rep
  val-temporal   = F.temporal-is-1

  val-edge      : edge-count r   ‚Č° edge-count canonical-rep
  val-edge      = F.edge-is-6

  val-faces      : face-count r   ‚Č° face-count canonical-rep
  val-faces      = F.faces-is-4

  val-euler      : euler r        ‚Č° euler canonical-rep
  val-euler      = F.euler-is-2

  val-eval   : graph-eval r ‚Č° graph-eval canonical-rep
  val-eval   = F.eval-is-137

  val-codim        : codim r        ‚Č° codim canonical-rep
  val-codim        = F.codim-is-3

  val-clifford     : clifford-dim r   ‚Č° clifford-dim canonical-rep
  val-clifford     = F.clifford-is-16

  val-hierarchy  : hierarchy r    ‚Č° hierarchy canonical-rep
  val-hierarchy  = F.hierarchy-is-22

  val-auto       : auto-count r   ‚Č° auto-count canonical-rep
  val-auto       = F.auto-is-24

  val-vertex-euler       : vertex-euler-product r      ‚Č° vertex-euler-product canonical-rep
  val-vertex-euler       = F.vertex-euler-is-8

  val-bh         : norm-index r      ‚Č° norm-index canonical-rep
  val-bh         = F.norm-is-4

  val-uv         : res-index r    ‚Č° res-index canonical-rep
  val-uv         = F.res-is-1

  val-loop       : min-loop r     ‚Č° min-loop canonical-rep
  val-loop       = F.loop-is-1

  val-frac-num : frac-num r   ‚Č° frac-num canonical-rep
  val-frac-num = F.frac-num-is-1

  val-frac-den : frac-den r   ‚Č° frac-den canonical-rep
  val-frac-den = F.frac-den-is-6
\end{code}

\subsubsection*{Local graph observables}

The first block isolates the vertex-local calculations: row sums,
basis propagation, and the corresponding uniformity facts.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß Represented quantities as computable K‚āĄ functions
module K4Quantities where

  -- ¬ß Adjacency row sum at each vertex
  adjacency-row-sum : K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē
  adjacency-row-sum v =
    adjacent v v‚āÄ + adjacent v v‚āĀ + adjacent v v‚āā + adjacent v v‚āÉ

  -- ¬ß Row sum is vertex-uniform and equals degree
  adjacency-row-sum-is-degree : (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí adjacency-row-sum v ‚Č° degree-K4
  adjacency-row-sum-is-degree = law-adjacency-degree

  -- ¬ß Each row sum propagates independently (basis property)
  adjacency-row-sum-independent : (u v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí
    sum-neighbors (K4-basis u) v ‚Č° adjacent v u
  adjacency-row-sum-independent = law-basis-spreads

\end{code}

\subsubsection*{Global quantities built from the local layer}

Once the local quantities are fixed, the remaining global ones are
short derived computations.

\begin{code}

  -- ¬ß Boundary count: 2-simplex count = face count
  boundary-count : ‚Ąē
  boundary-count = faceCountK4

  -- ¬ß Self-duality: boundary = bulk (F = V for the tetrahedron)
  boundary-is-bulk : boundary-count ‚Č° vertexCountK4
  boundary-is-bulk = refl

  -- ¬ß Spectral width: largest minus smallest adjacency eigenvalue
  spectral-width : ‚Ąē
  spectral-width = degree-K4 + 1

  -- ¬ß Spectral width equals vertex count
  spectral-width-is-V : spectral-width ‚Č° vertexCountK4
  spectral-width-is-V = refl

  -- ¬ß Spectral-Euler product: spectral width √ó Euler characteristic
  spectral-euler-product : ‚Ąē
  spectral-euler-product = spectral-width * eulerChar-computed

  -- ¬ß Spectral-Euler product equals őļ-discrete
  spectral-euler-product-is-őļ : spectral-euler-product ‚Č° őļ-discrete
  spectral-euler-product-is-őļ = refl

  -- ¬ß Complement degree: V ‚ął deg
  complement-degree : ‚Ąē
  complement-degree = vertexCountK4 ‚ął degree-K4

  -- ¬ß Complement degree is 1
  complement-degree-is-1 : complement-degree ‚Č° 1
  complement-degree-is-1 = refl

  -- ¬ß Same computation, three names (all V ‚ął deg)
  complement-degree-is-max-prop : complement-degree ‚Č° max-propagation-per-edge
  complement-degree-is-max-prop = refl

  complement-degree-is-triangle : complement-degree ‚Č° triangle-loop-order
  complement-degree-is-triangle = refl

  complement-degree-is-lattice : complement-degree ‚Č° lattice-spacing-natural
  complement-degree-is-lattice = refl

  -- ¬ß Edge tally: total pairwise edges
  edge-tally : ‚Ąē
  edge-tally = edgeCountK4
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Observable representation and closure}
\label{sec:k4-representation:observable-representation-closure}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The representation can now be rebuilt from the observable quantities
alone. The resulting record is definitionally the canonical one, so no
additional assignment layer remains.

\begin{code}
  -- ¬ß K4Record from computed quantities (no bare numerals)
  observable-rep : K4Record
  observable-rep = record
    { dim          = vertexCountK4
    ; n-spatial    = degree-K4
    ; n-temporal   = complement-degree
    ; edge-count   = edge-tally
    ; face-count   = boundary-count
    ; euler        = eulerChar-computed
    ; graph-eval = simplex-eval
    ; codim        = degree-K4
    ; clifford-dim   = clifford-dimension
    ; hierarchy    = hierarchy-exponent
    ; auto-count   = vertexCountK4 * degree-K4
                   * (degree-K4 ‚ął 1) * (degree-K4 ‚ął 2)
    ; vertex-euler-product      = spectral-euler-product
    ; norm-index      = boundary-count
    ; frac-num   = complement-degree
    ; frac-den   = edge-tally
    ; res-index    = complement-degree
    ; min-loop     = complement-degree
    ; anchor       = refl
    ; cg-deg       = refl
    ; cg-temporal  = refl
    ; cg-edges     = refl
    ; cg-faces     = refl
    ; cg-euler     = refl
    ; ph-clifford    = refl
    ; ph-auto      = refl
    ; ph-eval  = refl
    ; ph-hierarchy = refl
    ; ph-codim       = refl
    ; ph-vertex-euler      = refl
    ; ph-norm        = refl
    ; ph-res        = refl
    ; ph-loop      = refl
    ; ph-frac-num = refl
    ; ph-frac-den = refl
    }

  -- ¬ß Closure: observable-rep IS canonical-rep (definitional equality)
  observable-is-canonical : observable-rep ‚Č° canonical-rep
  observable-is-canonical = refl
\end{code}


%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{The observable principle}
\label{sec:k4-representation:observable-principle}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

To close the semantic side, observables are specified explicitly.
Local observables are uniform vertex functions, and global observables
are drawn from a finite canonical list.

\subsubsection*{Observable types and invariance}

\begin{code}
  -- ¬ß K‚āĄ vertex-level invariance
  IsK4Invariant : (K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Set
  IsK4Invariant f = (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí f v ‚Č° f w

  -- ¬ß Local observable: vertex function + uniformity proof
  LocalObservable : Set
  LocalObservable = ő£ (K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē) IsK4Invariant

  -- ¬ß Global admissible observables collapse to four canonical codes.
  data GlobalObservable : Set where
    go-boundary go-spectral go-complement go-edges : GlobalObservable

  eval-global-observable : GlobalObservable ‚Üí ‚Ąē
  eval-global-observable go-boundary   = boundary-count
  eval-global-observable go-spectral = spectral-euler-product
  eval-global-observable go-complement   = complement-degree
  eval-global-observable go-edges   = edge-tally

  -- ¬ß Complete observable type
  Observable : Set
  Observable = LocalObservable ‚äé GlobalObservable

  -- ¬ß Every invariant function has a unique value, independent of vertex
  invariant-value : (f : K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē) ‚Üí IsK4Invariant f ‚Üí ‚Ąē
  invariant-value f _ = f v‚āÄ

  invariant-any-vertex : (f : K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē) ‚Üí (inv : IsK4Invariant f) ‚Üí
    (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí f v ‚Č° invariant-value f inv
  invariant-any-vertex f inv v = inv v v‚āÄ

  observable-value : Observable ‚Üí ‚Ąē
  observable-value (inj‚āĀ (f , inv)) = invariant-value f inv
  observable-value (inj‚āā g) = eval-global-observable g

\end{code}

\subsubsection*{Local observable witnesses}

This block records the local pattern once: define the observable,
prove uniformity, and extract its value.

\begin{code}

  -- ¬ß Adjacency-row-sum is a local observable
  adjacency-row-sum-invariant : IsK4Invariant adjacency-row-sum
  adjacency-row-sum-invariant v w =
    trans (adjacency-row-sum-is-degree v) (sym (adjacency-row-sum-is-degree w))

  adjacency-row-sum-observable : LocalObservable
  adjacency-row-sum-observable = adjacency-row-sum , adjacency-row-sum-invariant

  -- ¬ß Observed value is degree = 3
  adjacency-row-sum-observed-value : invariant-value adjacency-row-sum adjacency-row-sum-invariant
                        ‚Č° degree-K4
  adjacency-row-sum-observed-value = refl

  -- ¬ß Adjacency row sum is a local observable
  adj-row : K4Vertex ‚Üí K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē
  adj-row v w = adjacent v w

  adj-row-sum-invariant : IsK4Invariant (őĽ v ‚Üí
    adj-row v v‚āÄ + adj-row v v‚āĀ + adj-row v v‚āā + adj-row v v‚āÉ)
  adj-row-sum-invariant v w =
    trans (law-adjacency-degree v) (sym (law-adjacency-degree w))

\end{code}

\subsubsection*{Global observable witnesses}

For the global layer, admissibility is encoded by a finite datatype.
The type \texttt{GlobalObservable} has exactly four constructors, and
the exhaustiveness lemma records that no fifth primitive class has
been left open.

\begin{code}

  -- ¬ß Global observables (invariant by construction)
  obs-boundary   : Observable
  obs-boundary   = inj‚āā go-boundary

  obs-spectral : Observable
  obs-spectral = inj‚āā go-spectral

  obs-complement   : Observable
  obs-complement   = inj‚āā go-complement

  obs-edges   : Observable
  obs-edges   = inj‚āā go-edges

  global-observable-exhaustive :
    (g : GlobalObservable) ‚Üí
    (g ‚Č° go-boundary)
    ‚äé ((g ‚Č° go-spectral)
    ‚äé ((g ‚Č° go-complement) ‚äé (g ‚Č° go-edges)))
  global-observable-exhaustive go-boundary   = inj‚āĀ refl
  global-observable-exhaustive go-spectral = inj‚āā (inj‚āĀ refl)
  global-observable-exhaustive go-complement   = inj‚āā (inj‚āā (inj‚āĀ refl))
  global-observable-exhaustive go-edges   = inj‚āā (inj‚āā (inj‚āā refl))

  -- ¬ß Derived ledger invariants remain observable values, but not new primitive classes.
  obs-eval-value : ‚Ąē
  obs-eval-value = simplex-eval

  obs-clifford-value : ‚Ąē
  obs-clifford-value = clifford-dimension

  obs-hierarchy-value : ‚Ąē
  obs-hierarchy-value = hierarchy-exponent

  obs-euler-value : ‚Ąē
  obs-euler-value = eulerChar-computed
\end{code}

%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź
\chapter{\texorpdfstring{$K_4$}{K4} Vertex Permutations}
\label{ch:k4-vertex-permutations}
%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź

This chapter internalizes the action of
$\mathrm{Aut}(K_4) \cong S_4$ on the vertex set. Using the
permutation machinery already established on \texttt{EndoCase} and an
explicit bijection with \texttt{K4Vertex}, one obtains the lifted
permutations and the invariance tools needed later.

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Vertex bijection}
\label{sec:k4-perms:bijection}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The transfer begins with an explicit bijection between
\texttt{K4Vertex} and \texttt{EndoCase}. Once this identification is
fixed, the existing permutation lemmas on \texttt{EndoCase} can be
reused verbatim.

\begin{code}
k4-to-endo : K4Vertex ‚Üí EndoCase
k4-to-endo v‚āÄ = case-constL
k4-to-endo v‚āĀ = case-constR
k4-to-endo v‚āā = case-id
k4-to-endo v‚āÉ = case-dual

endo-to-k4 : EndoCase ‚Üí K4Vertex
endo-to-k4 case-constL = v‚āÄ
endo-to-k4 case-constR = v‚āĀ
endo-to-k4 case-id     = v‚āā
endo-to-k4 case-dual   = v‚āÉ

k4-endo-k4 : (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí endo-to-k4 (k4-to-endo v) ‚Č° v
k4-endo-k4 v‚āÄ = refl
k4-endo-k4 v‚āĀ = refl
k4-endo-k4 v‚āā = refl
k4-endo-k4 v‚āÉ = refl

endo-k4-endo : (e : EndoCase) ‚Üí k4-to-endo (endo-to-k4 e) ‚Č° e
endo-k4-endo case-constL = refl
endo-k4-endo case-constR = refl
endo-k4-endo case-id     = refl
endo-k4-endo case-dual   = refl
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Permutation record and lifting}
\label{sec:k4-perms:record}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

Permutations of \texttt{K4Vertex} are encoded as bijections with
explicit inverses. The lifting operation \texttt{liftPerm} is
conjugation through the established bijection, so bijectivity is
transported automatically.

\begin{code}
record K4Perm : Set where
  field
    k4to       : K4Vertex ‚Üí K4Vertex
    k4from     : K4Vertex ‚Üí K4Vertex
    k4-to-from : (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí k4to (k4from v) ‚Č° v
    k4-from-to : (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí k4from (k4to v) ‚Č° v

open K4Perm public

liftPerm : EndoPerm ‚Üí K4Perm
liftPerm ŌÉ = record
  { k4to      = őĽ v ‚Üí endo-to-k4 (eto ŌÉ (k4-to-endo v))
  ; k4from    = őĽ v ‚Üí endo-to-k4 (efrom ŌÉ (k4-to-endo v))
  ; k4-to-from = őĽ v ‚Üí
      trans (cong (őĽ e ‚Üí endo-to-k4 (eto ŌÉ e))
                  (endo-k4-endo (efrom ŌÉ (k4-to-endo v))))
            (trans (cong endo-to-k4 (eto-efrom ŌÉ (k4-to-endo v)))
                   (k4-endo-k4 v))
  ; k4-from-to = őĽ v ‚Üí
      trans (cong (őĽ e ‚Üí endo-to-k4 (efrom ŌÉ e))
                  (endo-k4-endo (eto ŌÉ (k4-to-endo v))))
            (trans (cong endo-to-k4 (efrom-eto ŌÉ (k4-to-endo v)))
                   (k4-endo-k4 v))
  }
\end{code}

Composition of two permutations inherits bijectivity from the components.

\begin{code}
k4Perm-comp : K4Perm ‚Üí K4Perm ‚Üí K4Perm
k4Perm-comp ŌÉ ŌĄ = record
  { k4to      = őĽ v ‚Üí k4to ŌÉ (k4to ŌĄ v)
  ; k4from    = őĽ v ‚Üí k4from ŌĄ (k4from ŌÉ v)
  ; k4-to-from = őĽ v ‚Üí
      trans (cong (k4to ŌÉ) (k4-to-from ŌĄ (k4from ŌÉ v)))
            (k4-to-from ŌÉ v)
  ; k4-from-to = őĽ v ‚Üí
      trans (cong (k4from ŌĄ) (k4-from-to ŌÉ (k4to ŌĄ v)))
            (k4-from-to ŌĄ v)
  }
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Decidable equality and adjacency lemmas}
\label{sec:k4-perms:decidable-equality}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

Since \texttt{K4Vertex} has four constructors, equality is decidable
by a finite case split. This allows later arguments to distinguish the
diagonal from the off-diagonal exactly.

\begin{code}
K4-decEq : (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí (v ‚Č° w) ‚äé (v ‚Č† w)
K4-decEq v‚āÄ v‚āÄ = inj‚āĀ refl
K4-decEq v‚āĀ v‚āĀ = inj‚āĀ refl
K4-decEq v‚āā v‚āā = inj‚āĀ refl
K4-decEq v‚āÉ v‚āÉ = inj‚āĀ refl
K4-decEq v‚āÄ v‚āĀ = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
K4-decEq v‚āÄ v‚āā = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
K4-decEq v‚āÄ v‚āÉ = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
K4-decEq v‚āĀ v‚āÄ = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
K4-decEq v‚āĀ v‚āā = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
K4-decEq v‚āĀ v‚āÉ = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
K4-decEq v‚āā v‚āÄ = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
K4-decEq v‚āā v‚āĀ = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
K4-decEq v‚āā v‚āÉ = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
K4-decEq v‚āÉ v‚āÄ = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
K4-decEq v‚āÉ v‚āĀ = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
K4-decEq v‚āÉ v‚āā = inj‚āā (őĽ ())
\end{code}

Two basic lemmas record the shape of \texttt{adjacent}: it is $0$ on
the diagonal and $1$ on every off-diagonal pair. Thus adjacency in
$K_4$ is exactly distinctness.

\begin{code}
adj-self : (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí adjacent v v ‚Č° 0
adj-self v‚āÄ = refl
adj-self v‚āĀ = refl
adj-self v‚āā = refl
adj-self v‚āÉ = refl

K4-distinct-adj : (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí v ‚Č† w ‚Üí adjacent v w ‚Č° 1
K4-distinct-adj v‚āÄ v‚āÄ ne = ‚ä•-elim (ne refl)
K4-distinct-adj v‚āÄ v‚āĀ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āÄ v‚āā _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āÄ v‚āÉ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āĀ v‚āÄ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āĀ v‚āĀ ne = ‚ä•-elim (ne refl)
K4-distinct-adj v‚āĀ v‚āā _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āĀ v‚āÉ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āā v‚āÄ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āā v‚āĀ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āā v‚āā ne = ‚ä•-elim (ne refl)
K4-distinct-adj v‚āā v‚āÉ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āÉ v‚āÄ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āÉ v‚āĀ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āÉ v‚āā _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj v‚āÉ v‚āÉ ne = ‚ä•-elim (ne refl)
\end{code}

Injectivity of any \texttt{K4Perm} follows from the round-trip law: compose with the inverse on both sides.

\begin{code}
k4-injective : (ŌÉ : K4Perm) ‚Üí (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí
  k4to ŌÉ v ‚Č° k4to ŌÉ w ‚Üí v ‚Č° w
k4-injective ŌÉ v w p =
  trans (sym (k4-from-to ŌÉ v))
        (trans (cong (k4from ŌÉ) p)
               (k4-from-to ŌÉ w))
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Transitivity and 2-transitivity}
\label{sec:k4-perms:transitivity}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The symmetry property needed later is $2$-transitivity on ordered
pairs of distinct vertices. After the one-point transport lemma
\texttt{k4Perm-send}, the map \texttt{edge-map} sends the reference
edge $(v_0,v_1)$ to any off-diagonal pair.

\begin{code}
k4Perm-send : (a a' : K4Vertex) ‚Üí ő£ K4Perm (őĽ ŌÉ ‚Üí k4to ŌÉ a ‚Č° a')
k4Perm-send a a' =
  let ep = endoPerm-send (k4-to-endo a) (k4-to-endo a')
  in (liftPerm (fst ep) ,
      trans (cong endo-to-k4 (snd ep)) (k4-endo-k4 a'))
\end{code}

The construction is entirely explicit: each off-diagonal target is
reached by at most two transpositions, and every branch closes by
computation.

\begin{code}
edge-map : (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí v ‚Č† w ‚Üí
  ő£ K4Perm (őĽ ŌÉ ‚Üí ő£ (k4to ŌÉ v‚āÄ ‚Č° v) (őĽ _ ‚Üí k4to ŌÉ v‚āĀ ‚Č° w))
edge-map v‚āÄ v‚āÄ ne = ‚ä•-elim (ne refl)
edge-map v‚āÄ v‚āĀ _  = (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-constL)
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āÄ v‚āā _  = (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-id)
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āÄ v‚āÉ _  = (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-dual)
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āĀ v‚āÄ _  = (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-constR)
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āĀ v‚āĀ ne = ‚ä•-elim (ne refl)
edge-map v‚āĀ v‚āā _  = (k4Perm-comp (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-id))
                                   (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-id))
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āĀ v‚āÉ _  = (k4Perm-comp (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-dual))
                                   (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-dual))
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āā v‚āÄ _  = (k4Perm-comp (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-id))
                                   (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-id))
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āā v‚āĀ _  = (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-id)
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āā v‚āā ne = ‚ä•-elim (ne refl)
edge-map v‚āā v‚āÉ _  = (k4Perm-comp (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-id))
                                   (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-dual))
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āÉ v‚āÄ _  = (k4Perm-comp (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-dual))
                                   (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-dual))
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āÉ v‚āĀ _  = (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-dual)
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āÉ v‚āā _  = (k4Perm-comp (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-dual))
                                   (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-id))
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map v‚āÉ v‚āÉ ne = ‚ä•-elim (ne refl)
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Adjacency preservation}
\label{sec:k4-perms:adjacency-preservation}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

Because adjacency in $K_4$ is equivalent to distinctness, every vertex
permutation preserves adjacency automatically. The proof splits into
the diagonal and off-diagonal cases.

\begin{code}
k4-perm-preserves-adj : (ŌÉ : K4Perm) ‚Üí (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí
  adjacent v w ‚Č° adjacent (k4to ŌÉ v) (k4to ŌÉ w)
k4-perm-preserves-adj ŌÉ v w with K4-decEq v w
... | inj‚āĀ refl = trans (adj-self v) (sym (adj-self (k4to ŌÉ v)))
... | inj‚āā ne   = trans (K4-distinct-adj v w ne)
                        (sym (K4-distinct-adj (k4to ŌÉ v) (k4to ŌÉ w)
                              (őĽ p ‚Üí ne (k4-injective ŌÉ v w p))))
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{K4-invariance and uniform edge weight}
\label{sec:k4-perms:invariance}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

An invariant function on ordered vertex pairs is one that is unchanged
under every \texttt{K4Perm}. Combined with $2$-transitivity, this
implies that any such function is constant on the off-diagonal.

\begin{code}
IsK4PermInvariant : (K4Vertex ‚Üí K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē) ‚Üí Set
IsK4PermInvariant f = (ŌÉ : K4Perm) ‚Üí (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí
  f v w ‚Č° f (k4to ŌÉ v) (k4to ŌÉ w)
\end{code}

The proof compares arbitrary edges with the reference edge
$(v_0,v_1)$ using the permutations supplied by \texttt{edge-map}.

\begin{code}
invariant-uniform-edges : (f : K4Vertex ‚Üí K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē) ‚Üí
  IsK4PermInvariant f ‚Üí
  (v w v' w' : K4Vertex) ‚Üí v ‚Č† w ‚Üí v' ‚Č† w' ‚Üí f v w ‚Č° f v' w'
invariant-uniform-edges f inv v w v' w' ne ne' =
  trans (sym (to-ref v w ne)) (to-ref v' w' ne')
  where
    to-ref : (a b : K4Vertex) ‚Üí a ‚Č† b ‚Üí f v‚āÄ v‚āĀ ‚Č° f a b
    to-ref a b ne-ab =
      let em = edge-map a b ne-ab
          ŌÉ  = fst em
          p  = fst (snd em)
          q  = snd (snd em)
      in trans (inv ŌÉ v‚āÄ v‚āĀ)
               (trans (cong (őĽ x ‚Üí f x (k4to ŌÉ v‚āĀ)) p)
                      (cong (f a) q))
\end{code}

  This reduction to a single reference edge is the symmetry principle
  used in the next chapter.


%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź
\chapter{Forced Propagation}
\label{ch:forced-propagation}
%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź

Earlier chapters computed
$\texttt{max-propagation-per-edge} \equiv 1$, but that numerical
identity alone does not rule out competing propagation laws. This
chapter formulates propagation laws explicitly and shows that the
admissible constraints force the common off-diagonal value to be
exactly $1$.

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Propagation candidates}
\label{sec:forced-propagation:candidates}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

A propagation law is taken to be an arbitrary natural-valued function
on ordered vertex pairs. The definition is intentionally wide: any
restriction must come from the later hypotheses.

\begin{code}
PropagationLaw : Set
PropagationLaw = K4Vertex ‚Üí K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{The four admissibility filters}
\label{sec:forced-propagation:filters}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The hypotheses are separated by role. Locality fixes the diagonal at
$0$, while lower and upper bounds control the off-diagonal values. In
the admissible record these bounds are stored only on the reference
edge $(v_0,v_1)$; invariance later transports them to every other
off-diagonal pair.

\begin{code}
IsLocal : PropagationLaw ‚Üí Set
IsLocal P = (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí P v v ‚Č° 0
\end{code}

Edge support asks only for a lower bound of $1$ on off-diagonal pairs.
At this stage it does not yet identify the value.

\begin{code}
HasEdgeSupport : PropagationLaw ‚Üí Set
HasEdgeSupport P = (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí v ‚Č† w ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ P v w

ReferenceEdgeLower : PropagationLaw ‚Üí Set
ReferenceEdgeLower P = 1 ‚Č§ P v‚āÄ v‚āĀ
\end{code}

Boundedness supplies the complementary upper bound of $1$.

\begin{code}
IsBounded : PropagationLaw ‚Üí Set
IsBounded P = (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí P v w ‚Č§ 1

ReferenceEdgeUpper : PropagationLaw ‚Üí Set
ReferenceEdgeUpper P = P v‚āÄ v‚āĀ ‚Č§ 1
\end{code}

$K_4$-invariance requires compatibility with every vertex permutation.
By the results of the previous chapter, this is the condition that
later collapses all off-diagonal values to a single common quantity.

The admissibility record packages these four conditions together.
Uniqueness arises only from their combination.

\begin{code}
record AdmissiblePropagation (P : PropagationLaw) : Set where
  field
    local     : IsLocal P
    invariant : IsK4PermInvariant P
    ref-lower : ReferenceEdgeLower P
    ref-upper : ReferenceEdgeUpper P
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{The elimination}
\label{sec:forced-propagation:elimination}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The elimination argument has three steps. First, invariance reduces
all off-diagonal values to the reference edge value
$P\,v_0\,v_1$. Second, the lower and upper reference bounds are
transported to every edge. Third, the resulting inequalities
$1 \le r \le 1$ force $r = 1$.

\paragraph{Step 1: Invariance collapses off-diagonal to one value.}
By \texttt{invariant-uniform-edges}, every off-diagonal pair has the
same value as the reference edge.

\begin{code}
propagation-uniform : (P : PropagationLaw) ‚Üí IsK4PermInvariant P ‚Üí
  (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí v ‚Č† w ‚Üí P v w ‚Č° P v‚āÄ v‚āĀ
propagation-uniform P inv v w ne =
  invariant-uniform-edges P inv v w v‚āÄ v‚āĀ ne (őĽ ())
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 2: Invariance transports the brackets.}
The lower and upper bounds are stored only on the reference edge, but
invariance carries them to every off-diagonal pair.

\begin{code}
propagation-supported : (P : PropagationLaw) ‚Üí AdmissiblePropagation P ‚Üí
  (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí v ‚Č† w ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ P v w
propagation-supported P adm v w ne =
  subst (őĽ n ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ n)
    (sym (propagation-uniform P (AdmissiblePropagation.invariant adm) v w ne))
    (AdmissiblePropagation.ref-lower adm)

propagation-bounded : (P : PropagationLaw) ‚Üí AdmissiblePropagation P ‚Üí
  (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí P v w ‚Č§ 1
propagation-bounded P adm v w with K4-decEq v w
... | inj‚āĀ refl = subst (őĽ n ‚Üí n ‚Č§ 1) (sym (AdmissiblePropagation.local adm v)) z‚Č§n
... | inj‚āā ne   = subst (őĽ n ‚Üí n ‚Č§ 1)
                      (sym (propagation-uniform P (AdmissiblePropagation.invariant adm) v w ne))
                      (AdmissiblePropagation.ref-upper adm)
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 3: The sandwich.}
The transported inequalities force the common off-diagonal value to be
$1$.

\begin{code}
v‚āÄ‚Č†v‚āĀ : v‚āÄ ‚Č† v‚āĀ
v‚āÄ‚Č†v‚āĀ ()

propagation-edge-value : (P : PropagationLaw) ‚Üí AdmissiblePropagation P ‚Üí
  P v‚āÄ v‚āĀ ‚Č° 1
propagation-edge-value P adm =
  ‚Č§-antisym
    (AdmissiblePropagation.ref-upper adm)
    (AdmissiblePropagation.ref-lower adm)
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 4: Assembly.}
The diagonal is $0$ by locality and the off-diagonal is $1$ by the
sandwich, so the law coincides with \texttt{adjacent}.

\begin{code}
propagation-off-diag : (P : PropagationLaw) ‚Üí AdmissiblePropagation P ‚Üí
  (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí v ‚Č† w ‚Üí P v w ‚Č° 1
propagation-off-diag P adm v w ne =
  trans (propagation-uniform P (AdmissiblePropagation.invariant adm) v w ne)
        (propagation-edge-value P adm)

propagation-forced : (P : PropagationLaw) ‚Üí AdmissiblePropagation P ‚Üí
  (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí P v w ‚Č° adjacent v w
propagation-forced P adm v w with K4-decEq v w
... | inj‚āĀ refl = trans (AdmissiblePropagation.local adm v)
                        (sym (adj-self v))
... | inj‚āā ne   = trans (propagation-off-diag P adm v w ne)
                        (sym (K4-distinct-adj v w ne))
\end{code}

The theorem therefore reduces the whole comparison to the
diagonal/off-diagonal split; symmetry handles the transport and the
sandwich fixes the common edge value.

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Existence}
\label{sec:forced-propagation:existence}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

Existence is witnessed by the adjacency function itself. It is local
by definition, invariant under every \texttt{K4Perm}, and satisfies
both reference-edge bounds.

\begin{code}
adjacent-is-local : IsLocal adjacent
adjacent-is-local v‚āÄ = refl
adjacent-is-local v‚āĀ = refl
adjacent-is-local v‚āā = refl
adjacent-is-local v‚āÉ = refl

adjacent-supported : HasEdgeSupport adjacent
adjacent-supported v w ne = subst (őĽ n ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ n)
  (sym (K4-distinct-adj v w ne)) (s‚Č§s z‚Č§n)

adjacent-bounded : IsBounded adjacent
adjacent-bounded v‚āÄ v‚āÄ = z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āÄ v‚āĀ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āÄ v‚āā = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āÄ v‚āÉ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āĀ v‚āÄ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āĀ v‚āĀ = z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āĀ v‚āā = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āĀ v‚āÉ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āā v‚āÄ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āā v‚āĀ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āā v‚āā = z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āā v‚āÉ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āÉ v‚āÄ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āÉ v‚āĀ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āÉ v‚āā = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n
adjacent-bounded v‚āÉ v‚āÉ = z‚Č§n

adjacent-invariant : IsK4PermInvariant adjacent
adjacent-invariant ŌÉ v w = k4-perm-preserves-adj ŌÉ v w

adjacent-ref-lower : ReferenceEdgeLower adjacent
adjacent-ref-lower = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

adjacent-ref-upper : ReferenceEdgeUpper adjacent
adjacent-ref-upper = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

adjacent-admissible : AdmissiblePropagation adjacent
adjacent-admissible = record
  { local     = adjacent-is-local
  ; invariant = adjacent-invariant
  ; ref-lower = adjacent-ref-lower
  ; ref-upper = adjacent-ref-upper
  }
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Summary and the forced edge weight}
\label{sec:forced-propagation:summary}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The record \texttt{ForcedPropagationResult} packages the conclusion:
adjacency is admissible, every admissible law coincides with it, and
every admissible edge weight is therefore $1$.

\begin{code}
record ForcedPropagationResult : Set where
  field
    witness   : AdmissiblePropagation adjacent
    unique    : (P : PropagationLaw) ‚Üí AdmissiblePropagation P ‚Üí
                (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí P v w ‚Č° adjacent v w
    rate-is-1 : (P : PropagationLaw) ‚Üí AdmissiblePropagation P ‚Üí
                (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí adjacent v w ‚Č° 1 ‚Üí P v w ‚Č° 1

theorem-forced-propagation : ForcedPropagationResult
theorem-forced-propagation = record
  { witness   = adjacent-admissible
  ; unique    = propagation-forced
  ; rate-is-1 = őĽ P adm v w hw ‚Üí trans (propagation-forced P adm v w) hw
  }
\end{code}

The constant \texttt{edge-weight} merely names the forced value $1$.
The closing lemma identifies it with the previously computed
\texttt{max-propagation-per-edge}.

\begin{code}
edge-weight : ‚Ąē
edge-weight = 1

edge-weight-is-propagation-rate : edge-weight ‚Č° max-propagation-per-edge
edge-weight-is-propagation-rate = refl
\end{code}

%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź
\chapter{Forced Minimal Action}
\label{ch:forced-minimal-action}
%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź

At the tetrahedral level there are four triangular faces, one opposite
each vertex. An action law assigns a natural-number weight to each
face, and the same symmetry-and-sandwich argument used for propagation
applies again: invariance identifies all face values, while the
reference lower and upper bounds fix the common value at $1$.

The candidate space is left unrestricted. The admissible record stores
only invariance together with the two bounds on a reference face, and
the global consequences are recovered afterward.

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Triangles and action candidates}
\label{sec:forced-action:triangles}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

Faces are represented by the unique vertex they omit. This makes the permutation action immediate: a vertex permutation acts on a face by permuting the omitted vertex.

The representation of faces by omitted vertices makes the symmetry
transparent. Defining \texttt{K4Triangle} to be the same type as
\texttt{K4Vertex} means that the permutation action on faces is simply
the already available action on vertices. No separate combinatorics of
three-element subsets is needed.

\begin{code}
K4Triangle : Set
K4Triangle = K4Vertex

triangle-map : K4Perm ‚Üí K4Triangle ‚Üí K4Triangle
triangle-map ŌÉ t = k4to ŌÉ t
\end{code}

An action law is an arbitrary function on faces. The auxiliary
predicates express the global notions of non-triviality, lower bounds,
and minimality, while the admissible record itself stores only
invariance together with the two bounds on the reference face
\texttt{tri-012}.

\begin{code}
ActionLaw : Set
ActionLaw = K4Triangle ‚Üí ‚Ąē

tri-012 : K4Triangle
tri-012 = v‚āÉ

tri-013 : K4Triangle
tri-013 = v‚āā

tri-023 : K4Triangle
tri-023 = v‚āĀ

tri-123 : K4Triangle
tri-123 = v‚āÄ
\end{code}

Once invariance is available, the two reference inequalities can be
transported to every face, and non-triviality becomes a consequence
rather than an extra axiom.

\begin{code}
IsNonTrivial : ActionLaw ‚Üí Set
IsNonTrivial A = (t : K4Triangle) ‚Üí A t ‚Č° 0 ‚Üí ‚ä•

IsActionInvariant : ActionLaw ‚Üí Set
IsActionInvariant A = (ŌÉ : K4Perm) ‚Üí (t : K4Triangle) ‚Üí
  A t ‚Č° A (triangle-map ŌÉ t)

ActionBound : ActionLaw ‚Üí Set
ActionBound A = (t : K4Triangle) ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ A t

ReferenceTriangleLower : ActionLaw ‚Üí Set
ReferenceTriangleLower A = 1 ‚Č§ A tri-012

IsMinimal : ActionLaw ‚Üí Set
IsMinimal A = (t : K4Triangle) ‚Üí A t ‚Č§ 1

ReferenceTriangleUpper : ActionLaw ‚Üí Set
ReferenceTriangleUpper A = A tri-012 ‚Č§ 1

record AdmissibleAction (A : ActionLaw) : Set where
  field
    invariant  : IsActionInvariant A
    ref-lower  : ReferenceTriangleLower A
    ref-upper  : ReferenceTriangleUpper A
\end{code}

The four canonical triangle faces of $K_4$ are the faces opposite $v_3$, $v_2$, $v_1$, and $v_0$ respectively.

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Existence: the unit action law}
\label{sec:forced-action:existence}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

Existence is witnessed by the constant action law with value $1$. It is
invariant by definition and satisfies both reference bounds
immediately.

\begin{code}
unit-action : ActionLaw
unit-action _ = 1

unit-action-invariant : IsActionInvariant unit-action
unit-action-invariant _ _ = refl

unit-action-bounded : ActionBound unit-action
unit-action-bounded _ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

unit-action-minimal : IsMinimal unit-action
unit-action-minimal _ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

unit-action-ref-lower : ReferenceTriangleLower unit-action
unit-action-ref-lower = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

unit-action-ref-upper : ReferenceTriangleUpper unit-action
unit-action-ref-upper = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

unit-action-admissible : AdmissibleAction unit-action
unit-action-admissible = record
  { invariant  = unit-action-invariant
  ; ref-lower  = unit-action-ref-lower
  ; ref-upper  = unit-action-ref-upper
  }
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Construction: invariance and the sandwich pin a single value}
\label{sec:forced-action:elimination}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The proof has the same shape as before. The lemma
\texttt{action-from-012} transports the value of the reference face to
an arbitrary face, the transported bounds give $1 \le r \le 1$, and
antisymmetry identifies the common value with $1$.

\begin{code}
action-from-012 : (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleAction A ‚Üí
  (t : K4Triangle) ‚Üí A tri-012 ‚Č° A t
action-from-012 A adm t =
  let st = k4Perm-send tri-012 t in
  let ŌÉ = fst st in
  let p = snd st in
  trans (AdmissibleAction.invariant adm ŌÉ tri-012)
        (cong A p)

action-all-equal-to-012 : (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleAction A ‚Üí
  (t : K4Triangle) ‚Üí A t ‚Č° A tri-012
action-all-equal-to-012 A adm t =
  sym (action-from-012 A adm t)

action-global-value : (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleAction A ‚Üí
  (t‚āĀ t‚āā : K4Triangle) ‚Üí A t‚āĀ ‚Č° A t‚āā
action-global-value A adm t‚āĀ t‚āā =
  trans (action-all-equal-to-012 A adm t‚āĀ)
        (action-from-012 A adm t‚āā)
\end{code}

\paragraph{Invariance transports the brackets.}
The lower and upper bounds are imposed only on the reference face
\texttt{tri-012}. Invariance transports them to every face.

\begin{code}
action-bound : (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleAction A ‚Üí ActionBound A
action-bound A adm t =
  subst (őĽ n ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ n)
    (action-from-012 A adm t)
    (AdmissibleAction.ref-lower adm)

action-minimal : (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleAction A ‚Üí IsMinimal A
action-minimal A adm t =
  subst (őĽ n ‚Üí n ‚Č§ 1)
    (action-from-012 A adm t)
    (AdmissibleAction.ref-upper adm)

action-nontrivial : (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleAction A ‚Üí IsNonTrivial A
action-nontrivial A adm t eq =
  suc‚Č§-impossible zero (subst (őĽ n ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ n) eq (action-bound A adm t))
\end{code}

\paragraph{The sandwich.}
The two reference inequalities force $A\,\texttt{tri-012} = 1$.

\begin{code}
action-ref-value : (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleAction A ‚Üí A tri-012 ‚Č° 1
action-ref-value A adm =
  ‚Č§-antisym (AdmissibleAction.ref-upper adm)
            (AdmissibleAction.ref-lower adm)
\end{code}

\paragraph{Assembly.}
Every face has value $1$: invariance reduces the problem to the
reference face, and the sandwich fixes that value.

\begin{code}
action-forced : (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleAction A ‚Üí
  (t : K4Triangle) ‚Üí A t ‚Č° 1
action-forced A adm t =
  trans (action-all-equal-to-012 A adm t)
        (action-ref-value A adm)
\end{code}

The summary record packages the existence witness, the global-value
theorem, and the uniqueness statement.

\begin{code}
record ForcedActionResult : Set where
  field
    witness      : AdmissibleAction unit-action
    global-value : (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleAction A ‚Üí
                   (t‚āĀ t‚āā : K4Triangle) ‚Üí A t‚āĀ ‚Č° A t‚āā
    unique       : (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleAction A ‚Üí
                   (t : K4Triangle) ‚Üí A t ‚Č° 1

theorem-forced-action : ForcedActionResult
theorem-forced-action = record
  { witness      = unit-action-admissible
  ; global-value = action-global-value
  ; unique       = action-forced
  }
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{The forced face weight}
\label{sec:forced-action:face-weight}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The constant \texttt{face-weight} merely names the forced value $1$.
The closing equality with \texttt{triangle-loop-order} identifies it
with the previously computed combinatorial length scale.

\begin{code}
face-weight : ‚Ąē
face-weight = 1

face-weight-is-loop-order : face-weight ‚Č° triangle-loop-order
face-weight-is-loop-order = refl
\end{code}


%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź
\chapter{Forced Vertex Weight}
\label{ch:forced-geometric-coupling}
%‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź‚ēź

The three forced constants live on the three simplicial dimensions of
$K_4$:
\[
\begin{array}{ccl}
\text{dim}\;1 & \text{(edges)}   & \longrightarrow\; \mathit{edge\text{-}weight} \\
\text{dim}\;2 & \text{(faces)}   & \longrightarrow\; \mathit{face\text{-}weight} \\
\text{dim}\;0 & \text{(vertices)} & \longrightarrow\; \mathit{vertex\text{-}weight}
\end{array}
\]
Propagation assigns a weight to each edge and the action law assigns a weight to each face. The remaining case is the $0$-dimensional one: the assignment of a weight to each vertex.

A curvature law is any function $K_4\text{Vertex} \to \mathbb{N}$, subject to four constraints---each of which admits genuine alternatives on its own:
\begin{enumerate}
\item \emph{Non-triviality} --- every vertex carries positive curvature.
\item \emph{$K_4$-invariance} --- the law is preserved under every vertex permutation.
\item \emph{Lower bound} --- every vertex carries curvature at least~$1$.
\item \emph{Minimality} --- no vertex carries curvature above~$1$.
\end{enumerate}
The vertex case is the $0$-dimensional analogue of the previous two
chapters. The candidate space is again unrestricted, invariance reduces
all values to a reference value, and the lower and upper bounds at that
reference vertex force the common value to be $1$.

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Curvature candidates}
\label{sec:forced-coupling:candidates}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

A curvature law is an arbitrary function \texttt{K4Vertex}
$\to \mathbb{N}$. If the value $1$ is to emerge, it must come from the
admissibility conditions and not from the choice of type.

\begin{code}
CurvatureLaw : Set
CurvatureLaw = K4Vertex ‚Üí ‚Ąē
\end{code}

The auxiliary predicates mirror those of the face case. As before, the
admissible record remembers only invariance and the lower and upper
bounds on the reference vertex $v_0$; the corresponding global versions
are derived afterward.

\begin{code}
IsCurvatureNonTrivial : CurvatureLaw ‚Üí Set
IsCurvatureNonTrivial C = (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí C v ‚Č° 0 ‚Üí ‚ä•

IsCurvatureInvariant : CurvatureLaw ‚Üí Set
IsCurvatureInvariant C = (ŌÉ : K4Perm) ‚Üí (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí
  C v ‚Č° C (k4to ŌÉ v)

CurvatureBound : CurvatureLaw ‚Üí Set
CurvatureBound C = (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ C v

ReferenceVertexLower : CurvatureLaw ‚Üí Set
ReferenceVertexLower C = 1 ‚Č§ C v‚āÄ

IsCurvatureMinimal : CurvatureLaw ‚Üí Set
IsCurvatureMinimal C = (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí C v ‚Č§ 1

ReferenceVertexUpper : CurvatureLaw ‚Üí Set
ReferenceVertexUpper C = C v‚āÄ ‚Č§ 1

record AdmissibleCurvature (C : CurvatureLaw) : Set where
  field
    invariant  : IsCurvatureInvariant C
    ref-lower  : ReferenceVertexLower C
    ref-upper  : ReferenceVertexUpper C
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Existence: the unit curvature law}
\label{sec:forced-coupling:existence}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

Existence is again given by the constant law with value $1$. It is
invariant, and the two reference bounds hold immediately.

\begin{code}
unit-curvature : CurvatureLaw
unit-curvature _ = 1

unit-curvature-invariant : IsCurvatureInvariant unit-curvature
unit-curvature-invariant _ _ = refl

unit-curvature-bounded : CurvatureBound unit-curvature
unit-curvature-bounded _ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

unit-curvature-minimal : IsCurvatureMinimal unit-curvature
unit-curvature-minimal _ = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

unit-curvature-ref-lower : ReferenceVertexLower unit-curvature
unit-curvature-ref-lower = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

unit-curvature-ref-upper : ReferenceVertexUpper unit-curvature
unit-curvature-ref-upper = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

unit-curvature-admissible : AdmissibleCurvature unit-curvature
unit-curvature-admissible = record
  { invariant  = unit-curvature-invariant
  ; ref-lower  = unit-curvature-ref-lower
  ; ref-upper  = unit-curvature-ref-upper
  }
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Construction: invariance and the sandwich pin a single value}
\label{sec:forced-coupling:elimination}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The proof has exactly the same structure as the previous two chapters.
The lemma \texttt{curvature-from-v0} transports the value at the
reference vertex to any other vertex, the transported bounds give
$1 \le r \le 1$, and antisymmetry identifies the common value with $1$.

\begin{code}
curvature-from-v0 : (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleCurvature C ‚Üí
  (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí C v‚āÄ ‚Č° C v
curvature-from-v0 C adm v =
  let st = k4Perm-send v‚āÄ v in
  let ŌÉ = fst st in
  let p = snd st in
  trans (AdmissibleCurvature.invariant adm ŌÉ v‚āÄ)
        (cong C p)

curvature-all-equal-to-v0 : (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleCurvature C ‚Üí
  (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí C v ‚Č° C v‚āÄ
curvature-all-equal-to-v0 C adm v =
  sym (curvature-from-v0 C adm v)

curvature-global-value : (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleCurvature C ‚Üí
  (v‚āĀ v‚āā : K4Vertex) ‚Üí C v‚āĀ ‚Č° C v‚āā
curvature-global-value C adm u w =
  trans (curvature-all-equal-to-v0 C adm u)
        (curvature-from-v0 C adm w)
\end{code}

\paragraph{Invariance transports the brackets.}
The lower and upper brackets are imposed only on the reference vertex $v_0$. Invariance transports them to every vertex.

\begin{code}
curvature-bound : (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleCurvature C ‚Üí CurvatureBound C
curvature-bound C adm v =
  subst (őĽ n ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ n)
    (curvature-from-v0 C adm v)
    (AdmissibleCurvature.ref-lower adm)

curvature-minimal : (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleCurvature C ‚Üí IsCurvatureMinimal C
curvature-minimal C adm v =
  subst (őĽ n ‚Üí n ‚Č§ 1)
    (curvature-from-v0 C adm v)
    (AdmissibleCurvature.ref-upper adm)

curvature-nontrivial : (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleCurvature C ‚Üí IsCurvatureNonTrivial C
curvature-nontrivial C adm v eq =
  suc‚Č§-impossible zero (subst (őĽ n ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ n) eq (curvature-bound C adm v))
\end{code}

\paragraph{The sandwich.}
The lower bound gives $1 \le C\,v_0$. The upper bound gives $C\,v_0 \le 1$. Antisymmetry closes to $C\,v_0 \equiv 1$.

\begin{code}
curvature-ref-value : (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleCurvature C ‚Üí C v‚āÄ ‚Č° 1
curvature-ref-value C adm =
  ‚Č§-antisym (AdmissibleCurvature.ref-upper adm)
            (AdmissibleCurvature.ref-lower adm)
\end{code}

\paragraph{Assembly.}
Every vertex equals $1$: invariance collapses to the reference value, the sandwich pins that value.

\begin{code}
curvature-forced : (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleCurvature C ‚Üí
  (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí C v ‚Č° 1
curvature-forced C adm v =
  trans (curvature-all-equal-to-v0 C adm v)
        (curvature-ref-value C adm)
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{The forced vertex weight}
\label{sec:forced-coupling:vertex-weight}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The record \texttt{ForcedCurvatureResult} has the same form as in the
previous chapters: an existence witness, a global-value theorem, and a
uniqueness statement. The definition of \texttt{vertex-weight} only
names the value that has already been forced.

\begin{code}
record ForcedCurvatureResult : Set where
  field
    witness      : AdmissibleCurvature unit-curvature
    global-value : (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleCurvature C ‚Üí
                   (v‚āĀ v‚āā : K4Vertex) ‚Üí C v‚āĀ ‚Č° C v‚āā
    unique       : (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleCurvature C ‚Üí
                   (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí C v ‚Č° 1

theorem-forced-curvature : ForcedCurvatureResult
theorem-forced-curvature = record
  { witness      = unit-curvature-admissible
  ; global-value = curvature-global-value
  ; unique       = curvature-forced
  }

vertex-weight : ‚Ąē
vertex-weight = 1

vertex-weight-forced : (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí AdmissibleCurvature C ‚Üí C v‚āÄ ‚Č° vertex-weight
vertex-weight-forced C adm = curvature-ref-value C adm
\end{code}

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Filter audit: sufficiency versus minimality}
\label{sec:forced-coupling:filter-audit}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

The three preceding limit theorems secure their constants through structural orbit-transport combined with a local sandwich. A severe test remains: are these admissibility filters truly minimal, or does logical bloat exist? If a filter could be derived purely from the others, the filter set itself would not be a fundamental physical law, merely an overlapping mathematical description. 

The next question concerns the choice of admissibility basis itself.
The lemmas in this section show why the record stores only reference
bounds together with invariance. If one imposed global pointwise lower
and upper bounds from the outset, then several uniqueness statements
would become trivial and invariance would cease to play an essential
role. The chosen formulation avoids that collapse by keeping the bounds
local and letting symmetry do the transport.

\begin{code}
propagation-forced-no-invariance :
  (P : PropagationLaw) ‚Üí IsLocal P ‚Üí HasEdgeSupport P ‚Üí IsBounded P ‚Üí
  (v w : K4Vertex) ‚Üí P v w ‚Č° adjacent v w
propagation-forced-no-invariance P local support upper v w with K4-decEq v w
... | inj‚āĀ refl = trans (local v) (sym (adj-self v))
... | inj‚āā ne   = trans (‚Č§-antisym (upper v w) (support v w ne))
                        (sym (K4-distinct-adj v w ne))

action-nontrivial-from-bound : (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí ActionBound A ‚Üí IsNonTrivial A
action-nontrivial-from-bound A lower t eq =
  suc‚Č§-impossible zero (subst (őĽ n ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ n) eq (lower t))

action-forced-no-invariance :
  (A : ActionLaw) ‚Üí ActionBound A ‚Üí IsMinimal A ‚Üí
  (t : K4Triangle) ‚Üí A t ‚Č° 1
action-forced-no-invariance A lower upper t =
  ‚Č§-antisym (upper t) (lower t)

curvature-nontrivial-from-bound :
  (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí CurvatureBound C ‚Üí IsCurvatureNonTrivial C
curvature-nontrivial-from-bound C lower v eq =
  suc‚Č§-impossible zero (subst (őĽ n ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ n) eq (lower v))

curvature-forced-no-invariance :
  (C : CurvatureLaw) ‚Üí CurvatureBound C ‚Üí IsCurvatureMinimal C ‚Üí
  (v : K4Vertex) ‚Üí C v ‚Č° 1
curvature-forced-no-invariance C lower upper v =
  ‚Č§-antisym (upper v) (lower v)
\end{code}

The force of these lemmas is conceptual, not destructive. They do \emph{not} weaken the uniqueness theorems; they justify the choice of admissibility basis. The constant chapters prove uniqueness with a genuinely non-pointwise admissibility architecture, and these lemmas confirm that the pointwise alternative would have been redundant. The question of whether an alternative set of predicates could achieve the same singleton collapse belongs to the meta-theory of filter selection rather than to the elimination itself.

%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
\section{Minimal admissibility basis}
\label{sec:forced-coupling:minimal-basis}
%‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

While the architecture uses four foundational criteria for edge propagation and three for action and curvature natively, its philosophical minimality must be formally established computationally. Each singular primitive clause must be objectively indispensable. When exactly one is deleted, exactly one rogue geometric map structurally survives.

Minimality is proved here by explicit counterexamples. For each omitted
filter a concrete rival law is exhibited that satisfies all the
remaining hypotheses but fails the desired conclusion. In this way the
architecture is justified not by general rhetoric about necessity but by
precise witnesses showing what survives when a single condition is
removed.

\paragraph{Propagation basis.}
Without locality, the everywhere-one law survives. Without invariance, a law can single out the reference edge and ignore the rest. Without the lower bracket, the zero law survives. Without the upper bracket, the doubled adjacency law survives.

\begin{code}
propagation-all-one : PropagationLaw
propagation-all-one _ _ = 1

propagation-all-one-invariant : IsK4PermInvariant propagation-all-one
propagation-all-one-invariant _ _ _ = refl

propagation-all-one-ref-lower : ReferenceEdgeLower propagation-all-one
propagation-all-one-ref-lower = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

propagation-all-one-ref-upper : ReferenceEdgeUpper propagation-all-one
propagation-all-one-ref-upper = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

propagation-all-one-not-adj : propagation-all-one v‚āÄ v‚āÄ ‚Č° adjacent v‚āÄ v‚āÄ ‚Üí ‚ä•
propagation-all-one-not-adj ()

propagation-reference-only : PropagationLaw
propagation-reference-only v‚āÄ v‚āĀ = 1
propagation-reference-only _  _  = 0

propagation-reference-only-local : IsLocal propagation-reference-only
propagation-reference-only-local v‚āÄ = refl
propagation-reference-only-local v‚āĀ = refl
propagation-reference-only-local v‚āā = refl
propagation-reference-only-local v‚āÉ = refl

propagation-reference-only-ref-lower : ReferenceEdgeLower propagation-reference-only
propagation-reference-only-ref-lower = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

propagation-reference-only-ref-upper : ReferenceEdgeUpper propagation-reference-only
propagation-reference-only-ref-upper = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

propagation-reference-only-not-adj :
  propagation-reference-only v‚āÄ v‚āā ‚Č° adjacent v‚āÄ v‚āā ‚Üí ‚ä•
propagation-reference-only-not-adj ()

propagation-zero : PropagationLaw
propagation-zero _ _ = 0

propagation-zero-local : IsLocal propagation-zero
propagation-zero-local v‚āÄ = refl
propagation-zero-local v‚āĀ = refl
propagation-zero-local v‚āā = refl
propagation-zero-local v‚āÉ = refl

propagation-zero-invariant : IsK4PermInvariant propagation-zero
propagation-zero-invariant _ _ _ = refl

propagation-zero-ref-upper : ReferenceEdgeUpper propagation-zero
propagation-zero-ref-upper = z‚Č§n

propagation-zero-not-adj : propagation-zero v‚āÄ v‚āĀ ‚Č° adjacent v‚āÄ v‚āĀ ‚Üí ‚ä•
propagation-zero-not-adj ()

propagation-double : PropagationLaw
propagation-double v w = adjacent v w +‚Ąē adjacent v w

propagation-double-local : IsLocal propagation-double
propagation-double-local v =
  subst (őĽ n ‚Üí n +‚Ąē n ‚Č° 0)
    (sym (adj-self v))
    refl

propagation-double-invariant : IsK4PermInvariant propagation-double
propagation-double-invariant ŌÉ v w =
  cong (őĽ n ‚Üí n +‚Ąē n) (k4-perm-preserves-adj ŌÉ v w)

propagation-double-ref-lower : ReferenceEdgeLower propagation-double
propagation-double-ref-lower = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

propagation-double-not-adj : propagation-double v‚āÄ v‚āĀ ‚Č° adjacent v‚āÄ v‚āĀ ‚Üí ‚ä•
propagation-double-not-adj ()
\end{code}

\paragraph{Action basis.}
Without invariance, the reference face can be privileged while the others collapse to zero. Without the lower bracket, the zero law survives. Without the upper bracket, the constant-two law survives.

\begin{code}
action-reference-only : ActionLaw
action-reference-only v‚āÉ = 1
action-reference-only _  = 0

action-reference-only-ref-lower : ReferenceTriangleLower action-reference-only
action-reference-only-ref-lower = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

action-reference-only-ref-upper : ReferenceTriangleUpper action-reference-only
action-reference-only-ref-upper = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

action-reference-only-not-forced : action-reference-only tri-013 ‚Č° 1 ‚Üí ‚ä•
action-reference-only-not-forced ()

action-zero : ActionLaw
action-zero _ = 0

action-zero-invariant : IsActionInvariant action-zero
action-zero-invariant _ _ = refl

action-zero-ref-upper : ReferenceTriangleUpper action-zero
action-zero-ref-upper = z‚Č§n

action-zero-not-forced : action-zero tri-012 ‚Č° 1 ‚Üí ‚ä•
action-zero-not-forced ()

action-two : ActionLaw
action-two _ = 2

action-two-invariant : IsActionInvariant action-two
action-two-invariant _ _ = refl

action-two-ref-lower : ReferenceTriangleLower action-two
action-two-ref-lower = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

action-two-not-forced : action-two tri-012 ‚Č° 1 ‚Üí ‚ä•
action-two-not-forced ()
\end{code}

\paragraph{Curvature basis.}
The same pattern repeats on the $0$-simplices. Without invariance, the reference vertex can be privileged. Without the lower bracket, the zero law survives. Without the upper bracket, the constant-two law survives.

\begin{code}
curvature-reference-only : CurvatureLaw
curvature-reference-only v‚āÄ = 1
curvature-reference-only _  = 0

curvature-reference-only-ref-lower : ReferenceVertexLower curvature-reference-only
curvature-reference-only-ref-lower = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

curvature-reference-only-ref-upper : ReferenceVertexUpper curvature-reference-only
curvature-reference-only-ref-upper = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

curvature-reference-only-not-forced : curvature-reference-only v‚āĀ ‚Č° 1 ‚Üí ‚ä•
curvature-reference-only-not-forced ()

curvature-zero : CurvatureLaw
curvature-zero _ = 0

curvature-zero-invariant : IsCurvatureInvariant curvature-zero
curvature-zero-invariant _ _ = refl

curvature-zero-ref-upper : ReferenceVertexUpper curvature-zero
curvature-zero-ref-upper = z‚Č§n

curvature-zero-not-forced : curvature-zero v‚āÄ ‚Č° 1 ‚Üí ‚ä•
curvature-zero-not-forced ()

curvature-two : CurvatureLaw
curvature-two _ = 2

curvature-two-invariant : IsCurvatureInvariant curvature-two
curvature-two-invariant _ _ = refl

curvature-two-ref-lower : ReferenceVertexLower curvature-two
curvature-two-ref-lower = s‚Č§s z‚Č§n

curvature-two-not-forced : curvature-two v‚āÄ ‚Č° 1 ‚Üí ‚ä•
curvature-two-not-forced ()
\end{code}

These witnesses prove that the admissibility basis is irreducible: every primitive clause excludes a live rival that the remaining clauses cannot block. Removing any single clause breaks the singleton collapse. Nothing remaining in the basis is decorative.

\paragraph{Filter minimality: invariance is non-redundant.}
A sharper question: is the $K_4$-invariance filter genuinely eliminative, or do the other three filters (locality/non-triviality, lower bound, upper bound) already force the unit constant? The answer is no---without invariance, a rival candidate survives in each simplicial dimension.

For propagation, the reference-only law $P(v_0, v_1) = 1$, $P = 0$ elsewhere, satisfies locality, the lower bracket on the reference edge, and the upper bracket---but is not $K_4$-invariant. Invariance is therefore the filter that blocks this candidate. For the action and curvature, the same pattern repeats: the reference-only face law and the reference-only vertex law satisfy every filter except invariance.

These examples isolate the role of invariance very clearly. In each
simplicial dimension there is a reference-only law that obeys the local
conditions and the two reference bounds but fails to spread uniformly
across the orbit. Invariance is exactly the hypothesis that excludes
such laws.

\begin{code}
-- ¬ß¬ß Filter minimality: invariance is the critical filter in each dimension.

-- ¬ß Propagation: without invariance, the reference-only law survives.
propagation-needs-invariance :
  IsLocal propagation-reference-only √ó
  ReferenceEdgeLower propagation-reference-only √ó
  ReferenceEdgeUpper propagation-reference-only √ó
  (propagation-reference-only v‚āÄ v‚āā ‚Č° adjacent v‚āÄ v‚āā ‚Üí ‚ä•)
propagation-needs-invariance =
  propagation-reference-only-local ,
  (propagation-reference-only-ref-lower ,
  (propagation-reference-only-ref-upper ,
  propagation-reference-only-not-adj))

-- ¬ß Action: without invariance, the reference-only face law survives.
action-needs-invariance :
  ReferenceTriangleLower action-reference-only √ó
  ReferenceTriangleUpper action-reference-only √ó
  (action-reference-only tri-013 ‚Č° 1 ‚Üí ‚ä•)
action-needs-invariance =
  action-reference-only-ref-lower ,
  (action-reference-only-ref-upper ,
  action-reference-only-not-forced)

-- ¬ß Curvature: without invariance, the reference-only vertex law survives.
curvature-needs-invariance :
  ReferenceVertexLower curvature-reference-only √ó
  ReferenceVertexUpper curvature-reference-only √ó
  (curvature-reference-only v‚āĀ ‚Č° 1 ‚Üí ‚ä•)
curvature-needs-invariance =
  curvature-reference-only-ref-lower ,
